Drives work order priority and PM frequency.
reference: ISO 55000 Asset Management; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; Building Engines CMMS

description: Current operational lifecycle status of the asset. 'active' = in normal service; 'inactive' = temporarily out of service; 'decommissioned' = permanently removed; 'under_repair' = currently being serviced; 'pending_replacement' = approved for capital replacement.
reference: ISO 55000 Asset Management; Building Engines CMMS

description: Externally-visible physical label or barcode affixed to the asset for field identification during inspections, work orders, and preventive maintenance rounds. Unique within a property. Sourced from Building Engines asset registry.
reference: ISO 55000 Asset Management; Building Engines CMMS

description: Specific equipment type within the asset category (e.g., 'Air Handling Unit', 'Chiller', 'Boiler', 'Fire Suppression Sprinkler System', 'Hydraulic Elevator', 'Standby Generator'). Drives preventive maintenance task templates in Building Engines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product given high cardinality]
reference: Building Engines CMMS; ASHRAE Standards; NFPA 25

description: Unique object identifier linking this asset to its corresponding component in the Building Information Modeling (BIM) model (e.g., Revit GUID or IFC GlobalId). Enables spatial visualization, clash detection, and digital twin integration.
reference: BIM IFC Standard ISO 16739; ISO 19650 BIM Management; U.S. Green Building Council LEED

description: Accounting classification indicating whether maintenance and replacement activities for this asset are capitalized (CAPEX) or expensed (OPEX) per FASB ASC 360 and internal accounting policy. 'mixed' applies to assets with both capital and operating components (e.g., partial HVAC overhaul).
reference: FASB ASC 360 Property Plant and Equipment; GAAP; SAP S/4HANA Fixed Assets Module

description: Current physical condition rating of the asset as assessed during the most recent condition assessment. Drives prioritization of CAPEX replacement and preventive maintenance frequency. Scale: excellent=new/like-new; good=minor wear; fair=moderate wear; poor=significant deterioration; critical=imminent failure risk.
reference: ISO 55000 Asset Management; ASTM E2018 Property Condition Assessment; Building Engines CMMS

description: Timestamp when this asset record was first created in the Building Engines CMMS and ingested into the maintenance domain data product. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; ISO 55000 Asset Management; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: Accounting depreciation method applied to this asset for financial reporting purposes. 'straight_line' is most common for real estate equipment. Aligns with Yardi Voyager Fixed Assets and SAP S/4HANA asset accounting configuration.
reference: FASB ASC 360 Property Plant and Equipment; GAAP; Yardi Voyager Fixed Assets; SAP S/4HANA

description: Manufacturer-specified energy efficiency rating or metric for the asset (e.g., SEER rating for HVAC, EER, COP, kW/ton). Null if not applicable. Used for ESG reporting, LEED documentation, and energy benchmarking under EPA ENERGY STAR.
reference: EPA ENERGY STAR; U.S. Green Building Council LEED; ASHRAE 90.1 Energy Standard; ESG Reporting

description: Indicates whether this asset has an energy efficiency rating or certification (e.g., ENERGY STAR, LEED-qualifying equipment). Supports ESG reporting, LEED certification tracking, and sustainability benchmarking.
reference: U.S. Green Building Council LEED; BREEAM; EPA ENERGY STAR; ESG Reporting Frameworks

description: Date on which any purchased extended warranty coverage expires. Null if no extended warranty is in place. Used alongside manufacturer warranty to determine warranty claim eligibility for repair work orders.
reference: Building Engines CMMS; ISO 55000 Asset Management

description: Date the asset was physically installed and commissioned at the property. Anchors useful life calculations, warranty start dates, and CAPEX replacement year projections.
reference: ISO 55000 Asset Management; FASB ASC 360 Property Plant and Equipment; Building Engines CMMS

description: Indicates whether the asset is owned by a tenant rather than the property owner. Tenant-owned assets are tracked for maintenance coordination but excluded from owner CAPEX planning and depreciation schedules. Relevant for Tenant Improvement (TI) assets.
reference: FASB ASC 842 Lease Accounting; IFRS 16; Building Engines CMMS; Yardi Voyager Lease Administration

description: Date on which the installation contractor's labor warranty expires. Covers defects in workmanship separate from equipment manufacturer warranty. Null if no labor warranty applies.
reference: Building Engines CMMS; ISO 55000 Asset Management; AIA Construction Contract Standards

description: Date of the most recent formal property condition assessment (PCA) or engineering inspection that produced the current condition rating. Indicates data freshness for capital planning purposes.
reference: ASTM E2018 Property Condition Assessment Standard Guide; ISO 55000 Asset Management

description: Date the most recent preventive maintenance (PM) service was completed on this asset. Used to calculate next scheduled PM date and identify overdue maintenance items.
reference: Building Engines CMMS; ISO 55000 Asset Management; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

description: Date of the most recent mandatory regulatory inspection or certification for this asset. Null if regulatory_inspection_required is false. Used for compliance reporting and COO maintenance.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code; NFPA 25; Local Building Codes

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this asset record in the source system or data product. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; ISO 55000 Asset Management; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: Date of the most recent warranty claim submitted for this asset. Null if no warranty claims have been filed. Used to track warranty utilization and vendor responsiveness.
reference: Building Engines CMMS; ISO 55000 Asset Management

description: Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) of the asset. Used for warranty claims, spare parts procurement, and vendor dispatch coordination.
reference: Building Engines CMMS; ISO 55000 Asset Management

description: Date on which the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) warranty expires. After this date, repair costs are no longer recoverable from the manufacturer. Critical for work order cost routing decisions.
reference: Building Engines CMMS; ISO 55000 Asset Management; UCC Article 2 Warranties

description: Manufacturer-assigned model or part number for the asset. Required for ordering replacement parts, scheduling OEM-certified service, and cross-referencing BIM component libraries.
reference: Building Engines CMMS; ISO 55000 Asset Management

description: Scheduled due date for the next preventive maintenance (PM) service on this asset, calculated from last PM date and PM schedule frequency. Drives work order scheduling and compliance reporting in Building Engines.
reference: Building Engines CMMS; ISO 55000 Asset Management

description: Required frequency of preventive maintenance (PM) inspections and servicing for this asset as defined in the O&M program. Drives automated PM work order generation in Building Engines CMMS.
reference: Building Engines CMMS; ASHRAE 180 Standard Practice for Inspection and Maintenance of Commercial HVAC Systems; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

description: Original acquisition cost of the asset in USD at time of purchase or installation. Used as the cost basis for fixed asset depreciation schedules in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA Fixed Assets module.
reference: FASB ASC 360 Property Plant and Equipment; SOX Section 302/404; Yardi Voyager Fixed Assets; SAP S/4HANA

description: Calendar year in which the asset is recommended for capital replacement based on condition assessment findings and remaining useful life estimate. Used to populate CAPEX schedules in Argus Enterprise and SAP S/4HANA project systems.
reference: ASTM E2018; ISO 55000 Asset Management; FASB ASC 360; Argus Enterprise

description: Type of refrigerant used in HVAC or cooling equipment (e.g., R-410A, R-22, R-134a, R-32). Null for non-refrigerant assets. Required for EPA Section 608 refrigerant management compliance and ESG greenhouse gas reporting.
reference: EPA Section 608 Clean Air Act; EPA ENERGY STAR; ESG Reporting; ASHRAE 34 Refrigerant Designation

description: Date on which the current regulatory inspection certificate or permit expires. Null if not applicable. Expiry triggers mandatory re-inspection scheduling to maintain compliance and avoid operational shutdowns.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code; NFPA 25; Local Building Codes; COO Requirements

description: Indicates whether this asset is subject to mandatory regulatory inspection and certification (e.g., elevators, boilers, fire suppression systems, pressure vessels). True triggers compliance tracking and certificate of occupancy (COO) dependencies.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code; NFPA 25; Local Building Codes; COO Requirements

description: Estimated number of years remaining before the asset requires capital replacement, as determined by the most recent condition assessment. Decimal precision supports mid-year estimates. Key input for CAPEX reserve modeling and Argus Enterprise cash flow projections.
reference: ASTM E2018; ISO 55000 Asset Management; FASB ASC 360

description: Current estimated cost in USD to replace the asset with a new equivalent unit, inclusive of equipment, labor, and installation. Used for CAPEX budgeting, insurance valuation, and reserve fund analysis. Expressed in base currency (USD).
reference: FASB ASC 360; ISO 55000 Asset Management; Appraisal Institute; IVSC

description: Specific room number, mechanical room designation, or building zone where the asset is installed (e.g., 'MER-301', 'Penthouse Mechanical', 'Zone A West Wing'). Supports precise technician navigation and BIM spatial coordination.
reference: Building Engines CMMS; BIM IFC Standard ISO 16739

description: Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number for the specific asset unit. Required for warranty registration, insurance claims, and regulatory compliance inspections (e.g., elevator certificates, boiler permits).
reference: Building Engines CMMS; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code

description: Native asset identifier from the originating source system (Building Engines CMMS). Preserves the upstream system key for reconciliation, lineage tracing, and re-ingestion deduplication in the Databricks lakehouse Silver layer.
reference: Building Engines CMMS; Data Governance Best Practices; SOX Section 302/404

description: Manufacturer or engineering-estimated useful life of the asset in years from installation date. Used for depreciation schedules (FASB ASC 360), CAPEX reserve planning, and remaining useful life calculations.
reference: FASB ASC 360 Property Plant and Equipment; IFRS 16; ISO 55000 Asset Management; ASHRAE 2019 HVAC Applications Handbook

description: Total number of warranty claims filed against any warranty (manufacturer, extended, or labor) for this asset since installation. Indicates reliability history and supports vendor performance evaluation.
reference: Building Engines CMMS; ISO 55000 Asset Management

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Externally-known, human-readable reference number assigned to the safety incident at the time of reporting. Used for cross-referencing with regulatory filings, insurance claims, and Building Engines work order records. Format: INC-YYYY-NNNNNN.

description: Current workflow state of the safety incident record, tracking its lifecycle from initial report through investigation, corrective action, and closure. Valid values: open, under_investigation, corrective_action_pending, closed, voided.
reference: ISO 45001:2018 Clause 10.2; OSHA 29 CFR Part 1904

description: The precise date and time at which the safety incident or near-miss event occurred at the property. This is the principal real-world event timestamp, distinct from the record creation timestamp. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR Part 1904.5; RIDDOR 2013 (UK); ISO 45001:2018

description: Primary classification of the safety incident by nature of the event. Drives regulatory recordability determination and reporting pathway. Valid values: injury, near_miss, property_damage, environmental, fire, security.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR Part 1904.7; RIDDOR 2013; ISO 45001:2018 Clause 6.1.2

description: Classification of the person involved in the safety incident by their relationship to the property or organization. Determines applicable regulatory reporting obligations and insurance coverage. Valid values: employee, contractor, tenant, visitor, vendor.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR Part 1904.31; RIDDOR 2013 Regulation 4

description: Description of the nature of the injury or occupational illness sustained, such as laceration, fracture, sprain, burn, or respiratory condition. Required for OSHA 300 log and workers compensation filings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: laceration|fracture|sprain_strain|burn|contusion|respiratory|eye_injury|amputation|fatality|other — promote to reference product]
reference: OSHA 29 CFR Part 1904.29 (Form 300 Column G); BLS Occupational Injury and Illness Classification Manual

description: Name or identifier of the property manager, safety officer, or facility operations lead responsible for conducting the incident investigation. Supports accountability and audit trail for the investigation process.
reference: ISO 45001:2018 Clause 10.2; OSHA Incident Investigation Guidelines; Building Engines CMMS

description: Date on which the formal incident investigation was completed and findings were documented. Marks the transition from investigation to corrective action phase in the incident lifecycle.
reference: ISO 45001:2018 Clause 10.2; OSHA Incident Investigation Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the safety incident involved a release of hazardous materials, chemicals, or pollutants into the environment. Triggers EPA reporting obligations and environmental compliance review when True.
reference: EPA CERCLA; EPA EPCRA Section 304; OSHA 29 CFR Part 1910.119

description: Indicates whether the safety incident meets OSHA recordability criteria under 29 CFR Part 1904 and must be entered on the OSHA 300 log. True if the incident resulted in medical treatment beyond first aid, days away from work, restricted work, job transfer, loss of consciousness, or diagnosis of a significant injury by a healthcare professional.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR Part 1904.7; OSHA Form 300

description: Indicates whether the safety incident is reportable under the UK Reporting of Injuries, Diseases and Dangerous Occurrences Regulations 2013 (RIDDOR). Applicable for properties under UK jurisdiction. Null for non-UK properties.
reference: RIDDOR 2013 (SI 2013/1471); Health and Safety Executive (HSE) UK

description: The date on which the safety incident was entered onto the OSHA 300 log. OSHA requires recordable incidents to be logged within seven calendar days of receiving information that a recordable case occurred. Nullable for non-OSHA-recordable incidents.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR Part 1904.29(b)(3); OSHA Form 300

description: The country jurisdiction governing the regulatory reporting obligations for this safety incident. Determines which reporting framework applies (OSHA 300 for USA, RIDDOR for GBR, etc.). Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|GBR|CAN|AUS|DEU|FRA|SGP|ARE|OTHER — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; OSHA 29 CFR Part 1904; RIDDOR 2013; OSHA Canada

description: Name or identifier of the person who initially reported the safety incident to the facility management team. May be the injured party, a witness, or a property manager. Supports incident reporting accountability.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR Part 1904.29; Building Engines CMMS

description: Date and time when the safety incident was formally reported to the facility management team or entered into the Building Engines CMMS system. May differ from incident_timestamp due to delayed reporting.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR Part 1904.29; Building Engines CMMS

description: The identified root cause of the safety incident as determined through investigation. Describes the underlying systemic or behavioral factor that allowed the incident to occur, such as inadequate PPE, equipment malfunction, procedural non-compliance, or environmental hazard.
reference: ISO 45001:2018 Clause 10.2; OSHA Incident Investigation Guidelines

description: Classification of the medical treatment provided following the safety incident. Determines OSHA recordability: first aid cases are generally not OSHA recordable, while medical treatment beyond first aid triggers recordability. Valid values: first_aid, medical_treatment, hospitalization, fatality, no_treatment.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR Part 1904.7; RIDDOR 2013 Regulation 4

description: Audit timestamp recording when the safety incident record was most recently modified. Tracks changes to investigation findings, corrective actions, or regulatory filing status.
reference: SOX Section 404; ISO 45001:2018

description: Names of individuals who witnessed the safety incident. Recorded as a comma-separated list or narrative. Used during incident investigation and may be required for regulatory filings or legal proceedings. Classified as confidential due to sensitivity of witness information.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR Part 1904; ISO 45001:2018; GDPR Article 5

description: Precision level of the geocoded latitude/longitude coordinates. description: Indicates whether this address is the primary address for the associated property or building. business_glossary_term: Gross Leasable Area (GLA) in Square Feet (SQF)
description: Total Gross Leasable Area (GLA) of the building measured in square feet (SQF). Represents the total floor area designed for tenant occupancy and exclusive use. Primary metric for retail and commercial leasing revenue calculations.
reference: BOMA International Floor Measurement Standards; RESO Data Dictionary

business_glossary_term: Gross Leasable Area (GLA) in Square Meters (SQM)
description: Total Gross Leasable Area (GLA) of the building measured in square meters (SQM). Required for international portfolio reporting, IFRS 16 compliance, and cross-border investment analysis.
reference: BOMA International; IASB IFRS 16 Leases; IVSC Valuation Standards

business_glossary_term: Heating Ventilation and Air Conditioning (HVAC) System Type
description: Description of the primary Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) system type installed in the building (e.g., 'VAV', 'chilled beam', 'VRF', 'central plant'). Used in maintenance scheduling, energy benchmarking, and ESG reporting.
reference: ASHRAE Standards; EPA ENERGY STAR Program; Building Engines Facility Operations

business_glossary_term: Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) Compliance Flag

business_glossary_term: Environmental Social and Governance (ESG) Reporting Flag

reference: SEC ESG Disclosure Requirements; GRESB Real Estate Assessment; USGBC LEED Standards

description: Timestamp when the building record was most recently modified in the enterprise data platform. Used for change data capture, data lineage, and audit trail compliance.

description: Ratio of rentable area to usable area (rentable/usable), representing the tenant's proportionate share of common areas. Also known as the add-on factor or core factor. Used in lease negotiations and CAM calculations.

description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the building in decimal degrees (WGS84). Enables GIS-based spatial analytics, proximity analysis, and map-based portfolio visualization.
reference: GIS Standards (WGS84); RESO Data Dictionary

business_glossary_term: Net Leasable Area (NLA) in Square Feet (SQF)

reference: BOMA International Floor Measurement Standards; RESO Data Dictionary

business_glossary_term: Net Leasable Area (NLA) in Square Meters (SQM)

description: Number of parking stalls per 1,000 square feet of rentable area. Standard metric for commercial leasing competitiveness and tenant attraction, particularly for office and retail properties.

description: Total number of parking stalls associated with the building, including surface, structured, and underground parking. Used in lease negotiations, parking revenue management, and zoning compliance.

description: Description of the primary structural system (e.g., 'moment frame', 'shear wall', 'flat plate', 'post-tensioned slab'). Required for engineering assessments, seismic compliance, and BIM documentation.

business_glossary_term: Total Rentable Area in Square Feet (SQF)
description: Total rentable area per BOMA measurement standards, including tenant usable area plus the tenant's pro-rata share of Common Area Maintenance (CAM) spaces. Basis for rent billing and CAM reconciliation.
reference: BOMA International Floor Measurement Standards; FASB ASC 842 Lease Accounting

description: Calendar year in which the building was originally constructed and received its Certificate of Occupancy (COO). Used in depreciation calculations, valuation models, and ESG reporting.

description: Floor Area Ratio (FAR) — the ratio of total building floor area to the area of the land parcel. Represents the maximum allowable development density per zoning regulations. Critical for development feasibility and air rights analysis. [Moved from building: FAR (Floor Area Ratio) is computed relative to a specific parcel's land area, not the building in isolation. When a building spans multiple parcels, FAR must be calculated per parcel using coverage_sqft and the parcel's lot_size_sqf. Storing a single FAR on the building record is ambiguous and incorrect in multi-parcel scenarios; it belongs on the site_coverage association where both building floor area and parcel land area are contextually available.]

description: Indicates whether this space can be physically subdivided into smaller demised units to accommodate multiple tenants or co-working configurations. Supports leasing flexibility analysis and TI space planning.

business_glossary_term: Average In-Place Rent Per Square Foot (PSF)
description: Weighted average contractual base rent per square foot (PSF) across all occupied leases as of the snapshot date. Core metric for rent roll analysis, mark-to-market rent spread calculations, and NOI benchmarking against CoStar market comparables.

description: Timestamp when this occupancy record was first created in the silver layer data product. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX Section 302 and data lineage tracking in the Databricks lakehouse.

description: Dollar value of rental income lost due to tenant delinquency, bad debt, or credit concessions during the reporting period. Distinct from vacancy loss; reflects economic occupancy shortfall versus physical occupancy. Used in EGI and NOI calculations.
reference: FASB ASC 842; Argus Enterprise; NCREIF

description: Quality status of this occupancy record as assessed during silver layer ingestion. Clean records are validated against source system; estimated records use interpolated values; reconciling records have discrepancies under investigation; error records failed validation rules.
reference: SOX Section 302; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Ratio of actual rent collected (or contractually due) to potential gross income (PGI) at 100% occupancy, expressed as a decimal. Accounts for rent concessions, free rent periods, and delinquencies. Critical for NOI calculations, DSCR covenant compliance, and investor reporting.
reference: NAREIT; FASB ASC 842; NCREIF; Argus Enterprise

description: Actual rental income after deducting vacancy loss and credit loss from PGI for the reporting period. EGI is the revenue basis for NOI calculations, DSCR covenant testing, and REIT FFO/AFFO reporting.
reference: Argus Enterprise; NCREIF; FASB ASC 842; NAREIT FFO White Paper

description: Total square footage of new leases commenced during the reporting period, without netting out space vacated. Measures leasing velocity and demand activity independent of move-outs. Used in CoStar market analytics and portfolio leasing reports.
reference: CoStar Suite; Urban Land Institute (ULI); NCREIF

description: Flag indicating whether this property qualifies for same-store NOI comparison in the current reporting period (i.e., owned and stabilized for at least 12 consecutive months). Required for REIT same-store NOI reporting per NAREIT guidelines.
reference: NAREIT FFO White Paper; SEC REIT Reporting Requirements

description: Flag indicating whether the property has reached stabilized occupancy (typically 90%+ physical occupancy for 90+ days). Stabilized assets are included in core portfolio NOI reporting; non-stabilized assets are tracked separately during lease-up.
reference: NCREIF; NAREIT; Argus Enterprise

description: Total square footage of leases scheduled to expire within the reporting period. Used for lease expiry schedule reporting, WALE/WALT calculations, and rollover risk assessment in Argus Enterprise cash flow models.
reference: FASB ASC 842; Argus Enterprise; NAREIT

description: Date on which the property entered the active lease-up phase following construction completion or major repositioning. Null for stabilized assets. Used to track lease-up velocity and project stabilization timelines in Argus Enterprise.
reference: Argus Enterprise; Procore; NCREIF

description: Estimated current market rent per square foot (PSF) for comparable space in the submarket as of the snapshot date, sourced from CoStar Suite or broker price opinions (BPO). Used to calculate mark-to-market rent spread and underwrite lease renewal assumptions in Argus.
reference: CoStar Suite; Appraisal Institute; IVSC

description: Granularity at which this occupancy record is measured — building-level for asset management dashboards, unit-level for rent roll reconciliation, portfolio-level for REIT investor reporting.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); NAREIT

description: Change in occupied area square footage between the current and prior reporting period (current occupied SQF minus prior occupied SQF). Positive values indicate demand exceeding supply; negative values indicate space being returned to market. Key CoStar market benchmarking metric.
reference: CoStar Suite; NCREIF; Urban Land Institute (ULI)

description: Square footage of brand-new lease agreements (not renewals or expansions) that commenced during the reporting period. Distinguishes new demand from renewal activity for leasing performance attribution.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); CoStar Suite; Yardi Voyager

description: Total square footage of GLA currently under an active lease or occupancy agreement as of the snapshot date. Core input for physical occupancy rate calculation and NOI modeling in Argus Enterprise.
reference: BOMA International; NAREIT; Argus Enterprise

description: Number of units currently under an active lease or occupancy agreement as of the snapshot date. Used for unit-based occupancy rate reporting in multifamily portfolios and MRI residential management.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); MRI Software; NAREIT

description: Granularity of the occupancy reporting period. Monthly periods support rent roll reconciliation; quarterly periods align with REIT FFO/AFFO investor reporting; point-in-time supports ad hoc snapshots.
reference: SEC REIT Reporting Requirements; NAREIT FFO White Paper

description: Ratio of physically occupied area to total GLA, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9250 = 92.50%). Measures actual space utilization regardless of rent collection status. Key metric for asset management dashboards and REIT same-store comparisons.
reference: NAREIT; NCREIF; SEC REIT Reporting Requirements

description: Total rental income the property would generate if 100% occupied at market rent for the full reporting period. PGI is the starting point for NOI calculations and EGI derivation in Argus Enterprise cash flow models.
reference: Argus Enterprise; NCREIF; Appraisal Institute; IVSC

description: Lifecycle status of this occupancy record within the data pipeline. Draft records are pending validation; published records are approved for investor reporting; superseded records have been replaced by a revised snapshot for the same period.
reference: SOX Section 302; SEC REIT Reporting Requirements

description: Square footage of lease renewals executed during the reporting period. Supports tenant retention rate analysis, WALE/WALT calculations, and same-store NOI stability assessment.
reference: FASB ASC 842; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); Yardi Voyager

description: The last calendar date of the reporting period (month or quarter) covered by this occupancy snapshot. Together with start date defines the exact measurement window.
reference: FASB ASC 842; SEC REIT Reporting Requirements

description: The first calendar date of the reporting period (month or quarter) covered by this occupancy snapshot. Used for same-store NOI comparisons and REIT FFO/AFFO period alignment.
reference: FASB ASC 842; SEC REIT Reporting Requirements

description: The specific calendar date on which the occupancy measurement was captured or as-of date for point-in-time snapshots. Enables historical trend analysis and WALE/WALT calculations.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); NAREIT

description: Operational system of record from which this occupancy data was extracted (e.g., Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, RealPage). Required for data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail in the Databricks silver layer.
reference: SOX Section 302; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: The native identifier of this occupancy record in the originating source system (e.g., Yardi Voyager property occupancy record ID, MRI occupancy snapshot key). Enables reconciliation between the silver layer and the operational system of record.
reference: SOX Section 302; Yardi Voyager; MRI Software

description: Date on which the property first achieved stabilized occupancy status. Marks the transition from lease-up to core portfolio reporting. Used for same-store inclusion eligibility and investment performance benchmarking.
reference: NCREIF; NAREIT; Argus Enterprise

business_glossary_term: Total Gross Leasable Area (GLA) Square Feet
description: Total Gross Leasable Area (GLA) of the property or unit in square feet as of the snapshot date. GLA is the standard denominator for occupancy rate calculations, NOI per square foot (PSF) analysis, and CAP rate benchmarking.
reference: BOMA International; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); FASB ASC 842

business_glossary_term: Total Net Leasable Area (NLA) Square Feet
description: Total Net Leasable Area (NLA) of the property or unit in square feet, excluding common areas and building core. NLA is used for NNN lease calculations, CAM reconciliation, and tenant PSF rent comparisons.
reference: BOMA International; FASB ASC 842; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Total number of leasable units within the property or building as of the snapshot date. Used as the denominator for unit-based occupancy rate calculations in multifamily and mixed-use assets tracked in MRI Software.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); MRI Software

description: Number of units where the tenant has issued a notice to vacate but the lease has not yet expired. Critical for forward-looking vacancy forecasting, WALE/WALT calculations, and leasing team pipeline management.
reference: FASB ASC 842; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); Yardi Voyager

description: Timestamp when this occupancy record was last modified in the silver layer, including revisions, restatements, or data quality corrections. Required for change data capture (CDC) and SOX audit trail compliance.
reference: SOX Section 302; GDPR Article 5

description: Dollar value of rental income lost due to vacant space during the reporting period (PGI minus income from occupied space). Used in NOI statements, EGI calculations, and investor reporting for portfolio performance monitoring.
reference: Argus Enterprise; NCREIF; Appraisal Institute

description: Total square footage of GLA not under any active lease or occupancy agreement as of the snapshot date. Equals total GLA minus occupied area. Used for vacancy loss calculations in NOI statements and EGI modeling.
reference: BOMA International; NAREIT; Argus Enterprise

description: Number of units not under any active lease or occupancy agreement as of the snapshot date. Equals total unit count minus occupied unit count. Supports vacancy trend analysis and leasing pipeline prioritization.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); MRI Software

description: Weighted Average Lease Expiry (WALE) in years as of the snapshot date, weighted by GLA. Measures the average time until leases expire across the occupied portfolio. Core metric for REIT investor reporting, debt covenant compliance, and asset management risk assessment.
reference: NAREIT; NCREIF; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Weighted Average Lease Term (WALT) in years as of the snapshot date, weighted by GLA. Measures the average remaining lease term across the occupied portfolio. Distinct from WALE in that it reflects the full contracted term rather than expiry proximity. Used in Argus Enterprise DCF modeling.
reference: NAREIT; Argus Enterprise; NCREIF

description: The date as of which the concluded value opinion is valid (also known as the value date). This is the principal real-world event date for the appraisal and may differ from the report date. Critical for investment decision-making and financial reporting under ASC 842 and IFRS 16.

description: Narrative description of any extraordinary assumptions made in the appraisal that, if found to be false, could alter the appraiser's opinions or conclusions. Required USPAP disclosure. Examples include assumed lease-up timelines or assumed environmental clearance.
reference: USPAP Standards Rule 2-2; Appraisal Institute; IVSC IVS 101

business_glossary_term: Gross Building Area (GBA) in Square Feet (SQF)
description: The total gross building area of the subject property in square feet (SQF) as measured or verified by the appraiser. Used to calculate per-square-foot (PSF) value indicators and compare to market comparables.
reference: Appraisal Institute; BOMA International Building Measurement Standards; RESO

description: The assumed investment holding period in years used in the Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis. Typically ranges from 5 to 15 years for commercial real estate. Drives the terminal value calculation.
reference: Appraisal Institute; IVSC IVS 105; Argus Enterprise

description: Narrative description of any hypothetical conditions applied in the appraisal that are contrary to known facts but assumed for the purpose of analysis (e.g., assuming a proposed building is complete as of the effective date). Required USPAP disclosure.
reference: USPAP Standards Rule 2-2; Appraisal Institute

description: The date on which the appraiser physically inspected the subject property. A desktop or drive-by appraisal may have no inspection date. Required for USPAP compliance certification.
reference: USPAP Standards Rule 2-2; Appraisal Institute

description: The stated purpose for which the appraisal report is intended to be used, as required by USPAP (e.g., mortgage financing, financial reporting, portfolio valuation, acquisition due diligence, estate planning, litigation support). Determines the scope of work and applicable standards.
reference: USPAP Standards Rule 2-2; Appraisal Institute; FIRREA

description: The party or parties identified by the appraiser as the intended user(s) of the appraisal report (e.g., lender name, client organization, REIT investment committee). Required USPAP disclosure. Restricts reliance on the report to named parties.
reference: USPAP Standards Rule 2-2; Appraisal Institute

description: The Internal Rate of Return (IRR) output from the DCF analysis, expressed as a decimal. Represents the annualized rate of return that equates the present value of projected cash flows to the concluded value. Key metric for investment decision-making.
reference: Appraisal Institute; IVSC IVS 105; Argus Enterprise; CFA Institute

description: Summary of the general and specific limiting conditions that govern the scope and reliance of the appraisal report. Standard USPAP certification element included in all appraisal reports.
reference: USPAP Standards Rule 2-2; Appraisal Institute

description: The Net Present Value (NPV) output from the DCF analysis, representing the present value of projected future cash flows discounted at the applied discount rate. Supports investment advisory and portfolio management decisions.
reference: Appraisal Institute; IVSC IVS 105; Argus Enterprise; CFA Institute

description: The count of comparable sales transactions analyzed and used in the sales comparison approach. Provides a quality indicator for the depth of market evidence supporting the concluded value.
reference: Appraisal Institute; USPAP Standards Rule 1-4; CoStar Suite comparable data

description: The date on which the appraisal report was signed and issued by the appraiser. May differ from the effective date of value, particularly for retrospective or prospective appraisals.
reference: USPAP Standards Rule 2-2; Appraisal Institute; IVSC IVS 101

description: The format type of the appraisal report as defined by USPAP and Fannie Mae guidelines. URAR (Uniform Residential Appraisal Report) is standard for residential; narrative and summary formats are common for commercial CRE engagements.
reference: USPAP Standards Rule 2-2; Fannie Mae Form 1004; Appraisal Institute

description: Externally-known unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to the report by the appraisal firm or internal valuation team. Used for cross-referencing with Argus Enterprise and client deliverables.
reference: Appraisal Institute; USPAP Standards Rule 2-2

description: The valuation scenario represented by this report. As-Is reflects current condition; As-Stabilized assumes full occupancy; As-Complete assumes construction completion; As-Dark assumes vacant/non-operating; Prospective reflects a future date. One engagement may produce multiple scenario reports.
reference: Appraisal Institute; IVSC IVS 400; USPAP Advisory Opinion 17

description: Current lifecycle state of the appraisal report workflow. Tracks progression from initial draft through internal review, approval, client issuance, and potential supersession or withdrawal.
reference: USPAP; Appraisal Institute; IVSC IVS 101

description: The current status of the internal or external review process for this appraisal report. Tracks whether a supervisory or desk review has been initiated, completed, approved, or rejected per Appraisal Institute and USPAP Standards Rule 3.
reference: USPAP Standards Rule 3; Appraisal Institute; OCC Interagency Appraisal Guidelines

description: The date on which the designated reviewer completed and approved the review of this appraisal report. Null if review is not yet completed or not required.
reference: USPAP Standards Rule 3; Appraisal Institute; OCC Interagency Appraisal Guidelines

description: Description of the type and extent of research and analyses applied in the appraisal assignment, as required by USPAP Scope of Work Rule. Includes data sources consulted, inspection type, and approaches to value considered.
reference: USPAP Scope of Work Rule; Appraisal Institute; IVSC IVS 101

description: The reversion or terminal CAP rate applied to the projected NOI at the end of the DCF holding period to estimate the terminal/residual value of the property. Used in Argus Enterprise DCF models.
reference: Appraisal Institute; IVSC IVS 105; Argus Enterprise

description: The timestamp when this appraisal report record was last modified in the Silver Layer data platform. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and data freshness monitoring.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; ISO 55000 Asset Management; GDPR Article 5

business_glossary_term: Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice (USPAP) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether the report was prepared in compliance with USPAP (Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice) as required by the Appraisal Institute and applicable US regulations.
reference: USPAP; Appraisal Institute; Financial Institutions Reform Recovery and Enforcement Act (FIRREA)

description: Primary valuation methodology applied in the report. Sales Comparison uses comparable transactions; Income Capitalization applies CAP rate to NOI; Cost approach estimates replacement cost less depreciation; DCF (Discounted Cash Flow) models future cash flows; Hybrid combines multiple approaches.
reference: IVSC IVS 105 Valuation Approaches; Appraisal Institute; USPAP Standards Rule 1-4

description: The concluded market value expressed on a per-square-foot (PSF) basis, calculated as concluded market value divided by gross building area. Key benchmarking metric for CRE investment analysis and market comparison.
reference: Appraisal Institute; CoStar Suite; NCREIF

description: The basis of value concluded in the report. Market Value is the most common standard; Investment Value reflects a specific investor's criteria; Liquidation Value applies to distressed scenarios; Insurable Value is used for insurance purposes; Assessed Value is used for tax purposes.
reference: IVSC IVS 104 Bases of Value; Appraisal Institute; USPAP

description: Primary key for approach
Reference to the parent valuation engagement or appraisal report to which this approach record belongs. Links the approach to its overarching valuation assignment.
reference: IVSC IVS 101 — Scope of Work; Appraisal Institute USPAP

description: Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: Approach outputs (concluded_market_rent_psf, replacement_cost_new) are expressed per area unit; the UOM reference ensures consistent measurement standards across cross-border portfolios. IVSC and RICS
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the subject property being valued under this approach. Ties the valuation approach to the specific real estate asset in the property master record.
reference: RESO Data Dictionary; IVSC IVS 105

description: Foreign key linking to reference.building_class. Business justification: Valuation approaches are calibrated by building class which affects cap rates, depreciation rates, and market rent benchmarks. The income approach and cost approach produce materially different output
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to valuation.cap_rate_benchmark. Business justification: valuation_approach uses cap_rate in direct capitalization. The applied cap rate should be benchmarked against market data. Adding cap_rate_benchmark_id links the approach model to the market benchmark
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to reference.construction_type. Business justification: The cost approach requires construction type to determine replacement_cost_new, depreciation method, and useful economic life. USPAP cost approach methodology explicitly requires construction type cla
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Valuation approach financial outputs (concluded_value, pgi, egi, noi, land_value, replacement_cost_new) are currency-denominated. Normalizing to a FK enables consistent currency handling in multi-curr
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Valuation approaches require submarket-level geographic context for market rent benchmarking and cap rate selection. The income approach uses submarket vacancy rates and rent growth assumptions; geogr
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to reference.lease_type. Business justification: The income approach requires lease type classification to determine expense recovery structure, effective gross income, and NOI. NNN vs gross lease structures produce materially different NOI calculat
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to reference.market_segment. Business justification: Valuation approaches are calibrated to specific investment market segments (core, value-add, opportunistic) which determines discount rates, hold period assumptions, and investor return expectations.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to marketing.market_survey. Business justification: Income approach and sales comparison approach valuations rely on market survey data for concluded_market_rent_psf, vacancy_credit_loss_rate, and pgi inputs. Appraisers cite the specific market survey
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.noi_statement. Business justification: The income approach in valuation_approach uses NOI as its primary input. Linking to the authoritative NOI statement documents the source of the NOI figure used in the income approach, supporting USPAP
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to property.parcel. Business justification: The land residual and cost approaches in appraisals require direct reference to the parcel for land value extraction. Approach links to asset but not parcel. For land valuation and development feasibi
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Valuation approach parameters (cap rate, GRM, cost indices, depreciation rates) are property-type-specific. A domain expert would expect the income, sales comparison, and cost approaches to reference
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: Valuation approaches for commercial leasehold interests are applied at the space level. The income approach for a specific suite requires space-level rent and area data. Approach links to asset but no
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to reference.space_use_type. Business justification: Income and cost approaches are calibrated differently by space use type (office vs retail vs industrial vs multifamily). Space use type drives concluded_market_rent_psf, load_factor, and expense recov
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to reference.transaction_type. Business justification: Valuation approaches are prepared for specific transaction types (sale, financing, estate, litigation) which determines the standard of value and methodology applied. USPAP requires the intended trans
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Effective date as of which the valuation approach analysis is performed. Represents the appraisal effective date for this specific methodology, which may differ from the report date.
reference: IVSC IVS 101 — Scope of Work; USPAP Standards Rule 1-2(b)

description: Current lifecycle state of this valuation approach record within the appraisal workflow. Tracks progression from initial draft through peer review, approval, and potential supersession by a revised approach.
reference: IVSC IVS 101 — Scope of Work; Appraisal Institute USPAP

description: Discriminator identifying the valuation methodology applied in this record. Supported types: direct_cap (Direct Capitalization), DCF (Discounted Cash Flow), cost (Cost Approach), sales_comparison (Sales Comparison Approach), GRM (Gross Rent Multiplier). Drives which set of methodology-specific fields are populated.
reference: IVSC IVS 105 — Valuation Approaches and Methods; Appraisal Institute USPAP Standard 1

description: Date on which this valuation approach was formally approved by the reviewing appraiser or valuation committee. Marks the transition from in_review to approved status in the approach lifecycle.
reference: Appraisal Institute USPAP; IVSC IVS 101 — Scope of Work

description: Reference identifier or file path for the Argus Enterprise cash flow model associated with this DCF valuation approach. Enables traceability back to the source Argus model for audit, review, and replication purposes. Populated for DCF approach type.
reference: Argus Enterprise — Valuation and Cash Flow Modeling; IVSC IVS 101 — Scope of Work

description: Overall capitalization rate (CAP rate) applied to stabilized NOI to derive the direct capitalization value. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0550 = 5.50%). Derived from market comparable sales analysis and reflects investor return expectations for the asset class and submarket.
reference: Appraisal Institute — The Appraisal of Real Estate, 15th Ed.; IVSC IVS 105; CoStar Market Analytics

description: Number of comparable sales or lease transactions used to support the approach inputs (e.g., CAP rate derivation for direct cap, market rent for income approaches, adjusted sales for sales comparison). Indicates the depth of market evidence supporting the concluded value.
reference: Appraisal Institute USPAP Standard 1; IVSC IVS 105; CoStar Comparable Data

business_glossary_term: Concluded Market Rent Per Square Foot (PSF)
description: Appraiser's concluded market rent per square foot (PSF) used as the basis for PGI estimation in income capitalization approaches. Derived from comparable lease analysis and market surveys. Expressed in the reporting currency per square foot per year.
reference: Appraisal Institute — The Appraisal of Real Estate, 15th Ed.; RESO Data Dictionary; CoStar Comparable Lease Data

description: The indicated market value produced by this specific valuation approach before reconciliation weighting. Represents the raw output of the methodology (e.g., direct capitalization value, DCF NPV, depreciated cost, or sales comparison adjusted value) in the reporting currency.
reference: IVSC IVS 105; FASB ASC 820 — Fair Value Measurement

description: Source of the construction cost index or cost manual used to estimate RCN in the cost approach. Common sources include Marshall & Swift Valuation Service and RSMeans Cost Data. Supports methodology transparency and IVSC IVS 105 compliance documentation.
reference: IVSC IVS 105 — Cost Approach; Marshall & Swift Valuation Service; RSMeans Cost Data

description: Timestamp recording when this valuation approach record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls for valuation records used in financial reporting.
reference: SOX Section 404 — Internal Controls; FASB ASC 820; GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity

description: Net value of the improvements after deducting total accrued depreciation (physical, functional, and external obsolescence) from RCN. Added to land value to derive the cost approach indicated value. Populated for cost approach type.
reference: IVSC IVS 105 — Cost Approach; Appraisal Institute USPAP

description: Indicated value derived by dividing stabilized NOI by the overall CAP rate (NOI / CAP Rate). Represents the concluded value under the direct capitalization method. Populated only when approach_type = direct_cap.
reference: Appraisal Institute — The Appraisal of Real Estate, 15th Ed.; IVSC IVS 105

description: Risk-adjusted discount rate (expressed as a decimal) used in the DCF analysis to convert projected future cash flows to present value. Reflects the required rate of return for the investment, incorporating market risk, asset type, and leverage. Populated for DCF approach type.
reference: IVSC IVS 105; Argus Enterprise DCF Methodology; Appraisal Institute

description: Total estimated economic life of the improvements in years — the period over which the building is expected to contribute positively to property value. Used with effective age to calculate the age-life depreciation ratio in the cost approach.
reference: IVSC IVS 105 — Cost Approach; Appraisal Institute — The Appraisal of Real Estate, 15th Ed.

description: Appraiser's estimate of the effective age of the improvements in years, reflecting the actual condition and utility of the building relative to a new structure of similar type. May differ from chronological age due to renovations or deferred maintenance. Used in cost approach depreciation calculations.
reference: IVSC IVS 105 — Cost Approach; Appraisal Institute — The Appraisal of Real Estate, 15th Ed.

description: Effective Gross Income — PGI adjusted for vacancy, credit loss, and other income (e.g., parking, ancillary revenue). Represents the realistic annual income the property is expected to generate. Populated for direct_cap and DCF approach types.
reference: Appraisal Institute — The Appraisal of Real Estate, 15th Ed.; IVSC IVS 105

description: Loss in value caused by factors external to the property, such as adverse market conditions, neighborhood decline, or proximity to negative influences. Also known as economic obsolescence. Deducted from RCN in the cost approach.
reference: IVSC IVS 105 — Cost Approach; Appraisal Institute — The Appraisal of Real Estate, 15th Ed.

description: Loss in value resulting from deficiencies or superadequacies in the design, layout, or functionality of the improvements relative to current market standards (e.g., outdated floor plans, inadequate HVAC systems). Deducted from RCN in the cost approach.
reference: IVSC IVS 105 — Cost Approach; Appraisal Institute — The Appraisal of Real Estate, 15th Ed.

description: Gross Rent Multiplier — the ratio of property value to gross annual (or monthly) rental income, derived from comparable sales. Applied to the subject property's gross rent to estimate value under the GRM approach. Populated when approach_type = GRM.
reference: Appraisal Institute — The Appraisal of Real Estate, 15th Ed.; IVSC IVS 105

description: Projected investment hold period in years used in the DCF analysis. Defines the number of annual cash flow periods modeled before the terminal reversion value is calculated. Typically ranges from 5 to 15 years for commercial real estate. Populated for DCF approach type.
reference: IVSC IVS 105; Argus Enterprise DCF Methodology

description: Internal Rate of Return — the discount rate at which the net present value of all projected cash flows (including acquisition cost and terminal reversion) equals zero. Key investment performance metric output from the DCF model. Populated for DCF approach type.
reference: IVSC IVS 105; Argus Enterprise; CFA Institute — Real Estate Valuation

description: IVSC IVS 105 methodology classification code identifying the specific valuation technique applied (e.g., IVS105-IC for Income Capitalization, IVS105-CA for Cost Approach, IVS105-SC for Sales Comparison). Supports regulatory compliance documentation and cross-border reporting.
reference: IVSC IVS 105 — Valuation Approaches and Methods

description: Estimated market value of the land component as if vacant and available for its highest and best use. Used in the cost approach as the foundation to which depreciated cost of improvements is added. Populated for cost approach type.
reference: IVSC IVS 105 — Cost Approach; Appraisal Institute USPAP

description: Free-text field capturing appraiser commentary on the rationale, assumptions, limitations, and adjustments specific to this valuation approach. Supports IVSC IVS 105 compliance, USPAP disclosure requirements, and peer review documentation.
reference: IVSC IVS 105; Appraisal Institute USPAP Standards Rule 2-2; FASB ASC 820

description: Net Operating Income — EGI minus total OPEX. The primary income metric used in direct capitalization and DCF valuation. Represents the annual pre-debt, pre-tax cash flow generated by the property. Populated for direct_cap and DCF approach types.
reference: Appraisal Institute — The Appraisal of Real Estate, 15th Ed.; IVSC IVS 105; FASB ASC 820

description: Net Present Value — the sum of all discounted future cash flows minus the initial investment cost, as output by the DCF model. A positive NPV indicates value creation above the required return. Populated for DCF approach type.
reference: IVSC IVS 105; Argus Enterprise; FASB ASC 820

description: Potential Gross Income — the maximum annual rental revenue the property could generate at 100% occupancy at market rent. Used as the starting point for income capitalization approaches (direct cap and DCF). Populated for direct_cap and DCF approach types.
reference: Appraisal Institute — The Appraisal of Real Estate, 15th Ed.; IVSC IVS 105

description: Loss in value attributable to physical deterioration of the improvements, including curable deferred maintenance and incurable structural wear. Deducted from RCN in the cost approach. Populated for cost approach type.
reference: IVSC IVS 105 — Cost Approach; Appraisal Institute — The Appraisal of Real Estate, 15th Ed.

description: Basis on which the concluded market rent is expressed. Indicates whether the rent is annual or monthly and the lease structure (NNN — Triple Net, gross, or modified gross) that underpins the income analysis.
reference: Appraisal Institute; RESO Data Dictionary; NAR Lease Standards

description: Replacement Cost New (RCN) — the estimated cost to construct a building of equivalent utility using current materials, standards, and design at current prices. Starting point for the cost approach before depreciation deductions. Populated for cost approach type.
reference: IVSC IVS 105 — Cost Approach; Marshall & Swift Valuation Service; RSMeans Cost Data

description: Narrative description of key sensitivity scenarios tested for this approach, such as the impact of a 25 bps change in CAP rate or discount rate on concluded value. Supports investment decision-making and risk disclosure in appraisal reports.
reference: IVSC IVS 105; Argus Enterprise Sensitivity Analysis; Appraisal Institute

description: Reversion or terminal CAP rate applied to the projected NOI in the final year of the hold period to estimate the property's residual (terminal) value in a DCF analysis. Typically higher than the going-in CAP rate to reflect future uncertainty. Populated for DCF approach type.
reference: IVSC IVS 105; Argus Enterprise DCF Methodology; Appraisal Institute

description: Total annual operating expenses (OPEX) for the subject property, including property taxes, insurance, management fees, maintenance, utilities, and reserves for replacement. Deducted from EGI to derive NOI. Populated for income capitalization approaches.
reference: Appraisal Institute — The Appraisal of Real Estate, 15th Ed.; IVSC IVS 105

description: Timestamp recording the most recent modification to this valuation approach record. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SOX Section 404 — Internal Controls; FASB ASC 820; ISO 55000 — Asset Management

description: Estimated rate (decimal fraction) applied to PGI to account for expected vacancy and uncollectible rent. For example, 0.05 represents a 5% vacancy and credit loss allowance. Used in income capitalization to derive EGI from PGI.
reference: Appraisal Institute — The Appraisal of Real Estate, 15th Ed.; IVSC IVS 105

description: Weighting factor (expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1) assigned to this approach in the final value reconciliation. For example, 0.60 indicates this approach contributes 60% to the concluded value. All approach weights for a given valuation should sum to 1.0.
reference: IVSC IVS 105 §50 — Reconciliation; Appraisal Institute USPAP Standard 1

description: Dollar value of any tax abatement granted by the taxing authority (e.g., development incentive abatement, payment-in-lieu-of-taxes (PILOT) arrangement). Distinct from exemptions; abatements are typically time-limited incentives tied to development agreements.
reference: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD); Urban Land Institute (ULI)

description: Date on which any tax abatement arrangement expires, after which the full tax liability applies. Critical for long-term cash flow modeling in Argus Enterprise and investment underwriting — abatement burn-off materially impacts NOI (Net Operating Income) projections.
reference: Urban Land Institute (ULI); FASB ASC 450

description: Statutory deadline by which a formal appeal of the assessed value must be filed with the taxing authority. Critical compliance date tracked in Yardi Voyager and DocuSign CLM for deadline management.
reference: National Association of Realtors (NAR); FASB ASC 450 Contingencies

description: Date on which the formal appeal was submitted to the taxing authority or appeals board. Null if no appeal has been filed. Used for tracking appeal timelines and legal deadlines.
reference: FASB ASC 450 Contingencies; National Association of Realtors (NAR)

description: Date on which the taxing authority or appeals board issued a final decision on the appeal. Null if appeal is still pending. Triggers revised tax liability calculations.
reference: FASB ASC 450 Contingencies

description: Final assessed value agreed upon or ordered as a result of the appeal process. Null if appeal is pending or not filed. Replaces the original assessed value for tax liability calculation upon resolution.
reference: Appraisal Institute; IVSC Valuation Standards; FASB ASC 450

description: Current status of any formal appeal filed against this assessment. Tracks the appeal lifecycle from filing through final determination. Informs contingent liability disclosures under FASB ASC 450. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_appealed|appeal_filed|hearing_scheduled|decision_pending|appeal_granted|appeal_denied — promote to reference product]
reference: FASB ASC 450 Contingencies; SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act)

description: The assessed value the property owner is seeking through the appeal process. Represents the owner's position on fair assessed value, typically supported by an independent appraisal or BPO (Broker Price Opinion). Used to quantify potential tax savings from a successful appeal.
reference: Appraisal Institute; IVSC Valuation Standards; FASB ASC 450

description: Reference identifier for the Argus Enterprise cash flow model in which this tax assessment's OPEX (Operating Expenditure) is incorporated. Enables traceability between the assessed tax expense and the DCF (Discounted Cash Flow) valuation model.
reference: Argus Enterprise Valuation and Cash Flow Modeling

description: Dollar value assigned by the taxing authority to the building and structural improvements on the property, exclusive of land. Drives depreciation analysis and CAPEX (Capital Expenditure) planning in Argus Enterprise models.
reference: FASB ASC 360 Property Plant and Equipment; IVSC Valuation Standards; Appraisal Institute

description: Dollar value assigned by the taxing authority to the land component of the property, exclusive of improvements. Used in land vs. improvement allocation analysis and depreciation studies. Sourced from Yardi Voyager Fixed Assets and Argus Enterprise.
reference: FASB ASC 360 Property Plant and Equipment; IVSC Valuation Standards; Appraisal Institute

description: The ratio of assessed value to estimated market value as applied by the taxing jurisdiction (e.g., 0.80 = 80% of market value). Varies by jurisdiction and property class. Used to back-calculate implied market value from assessed value for investment analysis.
reference: International Association of Assessing Officers (IAAO); Appraisal Institute; IVSC Valuation Standards

description: Current lifecycle state of the tax assessment record. Tracks progression from preliminary notice through certification, appeal, and final determination. Drives workflow in Yardi Voyager property accounting.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); FASB ASC 450 Contingencies

description: The tax year (calendar year) for which this assessment applies. Drives annual property tax expense calculations and year-over-year valuation trend analysis. Critical for NOI (Net Operating Income) OPEX reporting.
reference: FASB ASC 840/842; IVSC Valuation Standards

description: Phone number or contact detail for the assessor's office. Used for inquiry and appeal coordination. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.

description: Name of the government assessor or assessment office responsible for this assessment. Used for correspondence, appeal filings, and audit documentation.
reference: National Association of Realtors (NAR)

description: Timestamp when this tax assessment record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) financial controls.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); GDPR Article 5 (data integrity)

description: The effective property tax rate expressed as a decimal percentage of the total assessed value (e.g., 0.0125 = 1.25%). Accounts for all applicable levies across overlapping jurisdictions. Used in NOI (Net Operating Income) modeling and CAP Rate (Capitalization Rate) analysis in Argus Enterprise.
reference: IVSC Valuation Standards; Appraisal Institute; FASB ASC 740

description: Total dollar value of tax exemptions applied to this assessment (e.g., homestead exemption, REIT (Real Estate Investment Trust) exemption, historic preservation, enterprise zone). Reduces the taxable assessed value or gross tax liability.
reference: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD); SEC — for publicly traded REITs; FASB ASC 740

description: Classification of the primary tax exemption applied to this assessment. Determines eligibility criteria and renewal requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: homestead|senior|veteran|nonprofit|historic|enterprise_zone|reit — promote to reference product]
reference: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD); SEC — for publicly traded REITs

description: Gross annual property tax liability before application of any exemptions, abatements, or credits. Computed from assessed value and millage rate. Key OPEX (Operating Expenditure) component in NOI (Net Operating Income) calculations and Argus Enterprise cash flow models.
reference: FASB ASC 450; IVSC Valuation Standards; SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act)

description: Estimated market value implied by the taxing authority's assessment, calculated as total assessed value divided by the assessment ratio. Provides a reference point for comparing assessed value against independent appraisals and Argus Enterprise DCF (Discounted Cash Flow) valuations.
reference: IVSC Valuation Standards; Appraisal Institute; FASB ASC 820 Fair Value Measurement

description: Number of installment payments into which the annual tax liability is divided (e.g., 1 = lump sum, 2 = semi-annual, 4 = quarterly). Determines AP (Accounts Payable) payment scheduling and cash flow timing in Yardi Voyager.
reference: Yardi Voyager Accounts Payable

description: Indicates whether the property tax expense is recoverable from tenants under NNN (Triple Net Lease) or modified gross lease structures. True = tax is passed through to tenants as a CAM (Common Area Maintenance) or operating expense recovery; False = landlord bears the tax expense. Critical for EGI (Effective Gross Income) and NOI (Net Operating Income) modeling.
reference: FASB ASC 842 Lease Accounting; IASB IFRS 16 Leases; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Classification of the taxing authority by governmental level. Properties may have multiple overlapping jurisdictions; this field identifies the type for each assessment record.
reference: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

description: The tax rate expressed in mills (dollars of tax per $1,000 of assessed value) levied by the taxing jurisdiction. Multiplied by the assessed value to compute the gross annual tax liability before exemptions.
reference: National Association of Realtors (NAR); U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

description: Net annual property tax liability after deducting all exemptions, abatements, and credits from the gross tax. This is the actual cash tax expense recorded in Yardi Voyager GL (General Ledger) and reported as a property OPEX (Operating Expenditure) line item in NOI (Net Operating Income) statements.
reference: FASB ASC 450; FASB ASC 740; SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); IVSC Valuation Standards

description: Date the taxing authority issued the official assessment notice to the property owner. Establishes the start of the appeal window and triggers internal review processes.
reference: National Association of Realtors (NAR); U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

description: Date by which the property tax payment must be remitted to the taxing authority to avoid penalties and interest. Drives AP (Accounts Payable) scheduling in Yardi Voyager and cash flow planning.
reference: Yardi Voyager Accounts Payable; FASB ASC 450

description: Total assessed value from the immediately preceding tax year. Enables year-over-year change analysis and supports appeal arguments based on disproportionate value increases. Used in Argus Enterprise cash flow modeling.
reference: IVSC Valuation Standards; Appraisal Institute

description: Assessor's classification code for the property type (e.g., residential, commercial, industrial, agricultural). Determines applicable assessment methodology, exemption eligibility, and tax rate schedule.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); International Association of Assessing Officers (IAAO)

description: Free-text notes entered by the internal reviewer documenting assessment analysis, appeal rationale, comparable evidence, or instructions for the AP (Accounts Payable) team. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act).
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); Appraisal Institute

description: Official tax bill number issued by the taxing authority for the annual tax payment. Used for AP (Accounts Payable) invoice matching in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA to ensure accurate tax remittance.
reference: Yardi Voyager Accounts Payable; SAP S/4HANA Financial Consolidation

description: Annual net property tax liability expressed on a per-square-foot (PSF) basis. Enables benchmarking across properties of different sizes and is a standard metric in NNN (Triple Net Lease) lease analysis and CAM (Common Area Maintenance) reconciliations.

description: Total assessed value of the property as determined by the taxing authority, representing the sum of assessed land value and assessed improvement value. reference: FASB ASC 360; IVSC Valuation Standards; Appraisal Institute; International Association of Assessing Officers (IAAO)

description: Timestamp when this tax assessment record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Tracks revisions from appeal outcomes, value corrections, or payment status updates. Required for SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) audit trail.

description: The upper bound of the surveyed CAP rate range for this property type, asset class, and submarket combination, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0650 = 6.50%). Represents the most conservative (highest) CAP rate observed, typically associated with lower-quality assets or less competitive submarkets.
reference: Appraisal Institute; IVSC IVS 105; NCREIF

description: The lower bound of the surveyed CAP rate range for this property type, asset class, and submarket combination, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0450 = 4.50%). Represents the most aggressive (lowest) CAP rate observed in the market, typically associated with the highest-quality assets or most competitive submarkets.
reference: Appraisal Institute; IVSC IVS 105; NCREIF

description: The midpoint or median of the surveyed CAP rate range, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0550 = 5.50%). This is the primary benchmark value used by appraisers for CAP rate selection validation and by asset managers for NAV/GAV calculations in Argus Enterprise models.
reference: Appraisal Institute; IVSC IVS 105; NCREIF; PwC/Korpacz Real Estate Investor Survey

business_glossary_term: Prior Period Capitalization Rate (CAP Rate) Mid
description: The midpoint CAP rate from the immediately preceding survey period for the same property type, asset class, and submarket. Used to calculate period-over-period movement and validate the trend_direction field. Supports time-series analysis in investment committee presentations.
reference: Appraisal Institute; NCREIF; PwC/Korpacz Real Estate Investor Survey

description: The CoStar Suite internal market identifier for the submarket or market area covered by this benchmark. Enables direct linkage to CoStar market analytics, comparable transaction data, and property analytics for benchmark validation and enrichment.

description: The timestamp when this benchmark record was first created and loaded into the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and record management in compliance with SOX financial controls and GDPR data governance requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5; ISO 8000

description: A numeric score (0.00–100.00) representing the assessed quality and reliability of this benchmark record based on sample size, source credibility, methodology transparency, and recency. Used by the data governance team to flag low-confidence benchmarks and by appraisers to weight multiple sources.
reference: ISO 8000 Data Quality; Appraisal Institute USPAP; IVSC IVS 105

description: The date from which this benchmark record is considered valid and applicable for use in appraisals, NAV/GAV calculations, and investment committee presentations. May differ from survey_date if there is a publication lag.
reference: FASB ASC 820; IVSC IVS 105

description: The date after which this benchmark record is no longer considered current and should not be used for new valuations without review. Typically set to the next expected survey publication date.
reference: Appraisal Institute; IVSC IVS 105

business_glossary_term: Implied Value Per Square Foot (PSF) High
description: The upper bound of the implied market value per square foot derived from the CAP rate range and prevailing NOI levels for this segment. Used in conjunction with implied_value_psf_low to establish a market value range for portfolio-level reporting.
reference: Appraisal Institute; IVSC IVS 105; Argus Enterprise

business_glossary_term: Implied Value Per Square Foot (PSF) Low
description: The lower bound of the implied market value per square foot derived from the CAP rate range and prevailing NOI levels for this segment. Provides a sanity-check metric for appraiser CAP rate selections and supports portfolio-level GAV/NAV estimation.
reference: Appraisal Institute; IVSC IVS 105; Argus Enterprise

description: The prevailing investor sentiment toward this property type and market segment as reported in the source survey (bullish, neutral, bearish). Provides qualitative market context for investment committee presentations and portfolio strategy discussions.
reference: PwC/Korpacz Real Estate Investor Survey; Urban Land Institute (ULI) Emerging Trends

description: Indicates whether this benchmark applies specifically to institutional-grade investment properties meeting NCREIF/REIT portfolio standards (True) or to the broader market including non-institutional assets (False). Relevant for REIT portfolio NAV/GAV calculations and SEC reporting.
reference: NCREIF; SEC REIT Reporting; Urban Land Institute (ULI)

description: Indicates whether this record is designated as the primary (preferred) benchmark for the given property type, asset class, and submarket combination when multiple sources exist for the same period. True = primary benchmark to be used in appraisal validation; False = secondary/supplemental benchmark.
reference: Appraisal Institute USPAP; IVSC IVS 105

description: Qualitative assessment of the transaction liquidity in this market segment at the time of the survey (high, moderate, low, illiquid). Informs the reliability weighting of the benchmark and the appropriate discount for illiquidity in investment advisory and REIT portfolio reporting.
reference: IVSC IVS 105; Urban Land Institute (ULI); NCREIF

description: The NCREIF (National Council of Real Estate Investment Fiduciaries) Property Index segment code corresponding to this benchmark's property type and geographic classification. Enables alignment with NCREIF NPI return benchmarks for institutional investment performance reporting.
reference: NCREIF Property Index (NPI); SEC REIT Reporting Requirements

business_glossary_term: Net Operating Income (NOI) Per Square Foot (PSF) High

reference: Appraisal Institute; IVSC IVS 105; NCREIF

business_glossary_term: Net Operating Income (NOI) Per Square Foot (PSF) Low

reference: Appraisal Institute; IVSC IVS 105; NCREIF

description: The number of transactions, properties, or investor respondents included in the source survey for this specific property type, asset class, and submarket segment. A key indicator of statistical reliability; smaller samples warrant greater caution in appraisal application.
reference: Appraisal Institute USPAP; IVSC IVS 105; NCREIF

description: The name of the industry research organization or brokerage firm that published the survey from which this benchmark data was sourced (e.g., CoStar, CBRE, JLL, Cushman & Wakefield, NCREIF, PwC/Korpacz). Governs the credibility weighting applied during appraiser CAP rate selection validation. Enables traceability back to the original source document for audit and IVSC compliance.

description: The date on which the market survey or data collection was conducted or published by the source organization. This is the principal real-world event date for this benchmark record, representing when the CAP rate data was captured from the market.

description: The publication cadence of the source survey (quarterly, semi-annual, annual, monthly). Used to determine the expected expiry_date of each benchmark record and to schedule automated refresh workflows in the data pipeline.
