description: Narrative description of the data collection and analysis methodology used by the source organization to derive the CAP rate benchmarks (e.g., transaction-based, investor survey, broker opinion, hedonic regression). Required for IVSC-compliant methodology metadata and appraiser disclosure in appraisal reports.
reference: IVSC IVS 105; Appraisal Institute USPAP Standard 1; International Valuation Standards Council

description: The aggregate dollar value of all transactions included in the benchmark dataset for this segment and period, expressed in the currency indicated by currency_code. Provides context for the depth and liquidity of the market segment being benchmarked.
reference: NCREIF; CoStar Suite; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: The number of actual closed sale transactions underlying the CAP rate benchmark data for this segment and period, where the source provides transaction-based data. Distinct from sample_size which may include survey respondents. Supports statistical confidence assessment.

business_glossary_term: CAP Rate Trend Basis Points Change
description: The period-over-period change in the midpoint CAP rate expressed in basis points (bps), where 1 bps = 0.01%. Negative values indicate compression; positive values indicate expansion. Provides a precise quantitative measure of market movement to complement the qualitative trend_direction field.
reference: Appraisal Institute; NCREIF; IVSC IVS 105

description: Directional trend of CAP rates in this market segment relative to the prior survey period. Compressing indicates CAP rates are decreasing (values rising); stable indicates no material change; expanding indicates CAP rates are increasing (values falling). Critical context for investment committee presentations and portfolio NAV/GAV assessments.
reference: NCREIF; PwC/Korpacz Real Estate Investor Survey; Urban Land Institute (ULI)

description: The date on which the modeled investment hold period commences, typically the assumed acquisition or ownership transfer date.
reference: Argus Enterprise Hold Period Parameter; IVSC IVS 105; ULI Real Estate Finance

description: Total duration of the modeled investment hold period expressed in whole years (e.g., 10 for a 10-year hold). Drives the number of annual cash flow projection periods.
reference: Argus Enterprise; IVSC IVS 105; ULI Real Estate Finance

description: Indicates whether this DCF model incorporates debt financing (levered analysis). True = levered model with debt service; False = unlevered all-equity model.
reference: Argus Enterprise Financing Toggle; IVSC IVS 105

description: The Internal Rate of Return computed on levered (equity) cash flows after debt service. Reflects the actual return to equity investors accounting for financial leverage.
reference: Argus Enterprise IRR Output; IVSC IVS 105; CFA Institute Real Estate Valuation

description: Ratio of the debt amount to the acquisition price at the time of underwriting, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.65 for 65% LTV). Key metric for lender and investor risk assessment.
reference: CMBS/RMBS Underwriting Standards; FASB ASC 310; Federal Reserve Regulation Y

description: The governing valuation methodology standard applied in constructing this DCF model. Ensures compliance with applicable appraisal and financial reporting frameworks.
reference: IVSC IVS 105; Appraisal Institute USPAP; RICS Red Book; FASB ASC 820

description: The lowest annual DSCR observed across all projection periods in the hold period. Identifies the most stressed year for debt coverage, critical for lender covenant compliance.
reference: CMBS Underwriting Standards; Argus Enterprise DSCR Output; Federal Reserve Regulation Y

description: Free-text narrative documenting the key assumptions, market conditions, data sources, and qualifications underlying this DCF model. Required for IVSC-compliant methodology disclosure and investment committee review.
reference: IVSC IVS 101 Clause 20.4; Appraisal Institute USPAP SR 2-2; SEC Regulation S-X

description: Human-readable business identifier for this DCF model record, formatted as DCF-YYYY-NNNNNN. Used in investment committee presentations, audit trails, and cross-system references.
reference: Internal Valuation Governance Standard; IVSC IVS 105

description: Business purpose for which this DCF model was constructed. Determines the applicable methodology standards and reporting requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: acquisition_underwriting|disposition_analysis|refinancing|annual_valuation|portfolio_reporting|impairment_testing|loan_origination — promote to reference product]
reference: IVSC IVS 105; FASB ASC 820; SEC Regulation S-X

description: Current lifecycle state of the DCF model record. Controls which version is authoritative for investment decisions and financial reporting.
reference: IVSC IVS 101; FASB ASC 820; Internal Valuation Governance

description: Gross reversion value less estimated selling costs, representing the net cash inflow to the investor upon disposition at the end of the hold period.
reference: Argus Enterprise; IVSC IVS 105; ULI Real Estate Finance

business_glossary_term: Net Present Value (NPV) at Discount Rate
description: Net Present Value of all projected cash flows (including reversion) discounted at the model's target discount rate. Positive NPV indicates value creation above the required return threshold.
reference: IVSC IVS 105; FASB ASC 820; Argus Enterprise NPV Output

description: Assumed annual percentage increase in market rents applied across the projection period. Drives revenue escalation in the DCF cash flow schedule.
reference: Argus Enterprise Rent Growth Input; CoStar Market Analytics; IVSC IVS 105

description: Estimated gross sale proceeds at the end of the hold period, calculated by applying the terminal cap rate to the projected NOI in the reversion year. Represents the largest single cash inflow in the DCF model.
reference: Argus Enterprise Reversion Value; IVSC IVS 105; Appraisal Institute

description: Percentage of reversion value deducted for estimated disposition costs (brokerage commissions, transfer taxes, legal fees) to arrive at net reversion proceeds.
reference: Argus Enterprise Selling Costs Parameter; IVSC IVS 105; NAR Commission Standards

description: Levered IRR under the upside/bull case sensitivity scenario, reflecting optimistic assumptions (lower vacancy, higher rent growth, lower cap rate). Bounds the investment return range.
reference: Argus Enterprise Sensitivity Analysis; IVSC IVS 105; ULI Investment Analysis

description: Levered IRR under the downside/bear case sensitivity scenario, reflecting stressed assumptions (higher vacancy, lower rent growth, higher cap rate). Used in investment committee risk analysis.
reference: Argus Enterprise Sensitivity Analysis; IVSC IVS 105; ULI Investment Analysis

description: Projected NOI at stabilized occupancy, representing the normalized income potential of the property once lease-up or repositioning is complete. Used for terminal cap rate application.
reference: Argus Enterprise; Appraisal Institute; IVSC IVS 105

description: The capitalization rate applied to the final year Net Operating Income (NOI) to derive the reversion/terminal value at the end of the hold period. Critical assumption in DCF reversion calculation.
reference: Argus Enterprise Terminal Cap Rate; Appraisal Institute; IVSC IVS 105

description: The Internal Rate of Return computed on unlevered (all-equity, pre-financing) cash flows. Measures the intrinsic return of the asset independent of capital structure.
reference: Argus Enterprise IRR Output; IVSC IVS 105; CFA Institute Real Estate Valuation

description: Timestamp when this DCF model record was most recently modified. Used to detect stale models and enforce model refresh policies.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5(e); Internal Data Governance

description: Blended vacancy and credit loss rate applied to Potential Gross Income (PGI) to derive Effective Gross Income (EGI) in the model. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.05 for 5%).
reference: Argus Enterprise Vacancy Input; Appraisal Institute; CoStar Market Analytics

description: The effective date as of which the DCF model value conclusion is stated. This is the principal real-world event date for the model, distinct from the model creation or approval date.
reference: IVSC IVS 101 Clause 20.3; Appraisal Institute USPAP; FASB ASC 820-10-35

description: Projected Net Operating Income for the first year of the hold period, representing Effective Gross Income (EGI) less operating expenses. Foundational input for going-in cap rate and debt sizing.
reference: Argus Enterprise NOI Projection; IVSC IVS 105; NCREIF Income Definition

description: The broker's assessment of the directional trend in property values in the subject market area at the time of the BPO effective date.

description: The MLS listing number of the subject property if it is currently or was recently listed on the MLS, used to cross-reference active listing data in CoStar or local MLS systems.

description: The count of active or pending comparable listings used as competitive market evidence in the BPO.

description: The count of closed comparable sales transactions used as evidence in the BPO's sales comparison approach.

description: The occupancy status of the subject property at the time of the BPO inspection, which affects marketability, condition assessment, and value conclusions.

description: The broker's point-estimate opinion of the subject property's market value as of the effective date, expressed in the local currency (USD). This is the primary valuation output of the BPO.

description: The date on which the BPO was formally ordered or requested by the requesting party.

description: The estimated value achievable in an accelerated or distressed sale scenario (e.g., REO liquidation), typically below the as-is market value. Used by lenders for loss mitigation analysis.

description: The broker's recommended listing price for the subject property if placed on the open market, which may differ from the opinion of value based on current market absorption and competitive positioning.

description: The broker's estimate of the total cost of repairs and deferred maintenance items identified during the inspection, used to bridge the as-is value to the as-repaired value.

description: Total number of comparable properties (sold, active, and pending listings) used in the analysis. A higher count generally increases the reliability of the concluded value range.
reference: National Association of Realtors (NAR); Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: The number of months of historical sales data reviewed when selecting comparable sold properties. Typically 3–12 months depending on market activity and data availability.

description: The geographic radius in miles used to define the comparable property search area around the subject property. Smaller radii indicate tighter, more homogeneous market areas.

description: The upper bound of the analyst's concluded market value range for the subject property, expressed in USD. Together with concluded_value_low, defines the recommended pricing corridor.

description: The lower bound of the analyst's concluded market value range for the subject property, expressed in USD. Together with concluded_value_high, defines the recommended pricing corridor for seller consultations.
reference: National Association of Realtors (NAR); International Valuation Standards Council (IVSC)

description: Reference identifier for the CoStar Suite market report or comparable data export used as a data source for this CMA. Supports data lineage and source traceability.

reference: National Association of Realtors (NAR)

description: The date after which the CMA conclusions are considered stale and should not be relied upon for pricing decisions, typically 90–180 days from analysis date given market volatility.

reference: Appraisal Institute; International Valuation Standards Council (IVSC); USPAP

description: The average ratio of final sale price to original list price across comparable sold properties, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.97 = 97%). Indicates negotiation dynamics and pricing accuracy in the local market.
reference: National Association of Realtors (NAR); Multiple Listing Service (MLS)

description: Classification of the current supply-demand balance in the subject property's local market. Sellers markets have low inventory and high demand; buyers markets have high inventory and low demand.
reference: National Association of Realtors (NAR); CoStar Suite

description: Analyst's assessment of the current directional trend in the subject property's local market. Influences pricing strategy recommendations — appreciating markets may support aggressive pricing; declining markets warrant conservative pricing.
reference: National Association of Realtors (NAR); CoStar Suite

description: The MLS-defined geographic area or submarket code within which the subject property and comparables are located. Ensures comparables are drawn from the same MLS market area.

description: Estimated number of months it would take to sell all current active listings at the current absorption rate. Less than 3 months indicates a sellers market; more than 6 months indicates a buyers market.
reference: National Association of Realtors (NAR)

description: Number of pending (under contract) comparable listings included in the analysis. Pending comparables indicate near-term market clearing prices.
reference: National Association of Realtors (NAR); Multiple Listing Service (MLS)

description: The analyst's recommended pricing approach for the listing based on market conditions, seller objectives, and CMA findings. Aggressive pricing targets above-market value; below-market pricing aims for rapid sale.
reference: National Association of Realtors (NAR)

description: The specific listing price recommended by the analyst based on the CMA findings, market trend assessment, and pricing strategy. Falls within or near the concluded value range.
reference: National Association of Realtors (NAR); Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: City in which the comparable property is located. Used for geographic market segmentation and submarket assignment.

description: Full street address of the comparable property including street number, street name, suite or floor where applicable. Used for geographic proximity analysis in market rent conclusions.

description: Common or marketing name of the comparable property (e.g., 'One Embarcadero Center', 'Westfield Mall'). Used for identification and reporting in valuation narratives.
reference: CoStar Suite; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Externally-known identifier or deal reference number assigned to this comparable lease transaction, as sourced from CoStar, MLS, or direct broker confirmation. Serves as the business-facing unique code for the comp record.
reference: CoStar Suite; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Percentage adjustment applied to the comparable lease rent to account for differences in building condition, quality, or finish level between the comparable and subject properties.
reference: Appraisal Institute; International Valuation Standards Council (IVSC) IVS 105

description: CoStar Suite's unique property identifier for the comparable property. Enables direct cross-reference to CoStar market data for automated comp retrieval and market analytics.
reference: CoStar Suite

description: Primary source from which the comparable lease transaction data was obtained (e.g., CoStar Suite, MLS, direct landlord/tenant confirmation, broker, public record). Determines the reliability tier of the comp in IVSC-compliant methodology documentation.
reference: International Valuation Standards Council (IVSC) IVS 105; Appraisal Institute

description: Net effective rent per square foot after accounting for free rent periods, tenant improvement allowances, and other landlord concessions amortized over the lease term. Provides a true economic rent comparison across leases with varying concession packages.
reference: Appraisal Institute; International Valuation Standards Council (IVSC) IVS 105

description: Indicates whether the comparable lease includes a tenant expansion option for additional space. Relevant for assessing tenant demand and flexibility provisions in market analysis.
reference: Appraisal Institute; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Number of months of rent abatement (free rent) granted to the tenant at lease commencement. A landlord concession that reduces effective rent; used to normalize comparable leases to a common economic basis.
reference: Appraisal Institute; International Valuation Standards Council (IVSC) IVS 105

description: Date on which the comparable lease term officially commenced and the tenant took occupancy or began paying rent. Used to determine the effective market date and calculate free rent periods.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16

description: Date on which the comparable lease agreement was fully executed (signed) by both landlord and tenant. Establishes the market date for the transaction, critical for time-adjustment analysis in income approach valuations.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; Appraisal Institute

description: Scheduled expiration date of the comparable lease term. Used in conjunction with commencement date to validate the stated lease term in months.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16

description: Total leased area of the comparable lease expressed in square feet (SQF). Used to assess scale comparability with the subject property and to weight rent conclusions in market analysis.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); Appraisal Institute

description: Total leased area of the comparable lease expressed in square meters (SQM). Used for international market comparisons and IVSC-compliant valuation reports.
reference: International Valuation Standards Council (IVSC); Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Total duration of the comparable lease expressed in months from commencement to expiration. A key comparability factor in market rent analysis; longer terms typically command lower effective rents.
reference: Appraisal Institute; International Valuation Standards Council (IVSC) IVS 105

description: Percentage adjustment applied to the comparable lease rent to account for locational differences between the comparable and subject properties. Part of the grid adjustment methodology in income approach valuations.
reference: Appraisal Institute; International Valuation Standards Council (IVSC) IVS 105

business_glossary_term: Operating Expense Stop Per Square Foot (PSF)
description: Base year operating expense stop per square foot above which the tenant is responsible for expense increases. Common in modified gross leases; used to normalize rent comparisons across different expense structures.
reference: Appraisal Institute; FASB ASC 842

description: Postal or ZIP code of the comparable property location. Supports geographic clustering and submarket boundary analysis.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Timestamp when this comparable lease record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); ISO 55000 Asset Management

description: Timestamp when this comparable lease record was last modified. Supports change tracking, data quality monitoring, and audit trail requirements in the Silver layer.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); ISO 55000 Asset Management

description: Indicates whether the comparable lease includes one or more tenant renewal options. Presence of renewal options affects the economic value of the lease and is noted in market rent analysis.
reference: Appraisal Institute; FASB ASC 842

description: Duration in months of each renewal option period available to the tenant under the comparable lease. Used to assess the total potential lease commitment and WALT calculations.
reference: Appraisal Institute; FASB ASC 842

description: Annual rent escalation rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.03 for 3%) applicable to fixed or percentage-based escalation structures. Used in Argus Enterprise DCF models to project future rent cash flows.
reference: Argus Enterprise; Appraisal Institute

description: Method by which base rent escalates over the lease term (e.g., fixed annual percentage, CPI-linked, stepped increases, or flat). Used to assess the economic profile of the comparable lease in DCF-based valuation models.
reference: Appraisal Institute; Argus Enterprise

description: State or province in which the comparable property is located. Used for regulatory jurisdiction mapping and geographic market analysis.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

business_glossary_term: Tenant Improvement Allowance Per Square Foot (TI PSF)
description: Tenant Improvement (TI) allowance provided by the landlord expressed on a per-square-foot basis. A key concession metric used to normalize comparable leases and assess true economic rent in income approach valuations.
reference: Appraisal Institute; FASB ASC 842; CoStar Suite

description: Percentage adjustment applied to the comparable lease rent to account for market conditions between the lease execution date and the valuation date. Used in the sales comparison and income approaches to normalize historical comps to current market.
reference: Appraisal Institute; International Valuation Standards Council (IVSC) IVS 105

description: Date on which the comparable lease transaction data was verified through a reliable source. Required for IVSC-compliant methodology documentation and appraisal report quality control.
reference: International Valuation Standards Council (IVSC) IVS 105; Appraisal Institute

description: Current verification state of the comparable lease data indicating whether the transaction details have been confirmed through a reliable source such as broker confirmation, public record, or direct tenant/landlord contact. Drives reliability weighting in rent analysis.

description: Indicates whether the comparable sale was an arm's length transaction between unrelated parties with no undue influence (True) or a non-arm's length transaction (False) such as a foreclosure, related-party sale, or distressed sale. reference: Appraisal Institute USPAP Standard 1; IVSC IVS 105; FASB ASC 820

description: The implied capitalization rate (CAP rate) at the time of the comparable sale, calculated as the net operating income (NOI) divided by the sale price. A key market indicator used in income approach cross-checks and investment analysis.
reference: Appraisal Institute USPAP Standard 1; Argus Enterprise; IVSC IVS 105; NCREIF

description: The unique identifier of this comparable sale record within the originating source system (e.g., CoStar property ID, MLS listing number, county assessor parcel number). Enables traceability back to the source system for verification.

description: Indicates whether the comparable sale was a distressed transaction (True), such as a foreclosure, Real Estate Owned (REO) sale, short sale, or bankruptcy sale. Distressed sales may require exclusion or significant adjustment in USPAP-compliant appraisals.

description: Type of financing used in the comparable sale transaction (e.g., conventional mortgage, cash, seller financing, assumed mortgage, CMBS). Atypical financing may require a cash equivalency adjustment per USPAP.

business_glossary_term: Gross Leasable Area in Square Feet (GLA SQF)
description: Total gross leasable area (GLA) of the comparable sale property measured in square feet (SQF). Primary size metric used to calculate price per square foot (PSF) and to assess comparability with the subject property.
reference: Building Owners and Managers Association (BOMA) Measurement Standards; Appraisal Institute USPAP; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Percentage adjustment applied to the comparable sale price to account for locational differences between the comparable property and the subject property (e.g., submarket quality, access, visibility, corner location). Positive values indicate the subject is superior; negative values indicate the comparable is superior.
reference: Appraisal Institute USPAP Standard 1; IVSC IVS 105 Valuation Approaches and Methods

description: Total land area of the comparable sale property parcel measured in square feet. Critical for land valuation, FAR analysis, and comparability assessment for properties where land value is a significant component.
reference: Appraisal Institute USPAP Standard 1; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

business_glossary_term: Net Leasable Area in Square Feet (NLA SQF)
description: Net leasable area (NLA) of the comparable sale property measured in square feet, excluding common areas. Used as an alternative unit of comparison for properties where NLA is the market-standard measurement basis.
reference: Building Owners and Managers Association (BOMA) Measurement Standards; Appraisal Institute USPAP

description: The net operating income (NOI) of the comparable property at the time of sale, used to calculate the implied CAP rate and to support income approach cross-validation in the appraisal.
reference: Appraisal Institute USPAP Standard 1; Argus Enterprise; IVSC IVS 105

description: Total number of above-grade floors or stories in the comparable sale property. Used as a physical characteristic for comparability assessment and adjustment.
reference: Appraisal Institute USPAP Standard 1; CoStar Suite

description: Percentage of the comparable property that was occupied at the time of sale. Used to assess income-producing capacity and to determine whether occupancy-related adjustments are required.
reference: Appraisal Institute USPAP Standard 1; CoStar Suite

description: Total number of parking spaces (surface, structured, or underground) associated with the comparable sale property. Used to assess parking ratio and determine parking-related adjustments.
reference: Appraisal Institute USPAP Standard 1; CoStar Suite

description: Percentage adjustment applied to the comparable sale price to account for physical differences between the comparable and subject property (e.g., size, age, condition, quality, amenities). Positive values indicate the subject is superior.
reference: Appraisal Institute USPAP Standard 1; IVSC IVS 105

description: ZIP or postal code of the comparable sale property location. Used for submarket delineation, geographic clustering, and location adjustment analysis.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); CoStar Suite

description: Sale price divided by the gross leasable area or building area, expressed as price per square foot (PSF). A primary unit-of-comparison metric used in the sales comparison approach for commercial and residential appraisals.
reference: Appraisal Institute USPAP Standard 1; CoStar Suite; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Sale price divided by the building area expressed in square meters (SQM). Used for international valuations and IVSC-compliant reporting where metric units are required.
reference: IVSC International Valuation Standards; ISO 80000-3 Quantities and Units

description: More granular classification of the comparable property within its property type (e.g., Class A Office, Strip Center, Bulk Warehouse, Garden Apartment). Supports refined comparability analysis.

description: Rating of the construction quality and finish level of the comparable sale property. Used to assess quality-based adjustments in the sales comparison approach.
reference: Appraisal Institute USPAP Standard 1; Fannie Mae Uniform Appraisal Dataset (UAD) Quality Ratings

description: The date on which the comparable property sale was recorded or closed, used as the principal business event date for time adjustment calculations in the sales comparison approach.
reference: Appraisal Institute USPAP Standard 1; IVSC IVS 105; FASB ASC 820

description: The gross transaction price at which the comparable property sold, expressed in the local currency. This is the unadjusted arm's-length sale price as recorded in deed or public record.
reference: Appraisal Institute USPAP Standard 1; IVSC IVS 105; FASB ASC 820 Fair Value Measurement

description: Percentage adjustment applied to the comparable sale price to account for market conditions changes between the comparable sale date and the effective appraisal date. Reflects market appreciation or depreciation over the intervening period.
reference: Appraisal Institute USPAP Standard 1; IVSC IVS 105; FASB ASC 820

description: The timestamp when this comparable sale record was most recently modified, supporting audit trail, data lineage, and change tracking requirements.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) Section 404; GDPR Article 5 (data integrity); ISO 55000 Asset Management

description: The date on which the comparable sale data was verified against the primary source. Supports USPAP compliance documentation and audit trail requirements.

description: The calendar year in which the comparable sale property was originally constructed. Used to assess age-related physical depreciation and comparability with the subject property.

description: Total debt financing assumed in the underwriting model for this deal. Represents the debt portion of the capital stack. Used to calculate Loan-to-Value Ratio (LTV) and Debt Service Coverage Ratio (DSCR) in IC analysis.

description: Total equity capital required for the deal as underwritten in the IC memo. Represents the equity portion of the capital stack (total deal cost minus debt). Drives capital call planning and fund commitment tracking.
reference: Argus Enterprise — Investment Analysis; FASB ASC 946; SEC Regulation D

description: Underwritten Loan-to-Value Ratio (LTV) expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.6500 = 65%). Calculated as underwritten debt divided by underwritten value. Key leverage metric reviewed by the IC and lenders.
reference: Argus Enterprise; FASB ASC 310; CMBS underwriting standards

description: Stabilized Net Operating Income (NOI) as projected in the underwriting model. Represents effective gross income minus operating expenses, excluding debt service and capital expenditures. Foundation of CAP rate and DCF valuation.

description: Internal valuation of the asset as determined by the Asset Manager's (AM) underwriting model (typically a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) or direct capitalization approach in Argus Enterprise). Represents the AM's view of intrinsic value, distinct from asking price.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Most recent third-party or internal appraised market value of the asset as of the performance period. Used for CAP rate calculation, LTV computation, and NAV/GAV reporting.
reference: Appraisal Institute; IVSC; USPAP; Argus Enterprise — Valuation

description: Date on which this asset performance record was formally approved and finalized by the authorized asset manager or investment officer.
reference: SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act; SEC REIT Reporting

description: Capitalization rate for the asset during the period, calculated as NOI divided by current market value. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0550 = 5.50%). Primary valuation and benchmarking metric for income-producing real estate.
reference: Appraisal Institute; IVSC; NCREIF; Argus Enterprise — Valuation

description: Capital expenditures incurred on the asset during the period including tenant improvements (TI), leasing commissions, and building capital improvements. Distinct from OPEX; impacts cash-on-cash return and levered IRR.
reference: FASB ASC 840/842; NCREIF; Yardi Voyager — Fixed Assets

description: Annual pre-tax cash flow divided by total equity invested in the asset. Expressed as a decimal. Measures the actual cash income earned on the equity invested, before considering appreciation or leverage effects.
reference: NCREIF; ULI; Argus Enterprise — Investment Analysis

description: Timestamp when this asset performance record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: SOX Financial Controls; GDPR Article 5

description: Operational system of record from which this performance record was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements. Primary sources are Argus Enterprise and Yardi Voyager.
reference: SOX Financial Controls; ISO 55000 Data Governance

description: Risk-adjusted discount rate applied in the DCF model to calculate NPV and present value of projected cash flows. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0750 = 7.50%). Reflects required rate of return for the investment strategy.
reference: CFA Institute; Argus Enterprise — Valuation and Cash Flow Modeling

description: Ratio of NOI to total debt service (principal and interest) for the period. A DSCR above 1.0 indicates the property generates sufficient income to cover debt obligations. Critical metric for lenders and CMBS compliance.
reference: CMBS Underwriting Standards; FASB; Yardi Voyager — Accounts Payable

description: Actual gross income collected during the period after deducting vacancy and credit loss from PGI. EGI = PGI minus vacancy and credit loss. Core revenue metric for property-level performance.
reference: NCREIF; Appraisal Institute; Argus Enterprise — Cash Flow Modeling

description: Projected CAP rate applied to the terminal year NOI to calculate the asset's residual/terminal value at the end of the hold period. Expressed as a decimal. Key assumption in DCF terminal value calculation.
reference: Appraisal Institute; IVSC; Argus Enterprise — Valuation

description: Projected investment hold period in years as modeled in the DCF analysis. Determines the time horizon over which cash flows are projected and the terminal value is calculated.
reference: Argus Enterprise — Investment Analysis; ULI

description: Internal Rate of Return calculated on the asset's equity cash flows after accounting for debt financing. Expressed as a decimal. Reflects the return to equity investors after leverage effects.
reference: NCREIF; CFA Institute; Argus Enterprise — Investment Analysis

description: Ratio of outstanding debt to current appraised or market value of the asset. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.6500 = 65%). Key leverage metric for lenders, CMBS covenants, and portfolio risk management.
reference: CMBS Underwriting Standards; FASB; Federal Reserve Guidance

business_glossary_term: Net Leasable Area in Square Feet (NLA SQF)
description: Net leasable area of the property in square feet as of the performance period. Used as the denominator for PSF metrics (OPEX PSF, revenue PSF) and occupancy calculations.
reference: BOMA Standards; RESO Data Dictionary

description: Net Operating Income for the period calculated as EGI minus operating expenses. Primary measure of a property's income-generating ability before debt service and capital expenditures. SSOT metric for investment performance.
reference: NCREIF; Appraisal Institute; FASB ASC 842; Argus Enterprise

description: Net Present Value of the asset's projected cash flows discounted at the model's discount rate. Positive NPV indicates value creation above the required return threshold. Sourced from Argus Enterprise DCF model.
reference: CFA Institute; Argus Enterprise — Valuation and Cash Flow Modeling

description: Percentage of total leasable area that is occupied and generating income during the performance period. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9150 = 91.50%). Core leasing performance indicator.
reference: RESO Data Dictionary; NCREIF; CoStar Suite — Property Analytics

description: Total property-level operating expenditures for the period including property management fees, insurance, taxes, utilities, repairs, and CAM costs. Excludes debt service and capital expenditures.
reference: NCREIF; FASB ASC 842; Yardi Voyager — General Ledger

business_glossary_term: Operating Expense Per Square Foot (OPEX PSF)
description: Total operating expenses divided by the property's net leasable area in square feet. Expressed as currency per square foot (PSF). Enables cross-property benchmarking of operating efficiency.
reference: BOMA Standards; NCREIF; CoStar Suite — Property Analytics

description: Last day of the reporting period for which this asset performance record is calculated. Together with performance_period_start_date defines the measurement window.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IFRS 16; Argus Enterprise — Cash Flow Modeling

description: First day of the reporting period for which this asset performance record is calculated. Used to define the measurement window for NOI, EGI, occupancy, and other period-level metrics.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IFRS 16; Argus Enterprise — Cash Flow Modeling

description: Current lifecycle state of this asset performance record. Draft indicates in-progress calculation; preliminary is unaudited; final is approved; restated reflects post-close corrections; archived is superseded.
reference: SOX Financial Controls; FASB ASC 842

description: Granularity of the performance measurement period. Determines whether the record represents a monthly, quarterly, annual, trailing 12-month, or year-to-date snapshot.
reference: Argus Enterprise — Portfolio Reporting; ULI Best Practices

description: Total scheduled rental income assuming 100% occupancy at market or contracted rents for the period. Represents the theoretical maximum revenue before vacancy and credit loss deductions. Sourced from Yardi Voyager rent rolls.
reference: NCREIF; Appraisal Institute; Argus Enterprise — Cash Flow Modeling

description: Annual NOI growth rate assumption applied in the DCF model for projecting future cash flows. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0300 = 3.00%). Reflects rent escalation, occupancy improvement, and expense management assumptions.
reference: Argus Enterprise — Cash Flow Modeling; NCREIF

description: Effective gross income divided by the property's net leasable area in square feet. Expressed as currency per square foot (PSF). Benchmarks revenue productivity across the portfolio.
reference: BOMA Standards; NCREIF; CoStar Suite — Property Analytics

description: DCF sensitivity scenario label for this performance record. Base is the primary underwriting case; upside reflects optimistic assumptions; downside reflects conservative assumptions; stress reflects severe adverse conditions.
reference: Argus Enterprise — Investment Analysis; ULI; NCREIF

description: Projected residual value of the asset at the end of the DCF hold period, calculated as terminal year NOI divided by exit CAP rate. Represents the expected sale proceeds at disposition.
reference: Argus Enterprise — Valuation and Cash Flow Modeling; CFA Institute

description: Total investment return for the period combining income return (NOI yield) and capital appreciation return. Expressed as a decimal. NCREIF-standard metric for benchmarking against property indices.
reference: NCREIF Property Index (NPI); CFA Institute; Argus Enterprise

description: Internal Rate of Return calculated on the asset's cash flows before debt financing (unlevered/all-equity basis). Expressed as a decimal. Measures the intrinsic return of the asset independent of capital structure.
reference: NCREIF; CFA Institute; Argus Enterprise — Investment Analysis

description: Timestamp when this asset performance record was last modified. Tracks restatements, corrections, and updates to performance data throughout the record lifecycle.
reference: SOX Financial Controls; GDPR Article 5

description: Deduction from PGI representing income lost due to vacant units and uncollectable rents during the period. Used to derive EGI from PGI.
reference: Appraisal Institute; NCREIF; Argus Enterprise — Cash Flow Modeling

description: Percentage of total leasable area that is unoccupied during the performance period. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0850 = 8.50%). Inverse of occupancy rate; key driver of EGI and NOI.
reference: RESO Data Dictionary; NCREIF; CoStar Suite — Property Analytics

description: Weighted average date on which leases across the asset expire, weighted by rental income or NLA. Expressed as a date. Indicates the point in time when the portfolio faces significant re-leasing risk.
reference: RESO; NCREIF; Argus Enterprise — Lease Management

description: Weighted average remaining lease term across all occupied tenants, weighted by rental income or NLA. Expressed in years. Measures lease duration risk and income security of the asset.
reference: RESO; NCREIF; Argus Enterprise — Lease Management

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Name of the independent external auditor engaged to audit the fund's annual financial statements. Required for SEC-registered funds and institutional LP due diligence.

description: Cumulative capital returned to Limited Partners (LPs) through asset dispositions, refinancing proceeds, and income distributions, expressed in the fund's base currency. Key input for DPI multiple and IRR calculations.

business_glossary_term: Environmental Social and Governance (ESG) Reporting Framework
description: Primary ESG reporting framework adopted by the fund (e.g., GRESB for real estate, UN PRI, TCFD). Null if esg_mandate is false. Drives sustainability data collection and disclosure requirements.
reference: GRESB Real Estate Assessment; UN Principles for Responsible Investment (PRI); TCFD Recommendations; SFDR

description: Indicates whether the fund operates under a formal Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) investment mandate requiring ESG screening, LEED/BREEAM certification targets, or sustainability reporting obligations.
reference: U.S. Green Building Council (USGBC) LEED; BREEAM; SEC ESG Disclosure Rules; SFDR (EU Sustainable Finance Disclosure Regulation)

description: Number of permissible one-year extension options available to the General Partner (GP) beyond the base fund term, as specified in the LPA. Supports fund lifecycle planning and investor communication.
reference: LPA Standard Terms; FASB ASC 946

description: The capital base upon which the annual management fee rate is applied. Committed capital is common during the investment period; invested capital or NAV is common during the harvest period.
reference: FASB ASC 946; LPA Standard Terms; ILPA Fee Transparency Template

description: Maximum absolute dollar amount of management fees that may be charged to the fund in any given year, expressed in the fund's base currency. Null if no cap is specified in the LPA.
reference: LPA Standard Terms; ILPA Fee Transparency Template

description: Narrative description of conditions under which management fees may be waived or reduced (e.g., GP co-investment, fund underperformance, LP negotiated side letter terms). Sourced from LPA and side letter agreements in DocuSign CLM.
reference: LPA Standard Terms; SEC Form ADV Part 2A; ILPA Fee Transparency Template

description: Date on which the fund completed its capital raising period and accepted its last investor commitment. Marks the end of the fundraising phase and the start of the investment period. Null for open-end funds.
reference: SEC Investment Company Act of 1940; FASB ASC 946

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the fund across systems (e.g., MRI Software, Argus Enterprise, SAP S/4HANA). Used as the business key in cross-system reconciliation and regulatory filings.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); SEC EDGAR Fund Identifier

description: Full legal name of the investment fund or vehicle as registered with the relevant regulatory authority (e.g., SEC, FINRA). Used in investor communications, regulatory filings, and reporting.
reference: SEC Form ADV; FASB ASC 946 Financial Services — Investment Companies

description: Current lifecycle state of the fund. Drives operational workflows, investor reporting obligations, and capital call eligibility. Fundraising indicates the fund is accepting commitments; fully_invested indicates deployment is complete; harvesting indicates asset disposition phase.
reference: FASB ASC 946; Urban Land Institute (ULI) Fund Lifecycle Framework

description: Classification of the investment vehicle structure. Determines regulatory treatment, investor redemption rights, and accounting framework. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open_end|closed_end|reit|partnership|cmbs_trust|rmbs_trust|separate_account|co_investment — promote to reference product]
reference: FASB ASC 946; SEC Investment Company Act of 1940; IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements

description: Gross Asset Value (GAV) of the fund representing the total fair market value of all real estate assets before deducting fund-level debt and liabilities, expressed in the fund's base currency. Used in leverage ratio and LTV calculations.
reference: IVSC Valuation Standards; NCREIF PREA Reporting Standards; FASB ASC 946

description: Legal name of the General Partner entity responsible for managing the fund, making investment decisions, and fulfilling fiduciary obligations to Limited Partners (LPs). Sourced from MRI Software Investment Management module.
reference: SEC Form ADV; Investment Advisers Act of 1940; FASB ASC 946

description: Date on which the fund was legally formed and commenced operations. Used as the start date for IRR, equity multiple, and since-inception performance calculations in Argus Enterprise.
reference: FASB ASC 946; SEC Form N-2; IFRS 16

description: Date on which the fund's investment period expires and the GP is no longer permitted to make new investments or call capital for new acquisitions. After this date, capital calls are limited to follow-on investments and fund expenses.
reference: LPA Standard Terms; FASB ASC 946; ILPA Reporting Standards

description: Risk-return strategy classification of the fund per industry convention. Core strategies target stabilized assets with lower risk; opportunistic strategies target higher-risk, higher-return repositioning or development. Drives benchmark selection and performance attribution in Argus Enterprise and MRI Software.
reference: National Council of Real Estate Investment Fiduciaries (NCREIF); Urban Land Institute (ULI) Strategy Classification

description: Annual management fee charged by the GP to the fund, expressed as a decimal of the fee basis (e.g., 0.0150 = 1.50% of committed capital). Sourced from LPA terms and tracked in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software.
reference: FASB ASC 946; LPA Standard Terms; SEC Form ADV Part 2A

description: Maximum individual asset or transaction size (in base currency) that the fund is mandated to consider for investment. Prevents over-concentration in a single asset and ensures portfolio diversification per LPA guidelines.
reference: LPA Investment Guidelines; ULI Investment Mandate Standards

description: Minimum individual asset or transaction size (in base currency) that the fund is mandated to consider for investment. Ensures portfolio concentration and operational efficiency thresholds are maintained.
reference: LPA Investment Guidelines; ULI Investment Mandate Standards

description: Net Asset Value (NAV) of the fund as of the most recent valuation date, calculated as total assets minus total liabilities, expressed in the fund's base currency. Primary metric for open-end fund unit pricing and investor redemption calculations.
reference: FASB ASC 946; IVSC Valuation Standards; NCREIF PREA Reporting Standards

description: Minimum annualized return rate (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.0800 = 8.00%) that must be distributed to Limited Partners (LPs) before the General Partner (GP) is entitled to receive carried interest. Also known as the hurdle rate or preferred return.
reference: FASB ASC 946; LPA Standard Terms; ILPA Reporting Standards

description: Frequency at which the fund produces and distributes financial and performance reports to Limited Partners (LPs). Drives reporting calendar in MRI Software and Argus Enterprise.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; SEC Form N-CEN; FASB ASC 946

description: Target total Assets Under Management (AUM) the fund intends to reach at full deployment, expressed in the fund's base currency. Used in fundraising materials, investor presentations, and portfolio planning in Argus Enterprise.
reference: SEC Form ADV Part 1A; NCREIF PREA Reporting Standards; FASB ASC 946

description: Target equity multiple (TVPI — Total Value to Paid-In) the fund aims to return to investors, expressed as a ratio (e.g., 1.80 = 1.8x). Represents total distributions plus residual NAV divided by total called capital.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; NCREIF PREA; CFA Institute GIPS Standards

description: Target net Internal Rate of Return (IRR) expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1200 = 12.00%) that the fund aims to deliver to investors over its life. Used in fund marketing materials, LP reporting, and performance benchmarking in Argus Enterprise.
reference: CFA Institute GIPS Standards; NCREIF PREA; Appraisal Institute

description: Maximum Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio permitted under the fund's investment mandate, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.65 = 65%). Defines the upper bound of the fund's leverage range. Exceeding this threshold may trigger LP notification requirements.
reference: LPA Investment Guidelines; NCREIF PREA; Basel III

description: Minimum Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio permitted under the fund's investment mandate, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.40 = 40%). Defines the lower bound of the fund's leverage range for portfolio construction and risk management.
reference: LPA Investment Guidelines; NCREIF PREA; Basel III (applicable to fund-level credit facilities)

description: Contractual life of the fund in years from inception to scheduled termination, as defined in the Limited Partnership Agreement (LPA) or fund prospectus. Null for open-end funds with no fixed term.
reference: FASB ASC 946; SEC Form N-2; LPA Standard Terms

description: Scheduled or actual date on which the fund is dissolved and all assets are liquidated and distributed to investors. Null for open-end or perpetual-life funds. Drives WALT and WALE calculations for portfolio wind-down planning.
reference: FASB ASC 946; SEC Form N-14; ULI Fund Lifecycle Framework

description: Aggregate capital committed by all Limited Partners (LPs) to the fund as of the final close, expressed in the fund's base currency. Represents the maximum capital available for investment and fee calculation basis.
reference: FASB ASC 946; SEC Form D; LPA Standard Terms

description: Date of the most recent formal appraisal or valuation of the fund's portfolio used to calculate NAV and GAV. Typically quarterly for institutional funds. Sourced from Argus Enterprise valuation models.
reference: IVSC Valuation Standards; Appraisal Institute USPAP; FASB ASC 820 Fair Value Measurement

description: Adjusted Funds From Operations (AFFO) for the measurement period — FFO adjusted for recurring capital expenditures (CAPEX), straight-line rent adjustments, and other non-cash items. Considered a more accurate proxy for distributable cash flow than FFO.
reference: NAREIT FFO White Paper; SEC Form 10-K; FASB ASC 946

description: The fund's excess return over the benchmark return for the measurement period (TWR minus benchmark_return). Positive alpha indicates outperformance; negative alpha indicates underperformance relative to the benchmark.
reference: GIPS — CFA Institute; ILPA Reporting Standards

description: Name of the external audit firm that reviewed or signed off on this performance period (e.g., 'Deloitte', 'PwC', 'KPMG', 'Ernst & Young'). Required for SEC filings and investor transparency.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; SEC Regulation S-X; PCAOB Standards

description: The external auditor's sign-off status for this performance period. 'not_required' for interim periods; 'pending' for annual periods awaiting audit; 'signed_off' for unqualified audit opinion; 'qualified' or 'adverse' for modified opinions. Required for SOX and SEC compliance.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; SEC Regulation S-X; PCAOB Standards; FASB ASC 946

description: Total Assets Under Management (AUM) for the fund as of the period end date. May differ from GAV when leverage is included or when AUM is defined per the fund's limited partnership agreement. Used for management fee calculations and regulatory reporting.
reference: SEC Form ADV; ILPA Reporting Standards; FASB ASC 946

description: The name of the benchmark index used for performance comparison (e.g., 'NCREIF ODCE', 'MSCI US REIT Index', 'FTSE NAREIT All Equity REITs'). Provides context for interpreting benchmark_return and alpha.
reference: GIPS — CFA Institute; NCREIF; MSCI Real Estate Index

description: The return of the designated benchmark index (e.g., NCREIF ODCE, MSCI Real Estate, S&P 500) for the same measurement period. Used to compute alpha and assess relative fund performance.
reference: GIPS — CFA Institute; NCREIF; MSCI Real Estate Index

description: The weighted average Capitalization Rate (CAP Rate) applied across the fund's portfolio for the period — NOI divided by GAV. A key real estate valuation metric used in Argus Enterprise models and investor reporting.
reference: Appraisal Institute; IVSC — IVS 105; NAREIT; Argus Enterprise

description: Total Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) incurred by the fund's portfolio during the measurement period, including Tenant Improvement (TI) allowances, building improvements, and major repairs that extend asset life.
reference: NAREIT; FASB ASC 840/842; Yardi Voyager — Fixed Assets

description: The timestamp when this fund performance record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: SOX Section 302; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: The discount rate or hurdle rate applied in the fund's DCF (Discounted Cash Flow) valuation models for the period, expressed as a decimal. Used to compute NPV and to assess whether the fund is meeting its return threshold.
reference: Argus Enterprise — Valuation and Cash Flow Modeling; IVSC — IVS 105; FASB ASC 820

description: Cash distribution declared or paid per fund unit or share during the measurement period. Used for investor yield calculations, REIT dividend reporting, and AFFO payout ratio analysis.
reference: NAREIT; SEC Form 10-K; FASB ASC 946; ILPA Reporting Standards

description: Distributions to Paid-In Capital (DPI) ratio — total cumulative distributions paid to investors divided by total paid-in capital. Measures the realized return component. A DPI of 1.0 means investors have received back their full invested capital in cash.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; GIPS — CFA Institute

business_glossary_term: Equity Multiple (MOIC — Multiple on Invested Capital)
description: The ratio of total value (realized distributions plus unrealized NAV) to total invested capital, also known as Multiple on Invested Capital (MOIC). A value of 1.75 means investors have received or hold 1.75x their invested capital.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; GIPS — CFA Institute

description: Funds From Operations (FFO) for the measurement period — net income plus real estate depreciation and amortization, adjusted for gains/losses on property sales. The NAREIT-defined primary earnings metric for REITs, used in lieu of GAAP net income.
reference: NAREIT FFO White Paper; SEC Form 10-K; FASB ASC 946

description: Gross Asset Value (GAV) of the fund as of the period end date — the sum of appraised market values of all properties and investments held by the fund, before deducting liabilities. Sourced from Argus Enterprise appraisal models.
reference: IVSC — IVS 105; Appraisal Institute; FASB ASC 820; ILPA Reporting Standards

business_glossary_term: Gross Internal Rate of Return (Gross IRR)
description: The annualized gross Internal Rate of Return (IRR) for the fund over the measurement period, before management fees and carried interest. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1250 = 12.50%). Core metric for ILPA and SEC reporting.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; GIPS — CFA Institute; SEC Form N-2

description: The fund's leverage ratio as of the period end date, typically expressed as total debt divided by GAV (Loan-to-Value / LTV). Monitors compliance with fund-level debt covenants and risk parameters.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; SEC Form N-2; FASB ASC 946; Lender Covenant Agreements

description: Money-Weighted Return (MWR) for the measurement period, reflecting the actual investor experience including the timing and magnitude of capital calls and distributions. Equivalent to the period IRR from the investor's perspective.
reference: GIPS — CFA Institute; ILPA Reporting Standards

description: Net Asset Value (NAV) of the fund as of the period end date — GAV minus total liabilities. The primary valuation basis for investor redemptions, unit pricing, and REIT reporting. Sourced from MRI Software and Argus Enterprise.
reference: FASB ASC 946; IVSC; SEC Form N-2; ILPA Reporting Standards

description: The methodology used to determine the NAV for this period. 'independent_appraisal' uses third-party Appraisal Institute-certified appraisers; 'internal_valuation' uses Argus Enterprise DCF models; 'broker_opinion' uses Broker Price Opinion (BPO); 'automated_valuation' uses AVM models; 'hybrid' combines methods.
reference: IVSC — IVS 105; Appraisal Institute USPAP; FASB ASC 820; ILPA Reporting Standards

business_glossary_term: Net Asset Value Per Unit/Share (NAV per Unit)
description: NAV divided by total units or shares outstanding as of the period end date. The per-unit pricing basis for investor subscriptions, redemptions, and REIT share pricing.
reference: FASB ASC 946; SEC Form N-2; ILPA Reporting Standards

business_glossary_term: Net Internal Rate of Return (Net IRR)
description: The annualized net Internal Rate of Return (IRR) for the fund over the measurement period, after deducting management fees, carried interest, and fund expenses. Primary investor-facing performance metric.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; GIPS — CFA Institute; SEC Form N-2

description: Net Operating Income (NOI) of the fund's underlying real estate portfolio for the measurement period — total revenue minus operating expenses, before debt service, depreciation, and income taxes. Core metric for real estate valuation and CAP rate analysis.
reference: NAREIT; Appraisal Institute; FASB ASC 946; Argus Enterprise

description: Net Present Value (NPV) of the fund's projected cash flows discounted at the fund's hurdle rate or discount rate as of the period end date. Sourced from Argus Enterprise DCF models.
reference: Argus Enterprise — Valuation and Cash Flow Modeling; IVSC — IVS 105; FASB ASC 820

description: Total Operating Expenditure (OPEX) of the fund's underlying portfolio for the measurement period, including property management fees, insurance, utilities, repairs, and Common Area Maintenance (CAM) costs.
reference: NAREIT; FASB ASC 946; Yardi Voyager — General Ledger

description: The last calendar date of the measurement period for which fund performance metrics are reported (e.g., 2024-03-31 for Q1 2024).
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; SEC Regulation S-X; FASB ASC 946

description: The first calendar date of the measurement period for which fund performance metrics are reported (e.g., 2024-01-01 for Q1 2024).
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; SEC Regulation S-X; FASB ASC 946

description: Lifecycle state of the performance record. 'open' indicates the period is still accumulating data; 'closed' indicates the period is finalized; 'audited' indicates external auditor sign-off has been received; 'restated' indicates a prior-period correction has been applied.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; SEC Regulation S-X; SOX Section 302/404

description: Classification of the measurement period horizon. Drives how IRR, TWR, and benchmark comparisons are interpreted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: monthly|quarterly|annual|trailing_12|since_inception — promote to reference product if additional period types are required]
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; GIPS (Global Investment Performance Standards) — CFA Institute

description: The percentage premium or discount at which the fund's market price or transaction price trades relative to its NAV per unit. Positive values indicate a premium; negative values indicate a discount. Critical for publicly traded REITs and closed-end funds.
reference: SEC Form N-2; FASB ASC 946; NAREIT

description: Residual Value to Paid-In Capital (RVPI) ratio — the current NAV of unrealized investments divided by total paid-in capital. Measures the unrealized return component. RVPI + DPI = TVPI.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; GIPS — CFA Institute

description: The originating system record identifier from Argus Enterprise or MRI Software from which this performance record was sourced (e.g., Argus portfolio report ID or MRI period close reference). Supports data lineage and audit traceability.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; SOX Section 302

description: Total cash distributions paid to all fund investors during the measurement period. Feeds into DPI calculation and is a key component of ILPA cash flow reporting.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; NAREIT; SEC Form 10-K

description: Total outstanding liabilities of the fund as of the period end date, including mortgage debt, credit facilities, accrued fees, and other obligations. NAV = GAV minus total_liabilities.
reference: FASB ASC 946; IFRS 16; SEC Form N-2

description: Total revenue generated by the fund's underlying properties and investments during the measurement period, including Gross Rental Income (GRI), interest income, and other operating income. Sourced from MRI Software property accounting.
reference: FASB ASC 946; NAREIT FFO White Paper; MRI Software — Property Accounting

description: Total Value to Paid-In Capital (TVPI) ratio — the sum of DPI and RVPI. Represents the total value created per dollar of invested capital, combining both realized and unrealized returns.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; GIPS — CFA Institute

description: Time-Weighted Return (TWR) for the measurement period, eliminating the distorting effects of external cash flows (capital calls and distributions). The GIPS-compliant return metric for comparing fund manager performance against benchmarks.
reference: GIPS — CFA Institute; ILPA Reporting Standards

description: Total number of fund units or shares outstanding as of the period end date. Used to compute NAV per unit and to track dilution from new subscriptions or reduction from redemptions.

description: The timestamp when this fund performance record was most recently modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, restatement detection, and audit trail requirements.

description: The specific date as of which the fund's GAV and NAV were determined. May differ from period_end_date if appraisals are conducted on a lag (e.g., appraisal as of December 31 reported in February).

description: Timestamp when this portfolio asset record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Section 404 Internal Controls; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: Current carrying value of the asset on the portfolio's books after accumulated depreciation and any impairment charges. Used for balance sheet reporting, NAV calculations, and GAAP/IFRS financial statements.
reference: FASB ASC 360; IFRS 16; MRI Software — Property Accounting; Yardi Voyager — Fixed Assets

description: Gross sale price received upon disposition of the asset from the portfolio. Populated when asset_status = 'disposed'. Used for realized gain/loss calculation, IRR computation, and fund performance reporting.
reference: Argus Enterprise — Investment Analysis; FASB ASC 360; NCREIF

description: Total equity capital deployed into this asset by the portfolio, net of debt financing. Used as the denominator in equity multiple and ROI calculations, and for tracking capital deployment against fund commitments.
reference: Argus Enterprise — Investment Analysis; MRI Software — Investment Management; NCREIF

business_glossary_term: Gross Leasable Area (GLA) in Square Feet (SQF)
description: Total Gross Leasable Area (GLA) of the property asset measured in square feet (SQF). Used for PSF rent analysis, portfolio size reporting, and benchmarking against market comparables. Sourced from the property domain master record.
reference: BOMA International Measurement Standards; RESO Data Dictionary; CoStar Suite

description: Investment hold strategy assigned to this asset within the portfolio. Determines capital allocation priorities, return expectations, and exit planning. 'core_hold' = long-term stabilized income; 'value_add' = active repositioning for value creation; 'opportunistic' = higher-risk/return transformation; 'disposition_target' = approved for near-term sale; 'development' = ground-up or major redevelopment.
reference: Argus Enterprise — Investment Analysis; Urban Land Institute (ULI); MRI Software — Investment Management

description: Current annualized Net Operating Income (NOI) generated by the asset based on in-place leases and operating expenses. Used for CAP rate derivation, DSCR monitoring, and portfolio income reporting. Sourced from Yardi Voyager NOI statements.

description: Risk-return classification of this asset holding per institutional investment framework. Distinct from hold_strategy — this reflects the underwriting risk tier used for investor disclosure, fund mandate compliance, and performance benchmarking.
reference: NCREIF; Urban Land Institute (ULI); SEC — REIT Disclosure Requirements

description: Indicates whether the asset is subject to debt financing, liens, or other encumbrances (True) or is unencumbered (False). Used for portfolio leverage analysis, lender reporting, and unencumbered asset pool tracking for REIT compliance.
reference: SEC — REIT Disclosure; FASB ASC 470; MRI Software — Investment Management

description: Indicates whether this asset is held through a joint venture or co-investment structure (True) or is wholly owned by the portfolio (False). Drives proportional consolidation logic and joint venture reporting requirements.

description: Current physical occupancy rate of the property asset expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9200 = 92.00%). Key operational KPI for income stability assessment, lender covenant compliance, and portfolio performance dashboards.

description: Portfolio's percentage ownership interest in the property asset, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.7500 = 75.00%). Critical for proportional consolidation of NOI, GAV, debt, and equity in fund-level reporting. Supports joint venture and co-investment structures.

description: Projected stabilized Net Operating Income (NOI) upon completion of the asset's business plan (lease-up, repositioning, or value-add program). Used in exit valuation modeling, IRR projections, and investment committee reporting.

description: Underwritten target Capitalization Rate (CAP Rate) at acquisition, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0550 = 5.50%). Represents the expected NOI yield on the asset value used in the investment thesis and exit pricing assumptions.

description: Underwritten target equity multiple (total distributions + residual value divided by equity invested) for this asset at acquisition. Expressed as a ratio (e.g., 1.8500 = 1.85x). Used alongside target_irr for investment committee approval and performance tracking.

description: Planned or target date for disposition or exit of this asset from the portfolio. Used in hold period analysis, IRR projections, fund lifecycle planning, and WALT/WALE reporting. Nullable for open-ended core holds.

description: Underwritten target Internal Rate of Return (IRR) for this asset at the time of acquisition, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1200 = 12.00%). Represents the return threshold used in investment committee approval and ongoing performance benchmarking.

description: Investor's capital account balance at the start of the statement period, equal to the ending balance of the immediately preceding period. Serves as the opening balance for the period's capital account roll-forward reconciliation.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; FASB ASC 946; MRI Software Investment Management Module

description: Total capital contributed by the investor during the statement period in response to capital calls. Includes funded amounts from all capital calls settled within the period. Increases the capital account balance.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; FASB ASC 946; MRI Software Investment Management Module

description: Total distributions returned to the investor during the statement period, including return of capital, realized gains, and income distributions. Decreases the capital account balance. Reported as a positive number representing cash out to the investor.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; FASB ASC 946; MRI Software Investment Management Module

description: Performance-based carried interest (carry) allocated to the general partner and charged against this investor's capital account during the period, per the fund's waterfall provisions. Typically triggered after the preferred return hurdle is met.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; FASB ASC 946; SEC Regulation D; MRI Software Investment Management Module

description: Timestamp when this capital account statement record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404; FASB ASC 946; ILPA Reporting Standards

description: Total capital contributed by the investor from fund inception through the end of the statement period. Used for IRR calculation, return-of-capital tracking, and ILPA transparency reporting.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; FASB ASC 946; MRI Software Investment Management Module

description: Total distributions returned to the investor from fund inception through the end of the statement period. Used for DPI (Distributions to Paid-In) multiple calculation and ILPA transparency reporting.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; FASB ASC 946; MRI Software Investment Management Module

description: Distributions to Paid-In (DPI) multiple for this investor from inception through the statement period end date. Calculated as cumulative_distributions / cumulative_contributions. Measures the realized return multiple relative to invested capital.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; CFA Institute GIPS; Argus Enterprise

description: Investor's capital account balance at the close of the statement period. Calculated as: beginning_capital_balance + capital_contributions - capital_distributions + allocated_income_loss - management_fees_charged - carried_interest_allocated - other_fees_charged. This is the SSOT for the investor's fund position at period end.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; FASB ASC 946; MRI Software Investment Management Module

description: Fiscal quarter number (1–4) within the fiscal year for this statement period. Supports quarterly LP reporting packages and quarterly performance attribution analysis.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; FASB ASC 946

description: Four-digit fiscal year in which this statement period falls (e.g., 2024). Used for annual rollup reporting, tax K-1 preparation, and year-over-year performance comparison.
reference: FASB ASC 946; IRS Schedule K-1; ILPA Reporting Standards

description: Cumulative Internal Rate of Return (IRR) for this investor's capital account from fund inception through the statement period end date, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.142500 = 14.25%). Calculated using the XIRR methodology on all cash flows. Primary performance metric for LP reporting.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; CFA Institute GIPS; Argus Enterprise; MRI Software Investment Management Module

description: Indicates whether the investor is a tax-exempt entity (e.g., pension fund, endowment, foundation, ERISA plan) for purposes of UBTI (Unrelated Business Taxable Income) tracking and tax reporting. True = tax-exempt; False = taxable investor.

description: Asset management fees charged to this investor's capital account during the period, calculated per the fund's management fee schedule (typically expressed as a percentage of committed or invested capital). Reduces the capital account balance.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; SEC Investment Advisers Act; MRI Software Investment Management Module

description: Multiple on Invested Capital (MOIC) for this investor from inception through the statement period end date. Calculated as (ending_capital_balance + cumulative_distributions) / cumulative_contributions. Measures total value creation relative to invested capital.

description: Net Asset Value per fund unit or interest as of the statement period end date, used to value the investor's position. Derived from the fund's total NAV divided by total units outstanding. Supports investor-level NAV attribution.
reference: ILPA Reporting Standards; FASB ASC 946; IVSC Valuation Standards; Argus Enterprise

description: Free-text field for asset manager or fund administrator annotations regarding this capital account statement, such as restatement explanations, special allocation notes, or investor-specific adjustments.

description: Investor's percentage ownership interest in the fund as of the statement period end date, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.125000 = 12.5%). Used for income/loss allocation, voting rights, and NAV attribution calculations.

description: Frequency classification of the reporting period for this capital account statement. Determines the cadence of LP reporting obligations and aligns with fund governing document requirements.

description: Cumulative preferred return accrued but not yet distributed to the investor from fund inception through the statement period end date. Represents the outstanding preferred return obligation owed to the LP before carried interest can be earned.

description: Annual preferred return (hurdle rate) percentage applicable to this investor's capital account per the fund's limited partnership agreement (e.g., 0.0800 = 8.00%). Determines the threshold above which carried interest is charged to the investor.

description: Investor's pro-rata share of realized gains or losses from asset dispositions completed during the statement period. Represents the difference between net sale proceeds and cost basis allocated to this investor.

description: Residual Value to Paid-In (RVPI) multiple for this investor as of the statement period end date. Calculated as ending_capital_balance / cumulative_contributions. Measures the unrealized value remaining relative to invested capital.

description: Identifier or reference code from the originating system of record (e.g., MRI Software or Argus Enterprise) for this capital account statement record. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit traceability back to the operational system.

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: operating_income|asset_sale|inheritance|pension_fund|endowment_corpus|debt_financing|equity_raise|insurance_reserves|sovereign_allocation — promote to reference product]

description: Investor's target equity multiple (total distributions divided by total invested capital) for real estate investments. A multiple of 2.0x means the investor expects to receive twice their invested capital over the investment hold period. Used in Argus Enterprise for investment screening and portfolio performance benchmarking.
reference: IVSC Valuation Standards; Argus Enterprise; NCREIF

business_glossary_term: Target Internal Rate of Return (IRR) Maximum
description: Maximum acceptable Internal Rate of Return (IRR) threshold expressed as a decimal percentage. Defines the upper bound of the investor's risk-return appetite. Investors with low IRR maximums typically prefer core or core-plus strategies. Used alongside target_irr_min to define the investor's return corridor in Argus Enterprise.
reference: IVSC Valuation Standards; Argus Enterprise; CFA Institute Standards

business_glossary_term: Target Internal Rate of Return (IRR) Minimum
description: Minimum acceptable Internal Rate of Return (IRR) threshold expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 0.0800 = 8.00%). Represents the investor's stated return hurdle below which investment opportunities are not considered. Used in Argus Enterprise for deal screening and portfolio matching against investor mandates.
reference: IVSC Valuation Standards; Argus Enterprise; CFA Institute Standards

description: Classification of the tax identification number provided. EIN (Employer Identification Number) for US entities, SSN (Social Security Number) for US individuals, ITIN (Individual Taxpayer Identification Number) for non-resident individuals, Foreign TIN for non-US entities, GIIN (Global Intermediary Identification Number) for FATCA-registered entities.
reference: IRS Publication 515; FATCA (IRC Section 1471-1474)

description: Government-issued tax identification number for the investor. For US entities, this is the EIN (Employer Identification Number) or SSN (Social Security Number). For non-US entities, the equivalent national tax registration number. Used for IRS Form K-1 issuance, FATCA reporting, and withholding tax calculations.
reference: IRS Publication 515; FATCA (IRC Section 1471-1474); GDPR Article 9

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the investor's country of tax residency, which may differ from domicile country. Used for CRS (Common Reporting Standard) automatic exchange of information and applicable withholding tax treaty determination.

description: The rate used to discount future lease payments to present value — either the rate implicit in the lease or, if not determinable, the lessee's incremental borrowing rate (IBR). Stored as a decimal (e.g., 0.055000 = 5.5%). Required for weighted-average rate disclosure.
