description: Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned by Yardi Voyager or SAP S/4HANA to uniquely identify this lease accounting entry for cross-system reconciliation and audit trail.
reference: FASB ASC 842; SOX Section 404

description: Current workflow state of the lease accounting entry. 'draft' = pending review; 'posted' = recorded in GL; 'reversed' = correcting entry applied; 'adjusted' = amended after initial posting; 'under_review' = flagged for auditor review.
reference: FASB ASC 842; SOX Section 302

description: Discriminator for the nature of the accounting entry: 'initial_recognition' = commencement date measurement; 'subsequent_measurement' = periodic amortisation/interest; 'remeasurement' = triggered by lease modification or reassessment; 'termination' = derecognition; 'modification' = lease term or payment change; 'disclosure_summary' = aggregated period-end disclosure record.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-35; IFRS 16 paragraphs 39–46

description: The fiscal quarter (1–4) within the fiscal year to which this entry belongs, supporting quarterly 10-Q disclosure requirements for publicly traded REITs.
reference: SEC Regulation S-X; FASB ASC 842

description: The fiscal year (four-digit integer) in which this accounting entry falls, used for annual disclosure reporting and REIT financial statement preparation.
reference: FASB ASC 842; SEC Regulation S-X

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 code for the reporting entity's functional currency. Used for foreign currency translation of lease liabilities and ROU assets under ASC 830 / IAS 21 when the lease currency differs from the functional currency.
reference: ISO 4217; FASB ASC 830; IAS 21

description: Exchange rate applied to translate lease amounts from the lease currency to the functional currency as of the measurement date. Equals 1.0 when both currencies are the same.
reference: FASB ASC 830; IAS 21

description: Interest accrued on the lease liability during the period using the effective interest method at the incremental borrowing rate or implicit rate. Recognised in the income statement for finance leases; included in operating lease cost for operating leases under ASC 842.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-35-5; IFRS 16 paragraph 26

description: ASC 842 / IFRS 16 classification of the lease: 'operating' = lessee recognises straight-line rent expense; 'finance' = lessee recognises interest + amortisation; 'short_term' = practical expedient, term ≤12 months; 'low_value' = IFRS 16 practical expedient for low-value assets.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-25; IFRS 16 paragraphs 22–49

description: Cash or non-cash lease incentives received from the lessor (e.g., Tenant Improvement allowances, free rent periods) that reduce the initial measurement of the ROU asset under ASC 842 / IFRS 16.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-30-5; IFRS 16 paragraph 24

description: Present value of remaining lease payments at the end of the accounting period, as reported on the balance sheet. Equals opening balance plus interest accrual minus principal repayment plus/minus remeasurement.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-50; IFRS 16 paragraph 53

description: Present value of remaining lease payments at the beginning of the accounting period, before current-period interest accrual and principal repayment.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-50; IFRS 16 paragraph 53

description: Adjustment to the lease liability resulting from a lease modification, change in lease term, change in variable payments indexed to a rate, or reassessment of a purchase/extension option. Positive = increase; negative = decrease.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-35-4; IFRS 16 paragraph 45

business_glossary_term: Lease Maturity Analysis — After Year 5 Undiscounted Payments
description: Undiscounted future lease payments due more than 5 years from the balance sheet date, completing the maturity analysis disclosure schedule under ASC 842-20-50-6 and IFRS 16 paragraph 58.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-50-6; IFRS 16 paragraph 58

business_glossary_term: Lease Maturity Analysis — Year 1 Undiscounted Payments
description: Undiscounted future lease payments due within 12 months of the balance sheet date, as required for the lease liability maturity analysis disclosure under ASC 842-20-50-6 and IFRS 16 paragraph 58.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-50-6; IFRS 16 paragraph 58

business_glossary_term: Lease Maturity Analysis — Years 2–5 Undiscounted Payments
description: Undiscounted future lease payments due between 1 and 5 years from the balance sheet date, forming part of the maturity analysis disclosure schedule under ASC 842-20-50-6 and IFRS 16 paragraph 58.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-50-6; IFRS 16 paragraph 58

description: The specific date on which the ROU asset and lease liability were measured or remeasured, which may differ from the accounting period date in the case of mid-period modifications or reassessments.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-35-4; IFRS 16 paragraph 45

description: Indicates whether this accounting entry was triggered by a lease modification event (e.g., extension, scope change, rent renegotiation) requiring remeasurement of the ROU asset and lease liability.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-35-3; IFRS 16 paragraph 44

description: The date and time when this accounting entry was posted to the General Ledger, representing the principal business event timestamp for the transaction lifecycle.
reference: FASB ASC 842; SOX Section 302

description: Indicates which ASC 842 / IFRS 16 practical expedient, if any, has been elected for this lease: 'short_term' = ≤12-month exemption; 'low_value' = IFRS 16 low-value asset exemption; 'portfolio' = portfolio-level application; 'hindsight' = hindsight expedient on transition; 'none' = no expedient applied.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-15-3; IFRS 16 paragraphs 5, 15

description: Cash payment applied to reduce the lease liability principal during the period, representing the financing portion of the lease payment.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-35-5; IFRS 16 paragraph 26

description: Increases to the ROU asset during the period from new lease commencements, lease modifications that increase scope, or remeasurements that increase the lease liability.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-35; IFRS 16 paragraph 39

description: Current-period amortisation charge applied to the ROU asset. For finance leases, amortised on a straight-line basis over the shorter of the lease term or useful life; for operating leases, the plug to achieve straight-line rent expense.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-35-6; IFRS 16 paragraph 31

description: Net carrying value of the ROU asset at the end of the accounting period after amortisation and any impairment, as reported on the balance sheet.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-50; IFRS 16 paragraph 53

description: Impairment loss recognised against the ROU asset during the period under ASC 360 / IAS 36, reducing the carrying value below the amortised cost.
reference: FASB ASC 360; IAS 36; IFRS 16 paragraph 33

description: Carrying value of the Right-of-Use (ROU) asset at the beginning of the accounting period before current-period amortisation, as required for ASC 842 / IFRS 16 roll-forward disclosure.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-50; IFRS 16 paragraph 53

description: Lease expense recognised for leases with a term of 12 months or less where the short-term practical expedient has been elected, excluded from ROU asset and lease liability recognition. Required disclosure under ASC 842-20-50-4.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-50-4; IFRS 16 paragraph 53(c)

description: Total operating lease cost recognised on a straight-line basis over the lease term for operating leases under ASC 842, combining the amortisation of the ROU asset and the unwinding of the lease liability discount.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-25-6; IFRS 16 paragraph 49

description: Income received or receivable from subleasing the right-of-use asset to a third party during the period. Required as a separate disclosure line under ASC 842-20-50-4 and IFRS 16 paragraph 53(b).
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-50-4; IFRS 16 paragraph 53(b)

description: System timestamp of the most recent update to this lease accounting entry record, supporting change tracking and incremental load processing in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5

description: Lease payments not included in the lease liability measurement that vary based on an index, rate, or usage (e.g., CPI-linked rent escalations above the initial index, percentage rent, CAM true-ups). Required disclosure under ASC 842-20-50 and IFRS 16 paragraph 53.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-50-4; IFRS 16 paragraph 53(a)

description: Weighted average incremental borrowing rate or implicit rate applied across the lease portfolio as of period-end, required for ASC 842-20-50-6 and IFRS 16 paragraph 58 disclosure. Stored as a decimal (e.g., 0.048500 = 4.85%).
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-50-6; IFRS 16 paragraph 58

business_glossary_term: Weighted Average Remaining Lease Term (WALT) in Years
description: Weighted Average Lease Term (WALT) remaining as of the period-end date, expressed in years. Populated on disclosure_summary entry types for aggregated portfolio-level ASC 842 / IFRS 16 footnote disclosure.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-50-6; IFRS 16 paragraph 58

business_glossary_term: Actual Gross Common Area Maintenance (CAM) Operating Expense
description: Total actual gross operating expenses incurred at the property level for the reconciliation period, before application of the gross-up factor or tenant pro-rata share. Sourced from the property general ledger via Yardi Voyager.
reference: FASB ASC 842; Yardi Voyager General Ledger; RESO

business_glossary_term: Actual Grossed-Up Common Area Maintenance (CAM) Expense
description: Actual CAM operating expenses after application of the gross-up factor. Represents the normalized total expense pool used as the basis for tenant pro-rata share allocation.
reference: FASB ASC 842; Yardi Voyager CAM Module; RESO

business_glossary_term: Actual Common Area Maintenance (CAM) Per Square Foot (PSF)
description: Actual CAM charge allocated to the tenant expressed on a per-square-foot basis (PSF). Calculated as tenant_cam_allocated divided by tenant_nla_sqft. Key metric for portfolio benchmarking and market comparisons.
reference: RESO; CoStar Suite; Yardi Voyager CAM Module

business_glossary_term: Common Area Maintenance (CAM) Administration Fee Amount
description: Calculated dollar amount of the landlord's administrative fee included in the tenant's CAM allocation for the reconciliation period. Derived from admin_fee_rate applied to the eligible CAM expense pool.
reference: Yardi Voyager CAM Module; FASB ASC 842

business_glossary_term: Common Area Maintenance (CAM) Administration Fee Rate
description: Percentage rate charged by the landlord as an administrative or management fee on top of actual CAM expenses, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.15 = 15%). Permitted under many NNN leases. Null if no admin fee applies.
reference: Yardi Voyager CAM Module; RESO; FASB ASC 842

business_glossary_term: Tenant CAM Audit Right Exercised Indicator
description: Indicates whether the tenant has exercised their contractual right to audit the landlord's CAM expense records for the reconciliation period. True if an audit has been formally requested or conducted.
reference: Yardi Voyager CAM Module; FASB ASC 842; SOX

business_glossary_term: Building Gross Leasable Area (GLA) in Square Feet (SQF)
description: Total Gross Leasable Area of the building in square feet used as the denominator for pro-rata share calculation. May differ from total building area if certain areas are excluded per lease terms.
reference: RESO; BOMA International; Yardi Voyager Property Management

business_glossary_term: Common Area Maintenance (CAM) Cap Applied Indicator
description: Indicates whether the CAM increase cap was triggered and applied during this reconciliation period. True if the calculated CAM increase exceeded the cap and was limited accordingly.
reference: Yardi Voyager CAM Module; FASB ASC 842

business_glossary_term: Common Area Maintenance (CAM) Annual Increase Cap Rate
description: Maximum annual percentage increase in CAM charges permitted under the lease, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.05 = 5% cap). Limits the tenant's CAM exposure year-over-year. Null if no cap applies.
reference: RESO; Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; FASB ASC 842

business_glossary_term: Common Area Maintenance (CAM) Cap Savings Amount
description: Monetary amount by which the tenant's CAM charge was reduced due to the application of the CAM cap. Represents the difference between the uncapped allocated amount and the capped amount billed. Zero if cap was not applied.
reference: Yardi Voyager CAM Module; FASB ASC 842

description: Specifies whether the CAM increase cap is cumulative (unused cap carries forward to future years) or non-cumulative (cap resets each year). Determines how the cap is applied in multi-year reconciliations.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; RESO; FASB ASC 842

description: Total dollar value of operating expense line items excluded from the CAM pool per lease terms (e.g., capital expenditures, management fees above a threshold, leasing commissions). Reduces the gross CAM expense pool before tenant allocation.
reference: Yardi Voyager CAM Module; FASB ASC 842; RESO

description: Timestamp when this CAM reconciliation record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements under SOX and FASB ASC 842.
reference: SOX; FASB ASC 842; GDPR Article 5

business_glossary_term: CAM Credit or Additional Charge Amount
description: Net monetary amount due to or from the tenant as a result of the CAM reconciliation. A positive value represents an additional charge invoiced to the tenant; a negative value represents a credit applied to the tenant's account. Drives the AR billing or credit memo in Yardi Voyager.
reference: FASB ASC 842; Yardi Voyager Accounts Receivable; SOX

description: Indicates whether the reconciliation outcome results in an additional charge to the tenant, a credit to the tenant, or a zero-balance settlement. Drives downstream AR and billing workflow routing in Yardi Voyager.
reference: Yardi Voyager Accounts Receivable; FASB ASC 842

description: Indicates whether the tenant has formally disputed the CAM reconciliation statement. True if a dispute has been raised; False if no dispute exists. Triggers dispute management workflow.
reference: Yardi Voyager CAM Module; FASB ASC 842

description: Free-text description of the tenant's stated reason for disputing the CAM reconciliation, as recorded by the property manager. Populated only when dispute_flag is True.
reference: Yardi Voyager CAM Module; FASB ASC 842

description: Date on which the CAM reconciliation dispute was formally resolved between landlord and tenant. Null if no dispute exists or dispute is still open.
reference: Yardi Voyager CAM Module; FASB ASC 842

business_glossary_term: Estimated Annual Common Area Maintenance (CAM) Charge
description: Total estimated CAM charges billed to the tenant over the reconciliation year, based on the budgeted CAM rate established at lease commencement or annual budget cycle. Represents the sum of monthly estimated CAM payments collected from the tenant.
reference: FASB ASC 842; Yardi Voyager CAM Module; RESO

business_glossary_term: Estimated Common Area Maintenance (CAM) Per Square Foot (PSF)
description: Estimated annual CAM charge expressed on a per-square-foot basis (PSF). Calculated as estimated_cam_annual divided by tenant_nla_sqft. Used for benchmarking and lease comparison analytics.
reference: RESO; CoStar Suite; Yardi Voyager CAM Module

description: Multiplier applied to actual CAM expenses to normalize costs to a fully occupied building (typically to 90–100% occupancy). Ensures tenants pay their fair share of variable operating costs regardless of actual vacancy. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 1.05 for a 5% gross-up).
reference: RESO; NAR; Yardi Voyager CAM Module; FASB ASC 842

description: Free-text field for property manager or asset manager annotations regarding the CAM reconciliation, including explanations of unusual variances, exclusion justifications, or tenant communication summaries.
reference: Yardi Voyager CAM Module

description: Date by which the additional CAM charge (if any) must be paid by the tenant, or by which the landlord must issue a credit. Drives AR billing and cash flow forecasting.
reference: Yardi Voyager Accounts Receivable; FASB ASC 842

description: Date on which the tenant's additional CAM payment was received and applied in Yardi Voyager. Null if no additional charge was due or payment has not yet been received.
reference: Yardi Voyager Accounts Receivable; FASB ASC 842

business_glossary_term: Prior Year Estimated Common Area Maintenance (CAM) Charge
description: Total estimated CAM charges billed to the tenant in the immediately preceding reconciliation year. Used for year-over-year variance analysis and CAM cap calculations.
reference: Yardi Voyager CAM Module; FASB ASC 842

description: Externally visible, human-readable identifier for this CAM reconciliation record as assigned by Yardi Voyager (e.g., CAMREC-2024-00123). Used in tenant correspondence, audit trails, and billing statements.
reference: Yardi Voyager CAM Module; FASB ASC 842

description: Last day of the CAM reconciliation period. Defines the boundary of actual operating expenses included in the reconciliation calculation.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IFRS 16; Yardi Voyager CAM Module

description: First day of the CAM reconciliation period. May differ from the calendar year start for leases with non-standard fiscal periods or mid-year commencement dates.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IFRS 16; Yardi Voyager CAM Module

description: Date on which the CAM reconciliation statement was formally delivered to the tenant. Triggers the tenant's review and dispute window as defined in the lease.
reference: Yardi Voyager CAM Module; FASB ASC 842; DocuSign CLM

description: Current workflow state of the CAM reconciliation record. Tracks progression from initial draft through tenant review, finalization, dispute resolution, and settlement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_review|finalized|disputed|settled|voided — promote to reference product if additional states are required]
reference: Yardi Voyager CAM Module; FASB ASC 842; SOX

description: Difference between the tenant's allocated actual CAM charge and the estimated CAM billed during the reconciliation period (tenant_cam_allocated minus estimated_cam_annual). A positive value indicates an underpayment (additional charge due from tenant); a negative value indicates an overpayment (credit due to tenant).
reference: FASB ASC 842; Yardi Voyager CAM Module; SOX; RESO

description: The calendar or fiscal year (four-digit integer, e.g., 2024) for which actual CAM operating expenses are being reconciled against estimated billings. Defines the reconciliation period.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IFRS 16; Yardi Voyager CAM Module

business_glossary_term: Tenant Allocated Common Area Maintenance (CAM) Charge
description: Actual CAM expense allocated to this specific tenant, calculated as the grossed-up actual CAM multiplied by the tenant's pro-rata share. This is the amount the tenant is contractually obligated to pay for the reconciliation period.
reference: FASB ASC 842; Yardi Voyager CAM Module; RESO

business_glossary_term: Tenant Net Leasable Area (NLA) in Square Feet (SQF)

description: The minimum annual CPI escalation rate guaranteed under the lease terms, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0200 = 2.00% floor). Protects the landlord from zero or negative CPI adjustments. Null for non-CPI escalation types.

description: The mechanism by which rent increases from one step to the next. 'fixed_step' = predetermined dollar or percentage increase; 'cpi' = Consumer Price Index-linked; 'percentage_of_sales' = percentage rent tied to tenant gross sales; 'market_review' = reset to fair market value; 'none' = flat rent with no escalation.

description: Indicates whether this clause has a material impact on IFRS 16 lease accounting classification, measurement, or disclosure. True = material IFRS 16 impact. Supports dual-standard reporting for international portfolios.
reference: IASB IFRS 16

description: Indicates whether the rights or obligations under this clause apply mutually to both landlord and tenant (True) or are unilateral in favor of one party (False). Relevant for indemnification, audit rights, and assignment clauses.
reference: NAR; RESO; DocuSign CLM

description: Indicates whether this clause was specifically negotiated and customized from the landlord's standard lease form (True) or is a standard boilerplate provision (False). Supports risk profiling and lease negotiation benchmarking.
reference: NAR; RESO

description: Indicates whether the clause has been triggered or activated based on a qualifying event (e.g., co-tenancy failure, kick-out threshold breach, force majeure event). True = currently triggered; False = not triggered.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; RESO

description: Section, article, or exhibit number within the lease document where this clause is located (e.g., 'Article 14', 'Section 22.3', 'Exhibit D'). Enables direct cross-reference to the source legal document.
reference: DocuSign CLM; Yardi Voyager Lease Administration

description: Dollar amount threshold that governs the activation or measurement of this clause (e.g., minimum sales volume for co-tenancy, kick-out sales threshold PSF, TI allowance cap for assignment). Expressed in the lease currency.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; RESO

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the monetary threshold value (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency portfolio management.
reference: ISO 4217; FASB ASC 842; IFRS 16

description: Free-text field for additional commentary, legal counsel observations, or operational notes regarding this clause that are not captured in structured fields. Used by property managers (PM) and asset managers (AM) for context.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration

description: Calculated or contractually specified deadline by which notice must be delivered to exercise or respond to this clause. Supports proactive portfolio-wide deadline monitoring.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; RESO

description: Number of days' advance written notice required to exercise, invoke, or respond to this clause. Critical for kick-out, ROFO, ROFR, and termination option clauses.
reference: RESO; NAR; DocuSign CLM

description: Numeric ordering of this clause relative to other clauses of the same type on the same lease, used when multiple clauses of the same type exist (e.g., multiple ROFR provisions with different priority rankings). Lower number = higher priority.
reference: RESO; Yardi Voyager Lease Administration

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code or section reference identifying this clause within the lease document (e.g., Section 12.4, Exhibit B-3). Sourced from DocuSign CLM or Yardi Voyager lease abstract.
reference: DocuSign CLM; Yardi Voyager Lease Administration

description: Percentage reduction in base rent applicable when this clause is triggered (e.g., co-tenancy failure entitles tenant to 50% rent abatement). Expressed as a percentage of base rent.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IFRS 16; Yardi Voyager Lease Administration

description: Portfolio risk classification assigned to this clause based on its potential financial or operational impact if triggered. Used by asset managers (AM) for portfolio-wide clause risk screening and prioritization.

description: Fixed dollar amount payable as a termination fee or penalty if this clause is exercised (e.g., kick-out fee, early termination payment). Expressed in the lease currency.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IFRS 16; Yardi Voyager Lease Administration

description: Abstracted plain-language summary of the clause's key provisions, obligations, and rights. Captured during lease abstraction from DocuSign CLM or Yardi Voyager. Not the full legal text but sufficient for portfolio screening and risk management.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; DocuSign CLM; NAR

description: Human-readable short title or heading of the clause as it appears in the lease document (e.g., 'Tenant Exclusivity Provision', 'Co-Tenancy Requirement'). Used for portfolio-wide clause screening and reporting.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; DocuSign CLM

description: Date on which the clause was triggered or activated by a qualifying event. Null if the clause has not been triggered. Used for risk tracking and legal action timelines.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; DocuSign CLM

description: Plain-language description of the event or condition that triggered or could trigger this clause (e.g., 'Anchor tenant vacancy exceeding 90 days', 'Sales threshold below $X PSF for two consecutive quarters').
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; NAR

description: Timestamp when this clause record was last modified in the silver layer data product. Supports incremental data processing, change detection, and audit trail requirements.

description: Date on which the abstracted clause data was reviewed and verified against the original executed lease document by a qualified reviewer. Null if not yet verified.
reference: SOX; Yardi Voyager Lease Administration

description: Native clause identifier assigned by Yardi Voyager lease abstract module. Supports reconciliation and lineage tracing between the silver layer data product and the Yardi Voyager system of record.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration

description: Indicates whether Common Area Maintenance (CAM) charges are applicable to this demised space. Drives CAM reconciliation processing in Yardi Voyager.

description: Timestamp when this demised space record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Audit trail for data governance and lineage.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; GDPR Article 5 (data integrity); ISO 55000

description: Incremental borrowing rate (IBR) or implicit rate used to discount future lease payments for ROU asset and lease liability measurement under ASC 842 / IFRS 16 for this demised space.

description: Date from which this demised space record is effective, supporting amendment and modification tracking. For the original lease, equals the lease commencement date; for amendments, reflects the amendment effective date.

description: Indicates whether the lease agreement grants the tenant an expansion option for additional space adjacent to or within the same building as this demised space.

business_glossary_term: Gross Leasable Area (GLA) in Square Feet
description: Total Gross Leasable Area (GLA) of the demised space measured in square feet (SQF) as defined in the lease agreement. GLA is the standard measurement basis for rent calculation in commercial leases.
reference: BOMA International Measurement Standards; RESO Data Dictionary; NAR

business_glossary_term: Gross Leasable Area (GLA) in Square Meters
description: Total Gross Leasable Area (GLA) of the demised space measured in square meters (SQM). Used for international reporting, IFRS 16 disclosures, and cross-border portfolio analytics.
reference: IVSC Valuation Standards; IFRS 16; ISO 55000

description: Indicates whether this demised space is the primary (anchor) space under the lease agreement. Relevant for multi-space leases where one space is designated as the primary premises.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration

description: Date on which the tenant's lease term for this specific demised space commences. May differ from the master lease commencement date for multi-space leases with staggered delivery.
reference: ASC 842-20-30; IFRS 16.13; Yardi Voyager Lease Administration

description: Date on which the tenant's lease term for this specific demised space expires. Used in WALT/WALE calculations and lease expiry schedule reporting.
reference: ASC 842-20-30; IFRS 16; Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; FASB

description: Initial lease liability recognized for this demised space at lease commencement under ASC 842 / IFRS 16. Represents the present value of future lease payments discounted at the incremental borrowing rate.
reference: FASB ASC 842-20-30; IFRS 16.26; SAP S/4HANA Lease Accounting

description: Ratio of Gross Leasable Area (GLA) to Usable Area, representing the tenant's proportionate share of common areas added to their usable space. Calculated as GLA / Usable Area. Typical range 1.00–1.25 for office buildings.
reference: BOMA International Office Building Standard (ANSI/BOMA Z65.1); NAR

business_glossary_term: Net Leasable Area (NLA) in Square Feet
description: Net Leasable Area (NLA) of the demised space in square feet, excluding common areas, structural elements, and mechanical spaces. Used for NLA-based rent calculations and PSF analysis.
reference: BOMA International Measurement Standards; RESO Data Dictionary

description: Date on which the landlord delivered physical possession of the demised space to the tenant. Triggers the lease commencement measurement period under ASC 842 / IFRS 16.
reference: ASC 842-20-30; IFRS 16.13; Yardi Voyager Lease Administration

description: Tenant's proportionate share of the building's total rentable area, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0523 = 5.23%). Used to allocate Common Area Maintenance (CAM) charges, operating expenses, and real estate taxes under NNN and modified gross leases.
reference: Yardi Voyager CAM Reconciliation; FASB ASC 842; BOMA Standards

description: Date on which the tenant's obligation to pay rent for this demised space begins. May be later than the lease commencement date due to free rent or fit-out periods.
reference: ASC 842; IFRS 16; Yardi Voyager Lease Administration

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all rent and financial amounts associated with this demised space (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, AUD).
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS 16; FASB ASC 842

description: Annual rent escalation rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.03 = 3%) applicable to this demised space. Used when rent_escalation_type is fixed_percent or CPI-based.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; Argus Enterprise Cash Flow Modeling

description: Method by which base rent escalates over the lease term for this demised space. Drives straight-line rent calculation and future cash flow modeling in Argus Enterprise.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; Argus Enterprise; ASC 842; IFRS 16

description: Indicates whether the tenant holds a Right of First Refusal (ROFR) on adjacent or specified spaces within the property under this lease.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; NAR

description: Initial Right-of-Use (ROU) asset value recognized for this demised space at lease commencement under ASC 842 / IFRS 16. Represents the present value of future lease payments plus initial direct costs and prepaid rent.

description: Current lifecycle status of the demised space within the lease. Indicates whether the space is actively occupied, vacant, under renovation, or has been surrendered.

description: The interest rate used to discount future lease payments to present value for ASC 842 / IFRS 16 measurement. Typically the implicit rate in the lease or the lessee's incremental borrowing rate (IBR), expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.045000 = 4.5%).

description: Indicates whether the lease contains an exclusivity clause restricting the landlord from leasing space to competing tenants within the same property. Common in retail leases.

description: The date on which all parties signed and the lease agreement became legally binding. Sourced from DocuSign CLM execution records.
reference: DocuSign CLM; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: The contractual date on which the lease term ends. Used for WALT/WALE calculations, renewal option tracking, and ASC 842 lease term determination. Null for month-to-month leases.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: The number of months of rent abatement (free rent) granted to the tenant at lease commencement or during the lease term. Recognized as a lease incentive under ASC 842 and amortized over the lease term.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; Yardi Voyager Lease Administration

description: The multiplier applied to the base rent if the tenant remains in occupancy beyond the lease expiration date without executing a renewal (holdover tenancy). Typically 1.25 to 1.50 (125%–150% of base rent).
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; National Association of Realtors (NAR)

description: The minimum general liability insurance coverage amount the tenant is contractually required to maintain and name the landlord as an additional insured, as specified in the lease abstract.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; OSHA; National Association of Realtors (NAR)

description: The timestamp when the lease agreement record was most recently updated in the source system. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and change data capture in the Databricks lakehouse.
reference: SOX; GDPR Article 5; Yardi Voyager

description: ASC 842 / IFRS 16 accounting classification of the lease as either an operating lease or a finance lease (capital lease). Determines balance sheet treatment, amortization method, and income statement presentation for the lessee.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; SEC

description: The present value of future lease payments recognized as a liability on the balance sheet at lease commencement under ASC 842 / IFRS 16. Decreases as lease payments are made over the lease term.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; SEC

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the lease agreement, as recorded in Yardi Voyager or MRI. Used for cross-system reference and tenant-facing communications.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; MRI Lease Management

description: Current lifecycle state of the lease agreement. Drives ASC 842 / IFRS 16 recognition, billing activation, and portfolio reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|executed|active|expired|terminated|holdover — promote to reference product if additional states are required]
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; Yardi Voyager Lease Administration

description: The total contractual lease term expressed in months from commencement to expiration. Used for WALT/WALE calculations, Argus Enterprise cash flow modeling, and portfolio duration analysis.
reference: FASB ASC 842; Argus Enterprise; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: The net leasable area (NLA) in square feet covered by this lease agreement. Used for PSF rent calculations, CAM pro-rata share computation, and GLA occupancy reporting.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); BOMA International

description: The date on which the Letter of Intent (LOI) was signed by the prospective tenant, marking the formal start of lease negotiation. Sourced from Salesforce CRM deal pipeline records.
reference: Salesforce CRM; National Association of Realtors (NAR)

description: The contractually defined purpose for which the tenant is permitted to use the leased premises (e.g., 'retail clothing store', 'general office use', 'restaurant and bar'). Restricts tenant operations and is a key lease abstract field.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; DocuSign CLM

description: Indicates whether the lease agreement belongs to the commercial or residential portfolio, driving applicable regulatory frameworks (ASC 842 vs. residential tenancy law) and reporting hierarchies.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; HUD

description: The tenant's proportionate share of the building's total rentable area, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.125000 = 12.5%). Used to allocate CAM, tax, and insurance charges in NNN and modified gross leases.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; BOMA International

description: The number of renewal options available to the tenant under the lease agreement. Each option typically specifies a term length and rent determination method.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; FASB ASC 842

description: Indicates whether the tenant has formally exercised a renewal option under this lease agreement. True when the renewal notice has been received and acknowledged by the landlord.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; DocuSign CLM

description: The duration in months of each renewal option period available to the tenant. Used in ASC 842 lease term assessment to determine if renewal options are reasonably certain to be exercised.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; Yardi Voyager Lease Administration

description: The date on which the tenant's obligation to pay base rent begins. May differ from the lease commencement date due to free-rent periods or tenant improvement construction periods.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; FASB ASC 842

description: The percentage or fixed amount by which base rent escalates per the rent_escalation_type. Expressed as a decimal for percentage types (e.g., 0.0300 = 3%). Null when escalation type is none or CPI-linked.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; FASB ASC 842

description: Method by which base rent increases over the lease term. Fixed percent applies a set annual percentage increase. CPI ties escalation to the Consumer Price Index. Fixed amount adds a set dollar increment. None indicates flat rent for the full term.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; FASB ASC 842

description: The initial measurement of the right-of-use (ROU) asset recognized on the balance sheet at lease commencement under ASC 842 / IFRS 16. Equals the present value of future lease payments plus initial direct costs and prepaid rent.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; SEC

description: The total security deposit held by the landlord as collateral against tenant default or damage. Tracked for AR reconciliation and return obligations upon lease expiration.
reference: Yardi Voyager Accounts Receivable; FASB ASC 842

business_glossary_term: Subordination Non-Disturbance and Attornment (SNDA) Agreement Executed Flag
description: Indicates whether a Subordination, Non-Disturbance, and Attornment (SNDA) agreement has been executed between the tenant, landlord, and lender. Required by most commercial mortgage lenders to protect tenant occupancy rights in foreclosure.
reference: ALTA; DocuSign CLM; CMBS standards

description: Indicates whether the tenant is contractually permitted to sublet all or part of the leased premises to a subtenant, subject to landlord consent conditions specified in the lease.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; DocuSign CLM

description: The timestamp when this ownership transfer record was most recently modified in the data platform. Used for incremental data processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5 (data integrity); ISO 55000 Asset Management

description: The principal amount of the mortgage or acquisition loan obtained by the transferee to finance this ownership interest transfer. Used for Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio computation and debt covenant monitoring.
reference: FASB ASC 310; CMBS regulatory framework; SOX Section 302

description: The ratio of the acquisition loan amount to the allocated property value at the time of transfer, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 65.0000 = 65% LTV). Key underwriting metric for lenders and portfolio risk management.
reference: FASB ASC 310; CMBS/RMBS regulatory frameworks; Basel III

description: Free-text field for additional context, special conditions, or qualifications related to the ownership transfer that are not captured in structured fields. Examples include right-of-first-refusal waivers, co-owner consent notes, or regulatory condition summaries.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: The transferor's remaining ownership percentage in the property immediately after this transfer event. Computed as pre_transfer_ownership_pct minus transfer_percentage. Stored for audit trail and consolidation analysis.
reference: FASB ASC 810 Consolidation; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: The transferor's ownership percentage in the property immediately before this transfer event. Provides the baseline for computing post-transfer ownership stakes and validating that transfer_percentage does not exceed the transferor's held interest.
reference: FASB ASC 810 Consolidation; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: The official book and page number (or document number in electronic recording systems) assigned by the recording jurisdiction upon filing of the title instrument. Used for public records retrieval and chain-of-title verification.
reference: American Land Title Association (ALTA)

description: The date on which the title instrument was officially recorded with the relevant governmental authority (county recorder, land registry). May differ from the transfer_date; recording establishes constructive notice to third parties.
reference: American Land Title Association (ALTA); Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

business_glossary_term: Subordination Non-Disturbance and Attornment (SNDA) Agreement Reference
description: Reference identifier for the Subordination, Non-Disturbance and Attornment (SNDA) agreement executed in connection with this transfer, protecting existing tenant leases from disruption due to the ownership change. Sourced from DocuSign CLM.
reference: DocuSign CLM; American Land Title Association (ALTA); FASB ASC 842

description: The operational system of record from which this ownership transfer record originated. Enables data lineage tracking and reconciliation across Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, Argus Enterprise, and other integrated platforms.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); SOX Section 404

description: The native record identifier from the originating operational system (e.g., Yardi Voyager ownership transfer ID, MRI Software transaction key). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver layer and the system of record.
reference: SOX Section 404; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: The transferor's adjusted tax basis in the ownership interest at the time of transfer. Used to compute realized gain or loss for income tax purposes. For 1031 exchanges, the carryover basis is tracked here.
reference: Internal Revenue Code Section 1031; FASB ASC 740; SOX Section 302

description: The policy number of the title insurance policy issued to the transferee (owner's policy) or lender (lender's policy) in connection with this transfer. Provides protection against title defects and encumbrances not discovered during due diligence.
reference: American Land Title Association (ALTA); Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Total transaction costs incurred in executing the ownership transfer, including legal fees, title search fees, recording fees, broker commissions, and due diligence costs. Capitalized as part of the cost basis per FASB ASC 805.
reference: FASB ASC 805; SOX Section 302; SEC Regulation S-X

description: The principal real-world effective date on which the ownership interest legally transferred from transferor to transferee. This is the date of record for title and tax purposes, distinct from the agreement execution date.
reference: American Land Title Association (ALTA); FASB ASC 805; Internal Revenue Code Section 1031

description: The percentage of the total ownership interest being transferred, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 25.0000 = 25%). Enables partial interest transfers within joint-venture and partnership structures. Must be between 0.0001 and 100.0000.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); FASB ASC 810 Consolidation

description: Externally-known business identifier for the ownership transfer event, used in correspondence, legal documents, and system cross-references. Sourced from Yardi Voyager or MRI Software ownership module.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); American Land Title Association (ALTA)

description: Current lifecycle state of the ownership transfer workflow. Tracks progression from initiation through legal completion. Values: pending (initiated, awaiting documentation), in_review (under legal/compliance review), approved (authorized for execution), completed (title recorded and transfer finalized), cancelled (transfer voided before completion), reversed (completed transfer subsequently unwound).
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); FASB ASC 805

description: The total real estate transfer tax, documentary stamp tax, or conveyance tax assessed on the ownership transfer by the applicable jurisdiction. Computed based on consideration amount and local tax rates. Required for tax compliance reporting.
reference: FASB ASC 805; SOX Section 302; SEC Regulation S-X

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each owner tax record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the specific property asset for which this tax obligation is recorded. A single owner may have multiple tax records across different properties.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Date on which a formal tax assessment appeal was filed with the taxing authority or appeals board. Null if no appeal has been filed. Used to track appeal deadlines and resolution timelines.
reference: Local Tax Authority Appeal Procedures; Appraisal Institute

description: Current status of any formal appeal or contest filed against the taxing authority's assessed value or tax bill. Tracks the appeal lifecycle from filing through resolution. A successful appeal may result in a tax abatement.
reference: Local Tax Authority Appeal Procedures; Appraisal Institute; IVSC

description: Gross tax amount assessed or levied by the taxing authority for this tax year and jurisdiction, expressed in the record currency. For property tax, this is the annual tax bill amount. For withholding tax, this is the amount withheld. For K-1 allocations, this is the allocated taxable income amount.
reference: FASB ASC 740; IRS Publication 527; SOX Section 404; SEC Regulation S-K

description: Actual date on which the tax return or filing was submitted to the taxing authority. Compared against filing_deadline_date to determine compliance. Supports SOX audit trail and SEC reporting for publicly traded REITs.
reference: IRS Filing Requirements; SOX Section 302; SEC Regulation S-K

description: Statutory or regulatory deadline for submitting the tax return or filing to the relevant authority. Distinct from the payment due date. Supports compliance monitoring and SOX control tracking.
reference: IRS Filing Deadlines; SOX Section 302; SEC Reporting Calendar

description: Current lifecycle status of the tax filing associated with this record. Tracks progression from not filed through submission, amendment, and acceptance or rejection by the taxing authority. Supports SOX compliance monitoring and SEC reporting for publicly traded REITs.
reference: SOX Section 302; SEC Regulation S-K; IRS Filing Requirements

business_glossary_term: Foreign Investment in Real Property Tax Act (FIRPTA) Withholding Rate
description: Applicable FIRPTA withholding tax rate for foreign owners on disposition of US real property interests. Standard rate is 15% (0.1500) for amounts realized over $1M; reduced rates may apply per IRS withholding certificates. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 = 15%).
reference: IRS FIRPTA (IRC §1445); IRS Revenue Procedure 2000-35; IRS Form 8288

description: Interest charges accrued on unpaid or underpaid tax balances as assessed by the taxing authority. Tracked separately from penalties and base tax for accurate OPEX and NOI reporting.
reference: IRS Interest Rules (IRC §6601); FASB ASC 740; SOX Section 404

description: Indicates whether the owner is a foreign person or entity subject to FIRPTA (Foreign Investment in Real Property Tax Act) withholding requirements. Drives withholding tax rate application and IRS Form 8288 filing obligations.
reference: IRS FIRPTA (IRC §1445); IRS Form 8288; IRS Form 8288-A

description: County, municipality, or special tax district within the state where the property tax is levied. Relevant for local property tax assessments and special assessment districts.
reference: Local Tax Authority; RESO Data Dictionary; NAR Property Tax Standards

business_glossary_term: Schedule K-1 Capital Gain Allocation Amount
description: Partner's or investor's share of net capital gain allocated via IRS Schedule K-1 (Form 1065). Includes short-term and long-term capital gains from property dispositions within the partnership. Null for non-partnership structures.
reference: IRS Schedule K-1 (Form 1065); IRS Form 1065; IRC §1231

description: Partner's or investor's share of depreciation deductions allocated via IRS Schedule K-1. Relevant for REIT FFO (Funds From Operations) and AFFO (Adjusted Funds From Operations) calculations and investor tax planning.
reference: IRS Schedule K-1 (Form 1065); IRS Form 4562; FASB ASC 740; REIT FFO/AFFO Standards

business_glossary_term: Schedule K-1 Ordinary Income Allocation Amount
description: Partner's or investor's share of ordinary business income allocated via IRS Schedule K-1 (Form 1065) for partnership entities. Applicable for JV entities, limited partnerships, and REIT operating partnerships. Null for non-partnership ownership structures.
reference: IRS Schedule K-1 (Form 1065); IRS Form 1065; FASB ASC 740

description: Free-text field for capturing supplementary information, special circumstances, or administrative notes related to this tax record — such as appeal rationale, installment payment arrangements, treaty election details, or correspondence references.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Owner's percentage equity stake in the property or partnership entity as of the tax year, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2500 = 25%). Drives K-1 income allocation calculations and proportional tax obligation attribution.
reference: IRS Form 1065; SEC Regulation S-K; FASB ASC 810 Consolidation

description: Monetary penalty assessed by the taxing authority for late filing, late payment, or underpayment of taxes. Tracked separately from the base tax obligation for OPEX reporting and NOI impact analysis.
reference: IRS Penalty and Interest Rules; FASB ASC 740; SOX Section 404

description: Timestamp when this owner tax record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail, SOX IT general controls, and data lineage tracking.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this owner tax record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports change tracking, SOX audit controls, and incremental data pipeline processing.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Amount of tax reduction granted through formal abatement, exemption, or appeal decision by the taxing authority. Reduces the net tax obligation. Common in development projects with tax incentive agreements or successful assessment appeals.
reference: Local Tax Authority Abatement Programs; FASB ASC 740; NAR

description: The statutory deadline by which the tax payment must be remitted to the taxing authority to avoid penalties and interest. May reflect installment due dates for jurisdictions with semi-annual or quarterly payment schedules.
reference: Local Tax Authority; IRS Publication 527; SOX Section 302

description: Indicates whether the owner or property qualifies for a full or partial tax exemption under applicable law (e.g., nonprofit ownership, government entity, REIT structural exemption, homestead exemption). Drives tax obligation calculation.
reference: IRS IRC §501(c)(3); Local Tax Exemption Laws; HUD; FASB ASC 740

description: Owner's Tax Identification Number (TIN) as assigned by the relevant tax authority — may be an EIN (Employer Identification Number), SSN (Social Security Number), ITIN (Individual Taxpayer Identification Number), or foreign equivalent. Used for FIRPTA withholding certificates, K-1 issuance, and 1099 reporting.
reference: IRS Form W-9; IRS Form W-8BEN; IRS FIRPTA (IRC §1445); GDPR Article 9; SOX Section 302

description: Actual amount of tax remitted or paid to the taxing authority for this record period. May differ from assessed amount due to appeals, abatements, installment payments, or partial payments. Supports cash flow reporting and DSCR calculations.
reference: FASB ASC 740; IRS Publication 527; SOX Section 404

description: Actual date on which the tax payment was remitted to the taxing authority. Used to determine timeliness of payment, calculate penalty and interest exposure, and support cash flow reporting.
reference: IRS Publication 527; FASB ASC 740; SOX Section 404

description: End date of the tax assessment or reporting period covered by this record. For annual property tax, typically December 31 of the tax year. Defines the boundary of the tax obligation period.
reference: IRS Publication 527; FASB ASC 740; Local Tax Authority

description: Start date of the tax assessment or reporting period covered by this record. For annual property tax, typically January 1 of the tax year. For withholding tax, the start of the applicable withholding period.
reference: IRS Publication 527; FASB ASC 740; Local Tax Authority

business_glossary_term: Tax Rate (Mill Rate / Effective Tax Rate)
description: The effective tax rate or mill rate applied by the taxing jurisdiction to the assessed property value to compute the property tax bill. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.012500 = 1.25% or 12.5 mills). Also used for income or withholding tax rates where applicable.
reference: Local Tax Authority; IAAO; IRS Publication 527; RESO Data Dictionary

description: Externally-known reference number assigned by the taxing jurisdiction or internal tax administration system for this specific tax obligation record. Used for correspondence with tax authorities and audit trails.
reference: IRS; State/Local Tax Authority; RESO Data Dictionary

description: Specific article or provision of the applicable bilateral tax treaty under which the owner claims reduced withholding or exemption (e.g., 'Article 6 — Income from Immovable Property'). Required for IRS Form W-8BEN treaty benefit claims.
reference: OECD Model Tax Convention; IRS Form W-8BEN; US Bilateral Tax Treaties

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the treaty country under which the owner claims a reduced withholding rate or tax exemption. Null if no treaty election applies. Used in conjunction with IRS Form W-8BEN treaty claims.
reference: OECD Model Tax Convention; IRS Form W-8BEN; US Tax Treaty Network

description: Classification of the tax obligation captured in this record. Covers property tax obligations by jurisdiction, income tax, withholding tax for foreign owners (FIRPTA), transfer tax on disposition, capital gains tax, and K-1 partnership income allocations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: property_tax|income_tax|withholding_tax|transfer_tax|capital_gains_tax|k1_partnership_allocation|excise_tax|franchise_tax — promote to reference product]
reference: IRS FIRPTA (IRC §1445); IRS Schedule K-1 (Form 1065); FASB ASC 740; IASB IFRS 16

description: The calendar or fiscal year to which this tax record applies (e.g., 2024). Used for annual tax reporting, K-1 allocations, and SOX compliance period alignment.
reference: IRS Publication 527; FASB ASC 740; SOX Section 302

description: Classification of the tax identification number provided — EIN (Employer Identification Number) for entities, SSN (Social Security Number) for US individuals, ITIN (Individual Taxpayer Identification Number) for non-resident individuals, or foreign TIN for international owners.
reference: IRS Form W-9; IRS Form W-8BEN; IRS FIRPTA (IRC §1445)

description: IRS-issued withholding certificate number (from Form 8288-B application) that authorizes a reduced or zero FIRPTA withholding rate for a foreign owner. Null if no certificate has been obtained. Required for FIRPTA compliance tracking.
reference: IRS FIRPTA (IRC §1445); IRS Form 8288-B; IRS Form 8288

description: Cross-reference code identifying the corresponding tax entity or property record in Yardi Voyager Property Management and General Ledger modules. Enables reconciliation between the owner tax record and Yardi GL tax entries and AR/AP tax transactions.
reference: Yardi Voyager System of Record; SOX Section 404 IT Controls

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Externally-known unique reference number or code assigned to this agreement, as recorded in Yardi Voyager or DocuSign CLM. Used for cross-system reconciliation and correspondence.
reference: DocuSign CLM; Yardi Voyager

description: Current lifecycle state of the owner agreement. Drives operational workflows in Yardi Voyager and DocuSign CLM — e.g., only 'active' agreements generate management fee invoices.

description: Contractually specified mechanism for resolving disputes arising under the agreement. Informs legal risk management and compliance reporting.
reference: NAR; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Unique envelope identifier from DocuSign CLM corresponding to the executed agreement. Enables direct retrieval of the signed document and audit trail from the contract lifecycle management system.

description: Date on which the agreement becomes legally binding and operational obligations commence. Aligns with the commencement date field in Yardi Voyager contract records.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IFRS 16; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

business_glossary_term: Environmental Social and Governance (ESG) Compliance Flag
description: Indicates whether the agreement includes Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) compliance obligations, such as LEED certification maintenance, BREEAM targets, or ESG reporting requirements.
reference: USGBC LEED; BREEAM; EPA; ULI ESG Framework

description: Indicates whether the agreement grants the management or advisory firm exclusive rights to manage or transact on behalf of the owner for the covered properties or portfolio.
reference: NAR; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Date on which all parties formally signed and executed the agreement. May differ from effective_date for agreements with retroactive or future commencement. Sourced from DocuSign CLM completion timestamp.
reference: DocuSign CLM; NAR; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Date on which the agreement is scheduled to expire or terminate. Null for open-ended or evergreen agreements. Used for WALT/WALE calculations and renewal pipeline management.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IFRS 16; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Frequency at which management and other recurring fees are invoiced and payable under the agreement. Drives Accounts Receivable (AR) billing cycles in Yardi Voyager.
reference: Yardi Voyager; FASB ASC 842

description: The legal jurisdiction whose laws govern interpretation and enforcement of this agreement (e.g., 'New York, USA', 'England and Wales'). Critical for dispute resolution and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: NAR; SEC; GDPR Article 3

description: Indicates whether the agreement includes a performance-based incentive or promote fee structure in addition to the base management fee.
reference: MRI Software; Argus Enterprise; SEC

description: Minimum return threshold (hurdle rate) that must be achieved before the incentive or promote fee becomes payable to the asset manager. Expressed as an annual percentage (e.g., IRR or ROI threshold).
reference: Argus Enterprise; MRI Software; SEC

description: Percentage of outperformance above the hurdle rate payable as an incentive or promote fee to the asset manager. Null if incentive_fee_flag is false.
reference: Argus Enterprise; MRI Software; SEC

description: Indicates whether the agreement specifies mandatory insurance coverage requirements (e.g., property insurance, liability, E&O) that the manager must maintain.
reference: NAR; OSHA; EPA

description: Date of the most recent formal amendment or addendum executed against this agreement. Null if no amendments have been made.
reference: DocuSign CLM; SOX

description: The income or revenue base upon which the management fee percentage is applied. EGI = Effective Gross Income; GRI = Gross Rental Income; NOI = Net Operating Income; NRI = Net Rental Income; GOI = Gross Operating Income; fixed = flat fee not tied to income.
reference: Yardi Voyager; MRI Software; Appraisal Institute

description: Fixed periodic management fee amount in the agreement currency, applicable when management_fee_basis is 'fixed'. Null when fee is percentage-based.
reference: Yardi Voyager; MRI Software

description: Base property or asset management fee expressed as a percentage of Effective Gross Income (EGI) or Gross Rental Income (GRI) as defined in the agreement. Core commercial term tracked in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software.
reference: Yardi Voyager; MRI Software; FASB ASC 842

description: Source system agreement or mandate reference number from MRI Software, used for investment management and property accounting reconciliation.
reference: MRI Software

reference: FinCEN Customer Due Diligence Rule (31 CFR 1010.230); SEC Regulation D; FASB ASC 323

description: Timestamp when the owner record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the authoritative audit trail creation marker for data governance and SOX compliance.
reference: SOX Section 302; GDPR Article 5; ISO 8601

description: Frequency at which investment distributions (income and/or return of capital) are made to the owner entity. Drives cash flow scheduling in Yardi Voyager AR module and investor reporting cycles.

description: Preferred method for receiving investment distributions. Wire Transfer = international/domestic wire; ACH = Automated Clearing House electronic transfer; Check = paper check; DRIP = Distribution Reinvestment Plan (reinvest distributions into additional units).

description: State or province of legal domicile or incorporation for the owner entity. Used for state-level tax compliance, regulatory filings, and jurisdictional reporting. Expressed as ISO 3166-2 subdivision code where applicable.

description: Legal classification of the owner entity. Determines applicable regulatory, tax, and reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: individual|corporation|partnership|trust|reit|sovereign_wealth_fund|family_office|joint_venture|llc|government_entity — promote to reference product]
reference: SEC Regulation S-K; FASB ASC 946; IRS Publication 541

business_glossary_term: Environmental Social and Governance (ESG) Mandate Flag
description: Indicates whether the owner entity has a formal ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) investment mandate requiring portfolio properties to meet specific sustainability criteria (e.g., LEED, BREEAM certifications). True = ESG mandate in place; False = no formal ESG requirement.
reference: U.S. Green Building Council (USGBC) LEED; BREEAM; GRESB Real Estate Assessment; SEC ESG Disclosure Rules

business_glossary_term: Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act (FATCA) Status
description: FATCA (Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act) classification of the owner entity. US Person = US citizen/resident/entity; FFI = Foreign Financial Institution; NFFE = Non-Financial Foreign Entity; Exempt = exempt from FATCA reporting; Unknown = not yet determined. Drives IRS Form W-8/W-9 requirements.
reference: FATCA (IRC Section 1471-1474); IRS Form W-8BEN-E; OECD Common Reporting Standard (CRS)

description: Current status of the Know Your Customer (KYC) due diligence process for the owner entity. Required for AML compliance and investor onboarding. Tracks whether identity verification, beneficial ownership disclosure, and sanctions screening have been completed.
reference: FinCEN Customer Due Diligence Rule (31 CFR 1010.230); GDPR Article 6; USA PATRIOT Act

description: Date on which the KYC verification was last successfully completed and approved for this owner entity. Used to track compliance currency and trigger periodic re-verification workflows.
reference: FinCEN Customer Due Diligence Rule (31 CFR 1010.230); USA PATRIOT Act

description: Full legal name of the owner or investor entity as registered with the relevant government authority. For individuals, this is the full legal name; for entities, this is the registered corporate or trust name. Used as the authoritative identity label across all portfolio records.
reference: GDPR Article 4; FASB ASC 842; SEC Regulation S-K

description: 20-character alphanumeric Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) code assigned by a GLEIF-accredited Local Operating Unit. Required for institutional investors and publicly traded REITs for regulatory reporting and counterparty identification in financial transactions.
reference: ISO 17442; GLEIF (Global Legal Entity Identifier Foundation); SEC Regulation S-K

description: City component of the owner entity's primary mailing address. Used in conjunction with state and postal code for address validation and regulatory correspondence.
reference: GDPR Article 4; USPS Addressing Standards

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the owner entity's primary mailing address. Determines applicable postal regulations, cross-border tax withholding, and FATCA/CRS reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1; FATCA (IRC Section 1471-1474); GDPR Article 4

description: First line of the owner entity's primary mailing address, including street number and street name. Used for legal notices, distribution checks, K-1 tax forms, and investor correspondence.
reference: GDPR Article 4; USPS Addressing Standards; ALTA

description: Date on which the owner entity was formally offboarded or terminated from the platform, following full disposition of holdings and settlement of obligations. Null if the owner is still active.
reference: GDPR Article 17 (Right to Erasure); Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); SOX Section 802

description: Date on which the owner entity was formally onboarded and activated in the platform, completing all KYC, AML, and documentation requirements. Marks the start of the active relationship lifecycle.
reference: FinCEN Customer Due Diligence Rule; GDPR Article 5; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Current lifecycle status of the owner entity within the platform. Drives access controls, KYC compliance gating, and operational workflows in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software. Pending = KYC in progress; Active = fully onboarded; Suspended = compliance hold; Inactive = no current holdings; Terminated = relationship ended.
reference: FASB ASC 842; FinCEN AML/KYC Guidelines; GDPR Article 5

description: High-level investor classification used for portfolio segmentation, AUM (Assets Under Management) reporting, and investment advisory targeting. Distinguishes institutional investors from private individuals, public REITs, family offices, and sovereign wealth funds.
reference: Urban Land Institute (ULI); NCREIF; SEC Regulation D

description: Indicates whether the owner entity or its beneficial owners are classified as Politically Exposed Persons (PEPs) — individuals who hold or have held prominent public positions. True = PEP identified; False = no PEP association. Triggers enhanced due diligence (EDD) requirements under AML regulations.
reference: FATF Recommendations; FinCEN Customer Due Diligence Rule; USA PATRIOT Act Section 312

description: Comma-delimited list of real estate asset classes preferred by the owner for investment (e.g., office, retail, multifamily, industrial, hospitality, mixed-use). Used for deal matching, investment advisory targeting, and portfolio strategy alignment in Argus Enterprise and Salesforce CRM.
reference: Urban Land Institute (ULI); NCREIF Property Index; MSCI Real Estate

description: Owner's stated preferred investment hold period in years. Used for portfolio strategy alignment, DCF (Discounted Cash Flow) modeling in Argus Enterprise, and WALT (Weighted Average Lease Term) matching during acquisition underwriting.
reference: Urban Land Institute (ULI); Appraisal Institute; NCREIF

description: Primary email address for official communications with the owner entity. Used for investor reporting, distribution notices, KYC correspondence, and portal access credentials. Sourced from Yardi Voyager and Salesforce CRM.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CAN-SPAM Act; CASL

description: Primary telephone number for the owner entity, stored in E.164 international format. Used for investor relations, KYC verification callbacks, and urgent operational communications.
reference: GDPR Article 4; ITU-T E.164

description: Indicates whether the owner entity qualifies as a qualified purchaser under the Investment Company Act of 1940 (Section 2(a)(51)), typically requiring $5M+ in investments. Determines eligibility for Section 3(c)(7) fund offerings.
reference: Investment Company Act of 1940 Section 2(a)(51); SEC Release IC-22597

description: Official registration or incorporation number issued by the relevant government authority (e.g., Secretary of State, Companies House). Used to verify legal existence and cross-reference with public registries. Applicable to corporations, LLCs, partnerships, and trusts.
reference: SEC Regulation S-K; National Association of Realtors (NAR); ISO 17442 Legal Entity Identifier (LEI)

description: Owner's stated investment risk tolerance profile, aligned with standard CRE (Commercial Real Estate) investment strategy classifications. Core = low risk/stable income; Core Plus = moderate risk; Value Add = higher risk/repositioning; Opportunistic = highest risk/development. Used in Argus Enterprise for portfolio strategy modeling.
reference: Urban Land Institute (ULI); NCREIF; INREV Investment Style Classification

description: Indicates whether the owner entity has been screened against OFAC SDN (Specially Designated Nationals) list, EU sanctions lists, and other applicable sanctions databases. True = screening completed with no match; False = not yet screened or match found requiring review.
reference: OFAC SDN List; USA PATRIOT Act; EU Sanctions Regulation; FinCEN AML Guidelines

description: Owner's stated target Capitalization Rate (CAP Rate) for property acquisitions, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.50 = 5.5%). Used for deal screening and investment underwriting in Argus Enterprise. CAP Rate = NOI / Property Value.
reference: Appraisal Institute; International Valuation Standards Council (IVSC); FASB ASC 820

business_glossary_term: Target Internal Rate of Return (IRR) Percentage
description: Owner's stated minimum target Internal Rate of Return (IRR) hurdle rate for investment decisions, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 12.00 = 12%). Used in Argus Enterprise for deal screening, investment committee approvals, and portfolio performance benchmarking.
reference: Appraisal Institute; International Valuation Standards Council (IVSC); Urban Land Institute (ULI)

description: Indicates whether the owner entity holds tax-exempt status (e.g., pension funds, endowments, sovereign wealth funds, charitable organizations). True = tax-exempt; False = taxable entity. Affects UBTI (Unrelated Business Taxable Income) calculations and withholding tax obligations.
reference: IRC Section 501(c); IRC Section 892 (sovereign wealth funds); IRS Publication 598

description: Classifies the type of tax identification number stored in tax_id. Determines applicable IRS reporting forms and withholding rules. EIN = Employer Identification Number; SSN = Social Security Number; TIN = Tax Identification Number (generic); ITIN = Individual Taxpayer Identification Number.
reference: IRS Publication 1635; FATCA (IRC Section 1471-1474)

description: Government-issued tax identification number for the owner entity. For US entities: Employer Identification Number (EIN) for corporations/partnerships or Social Security Number (SSN) for individuals. For non-US entities: equivalent Tax Identification Number (TIN). Used for IRS 1099 reporting, withholding tax, and FATCA compliance.
reference: IRS Publication 1635; FATCA (IRC Section 1471-1474); GDPR Article 9

description: Operating or trade name (Doing Business As) used by the owner entity in commercial dealings, if different from the legal name. Common for partnerships, family offices, and REITs operating under a brand name.
reference: National Association of Realtors (NAR); Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the owner record, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC), data lineage tracking, and audit compliance.
reference: SOX Section 302; GDPR Article 5; ISO 8601

description: Source system entity code assigned to this owner in Yardi Voyager. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the silver layer master registry and the Yardi Voyager operational system of record. Enables traceability back to the source.
reference: Yardi Voyager Data Dictionary; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each owner contact record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the owner_contact data product.
reference: ISO 11179 — Data Element Naming and Definition

description: Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Contact mailing country determines GDPR applicability, sanctions screening jurisdiction, and communication compliance requirements. Compliance teams require normalized country reference for cross-bord
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Date on which explicit consent was obtained from this contact for electronic communications and data processing. Required for GDPR Article 6 lawful basis documentation and CAN-SPAM/TCPA compliance for marketing and investor communications.

description: Type of legal basis or consent obtained for processing and communicating with this contact under GDPR Article 6. Explicit: affirmative opt-in consent. reference: GDPR Article 6; GDPR Article 7; ICO Guidance on Consent; CASL

description: Originating system or channel from which this contact record was created or imported. Supports data lineage tracking and master data management reconciliation across Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and Salesforce CRM.

description: Current lifecycle status of this contact record. Active: contact is current and reachable. Inactive: contact is no longer in use but retained for historical reference. Do_not_contact: contact has opted out or is legally restricted. Pending_verification: contact details awaiting KYC/AML validation. Deceased: individual contact is deceased.
reference: GDPR Article 17 (Right to Erasure); CAN-SPAM Act; Yardi Voyager — Contact Status; NAR Standards

description: Indicates whether this contact record represents an individual person (e.g., named representative) or an organizational entity (e.g., law firm, accounting firm) acting on behalf of the owner.
reference: RESO Data Dictionary — Contact Type; NAR Standards

description: Timestamp when this contact record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under SOX and GDPR data processing records.

description: Business unit or department within the owner's organization that this contact represents (e.g., Legal, Finance, Asset Management, Investor Relations, Operations). Supports targeted communication routing for institutional owners.

description: Result of the prior landlord reference check, verifying rental payment history, lease compliance, and tenancy conduct with previous landlords. A key qualitative input to the overall screening determination.

description: Free-text field for the reviewer to document observations, exceptions, individualized assessment findings, or other context relevant to the screening determination. Supports audit trail and fair housing compliance documentation.

description: Composite pass/fail determination across all screening check types performed. A conditional pass may indicate approval subject to additional conditions such as a co-signer or increased security deposit. Drives the underwriting decision in the application workflow.

description: Indicates whether the screening was conducted for a residential (B2C) or commercial (B2B) tenancy application. Determines applicable screening criteria, fair housing rules, and regulatory requirements.

description: Date after which the screening report is considered stale and a new screening must be ordered before a lease can be executed. Typically 30–90 days from completion date per company policy and FCRA guidelines.
