reference: RSMeans Construction Cost Data; Appraisal Institute — The Appraisal of Real Estate; IVSC IVS 410

business_glossary_term: Typical Construction Cost Low (PSF — Per Square Foot)
description: Lower bound of the typical hard construction cost range in USD per square foot (PSF) for this construction type. Used in development feasibility analysis, CAPEX budgeting, and replacement cost valuation in Argus Enterprise and Yardi Voyager.
reference: RSMeans Construction Cost Data; Appraisal Institute — The Appraisal of Real Estate; IVSC IVS 410

description: Typical clear span capability in feet for the structural system of this construction type. Informs GLA/NLA planning, column-free floor plate design, and tenant improvement (TI) feasibility assessments for commercial leasing.
reference: Appraisal Institute — The Appraisal of Real Estate; American Institute of Steel Construction (AISC)

description: Expected economic useful life in years for buildings of this construction type. Used for depreciation schedules under FASB ASC 360 and IFRS 16, fixed asset management in Yardi Voyager, and replacement reserve modeling in investment underwriting.
reference: FASB ASC 360 Property Plant and Equipment; IFRS IAS 16; Appraisal Institute — The Appraisal of Real Estate

description: Qualitative wind resistance performance rating (basic, enhanced, superior) for this construction type based on IBC and ASCE 7 wind load provisions. Supports property insurance underwriting and catastrophe risk modeling for portfolio management.
reference: American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE) 7 Chapter 26; International Building Code (IBC) Section 1609

description: Primary key for lease_type

Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: lease_type.area_measure_standard is a STRING describing the area measurement standard applicable to this lease type (e.g., BOMA 2017, IPMS, RICS). The uom table is the enterprise UOM registry that inc
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to reference.market_segment. Business justification: Lease types are associated with specific market segments (e.g., NNN leases are typical in Core commercial segments, gross leases in Value-Add residential segments). Linking to the market_segment regis
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Industry-standard abbreviation for the lease type used in market communications, CoStar listings, and brokerage reports (e.g., NNN, MG, FS, PCT, GL). Aligns with CoStar Suite and MLS syndication field conventions.
reference: CoStar Suite; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); National Association of Realtors (NAR)

description: The corresponding lease type identifier as used in Argus Enterprise cash flow and valuation models. Enables mapping between this reference record and Argus Enterprise lease structure assumptions for DCF modeling, CAP rate analysis, and portfolio reporting.
reference: Argus Enterprise Valuation and Cash Flow Modeling Module

description: Specifies the default lease classification under FASB ASC 842 for this lease type: 'operating' (lessee recognizes right-of-use asset and operating lease liability), 'finance' (lessee recognizes finance lease asset and liability with front-loaded expense), 'short_term_exemption' (leases ≤12 months exempt from balance sheet recognition), or 'not_applicable' (lessor-side or non-applicable structures). Drives accounting treatment in SAP S/4HANA and Yardi Voyager GL.
reference: FASB ASC 842; Financial Accounting Standards Board (FASB); Yardi Voyager General Ledger

business_glossary_term: Common Area Maintenance (CAM) Reconciliation Required Flag
description: Indicates whether annual CAM reconciliation is required for this lease type (True). CAM reconciliation compares estimated CAM charges billed to tenants against actual CAM expenses incurred. Required for NNN and modified gross leases; not applicable for full gross leases. Activates CAM reconciliation workflows in Yardi Voyager.
reference: Yardi Voyager CAM Administration; FASB ASC 842; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Indicates which party bears responsibility for Common Area Maintenance (CAM) charges under this lease type. 'landlord' means the landlord absorbs all CAM costs; 'tenant' means the tenant pays CAM directly (as in NNN); 'shared' means costs are pro-rated; 'not_applicable' for lease types where CAM is not a relevant concept (e.g., ground lease). Governs CAM reconciliation workflows in Yardi Voyager.
reference: FASB ASC 842; Yardi Voyager CAM Administration; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: The date and time when this lease type reference record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements under SOX financial controls, and reference data governance.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); FASB ASC 842; ISO 55000 Asset Management

description: The date from which this lease type classification became or becomes effective for use in new lease agreements. Supports temporal validity tracking for reference data governance and ensures that historical lease records retain the correct classification at the time of execution.
reference: FASB ASC 842; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Indicates the standard rent escalation methodology associated with this lease type: fixed_step (predetermined dollar or percentage increases), cpi_indexed (Consumer Price Index-linked adjustments), market_reset (rent reset to prevailing market rate at intervals), none (flat rent for term), or hybrid (combination of methods). Governs rent schedule escalation logic in Yardi Voyager and Argus Enterprise DCF models.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; Argus Enterprise; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

business_glossary_term: Environmental Social and Governance (ESG) Reporting Applicable Flag
description: Indicates whether ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) reporting obligations — such as green lease clauses, LEED/BREEAM compliance requirements, or energy consumption data sharing — are applicable to this lease type (True). Relevant for institutional and REIT portfolios subject to SEC ESG disclosure requirements and USGBC LEED certification programs.
reference: U.S. Green Building Council (USGBC) LEED; Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC); EPA Environmental Compliance; BREEAM

description: The date on which this lease type classification ceases to be valid for use in new lease agreements (nullable for open-ended classifications). Supports reference data lifecycle management and ensures deprecated lease types are not applied to new leases after their retirement date.
reference: FASB ASC 842; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Industry-standard or regulatory body reference code for this lease type, such as RESO standard codes, NCREIF property type codes, or CoStar lease type identifiers. Enables cross-system interoperability and alignment with industry data exchange standards.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); CoStar Suite; National Council of Real Estate Investment Fiduciaries (NCREIF)

description: Specifies the default lease classification under IASB IFRS 16 for this lease type: 'single_model' (all leases recognized on balance sheet as right-of-use asset and lease liability), 'short_term_exemption' (leases ≤12 months), 'low_value_exemption' (underlying asset of low value), or 'not_applicable'. Supports international financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA for entities reporting under IFRS.
reference: IASB IFRS 16; International Accounting Standards Board (IASB); SAP S/4HANA Financial Consolidation

description: Defines which party is responsible for property insurance premiums under this lease type. Relevant for NNN and modified gross lease structures where insurance cost allocation varies. Used in lease administration billing and CAM reconciliation processes.
reference: FASB ASC 842; Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Indicates whether this lease type is a ground lease (True), where the tenant leases land only and typically constructs improvements. Ground leases have distinct legal, accounting, and investment characteristics including long terms (50–99 years), land-only rent, and reversion of improvements to the landlord. Relevant for REIT portfolio classification and Argus Enterprise valuation modeling.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC); Urban Land Institute (ULI)

description: Indicates whether this lease type is classified as short-term (True), typically defined as a lease term of 12 months or less at commencement. Short-term leases qualify for the practical expedient exemption from balance sheet recognition under both FASB ASC 842 and IASB IFRS 16. Drives accounting treatment selection in SAP S/4HANA and Yardi Voyager.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; Financial Accounting Standards Board (FASB)

description: Indicates whether this lease type represents a sublease arrangement (True), where the original lessee (sublandlord) leases all or part of the demised space to a subtenant. Subleases require distinct accounting treatment under FASB ASC 842 (intermediate lessor classification) and separate tracking in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; Yardi Voyager Lease Administration

description: Broad property-use category to which this lease type applies — commercial, residential, industrial, mixed_use, or land. Determines which operational workflows in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software are applicable and which regulatory frameworks (ASC 842, IFRS 16) govern accounting treatment.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Short, system-facing alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the lease type (e.g., NNN, GROSS, MOD_GROSS, PCT, GROUND, SUBLEASE, MTM). Used as the canonical lookup key in Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and downstream analytical systems to join lease records to their classification.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; MRI Software Lease Management

description: Detailed narrative description of the lease type, including its defining characteristics, typical use cases, cost responsibility allocation, and distinguishing features from similar lease structures. Used in training materials, lease administration documentation, and business glossary entries.
reference: National Association of Realtors (NAR); Urban Land Institute (ULI); Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Full human-readable name of the lease structure classification (e.g., 'Triple Net Lease', 'Gross Lease', 'Modified Gross Lease', 'Percentage Lease', 'Ground Lease', 'Sublease', 'Month-to-Month'). Displayed in reporting, lease administration screens, and tenant-facing documents.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); National Association of Realtors (NAR)

description: Current lifecycle status of this lease type reference record: 'active' (available for use in new lease agreements), 'inactive' (temporarily disabled but retained for historical reference), or 'deprecated' (superseded by a newer classification; retained for legacy lease records only). Controls availability in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software lease type selection lists.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; MRI Software Lease Management; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Indicates whether a Letter of Intent (LOI) is a standard prerequisite in the deal process for this lease type (True). LOIs are standard for commercial leases but typically not required for residential or month-to-month arrangements. Governs deal pipeline workflow in Salesforce CRM and DocuSign CLM.
reference: Salesforce CRM Deal Pipeline; DocuSign CLM; National Association of Realtors (NAR)

description: The corresponding lease type code as configured in MRI Software Lease Management module. Enables direct mapping between this enterprise reference record and the MRI source system code for data integration and reconciliation, particularly for residential and investment management portfolios.
reference: MRI Software Lease Management Module

description: Classifies how this lease type impacts Net Operating Income (NOI) calculation: 'gross_revenue' (all income flows through gross revenue line before expense deductions), 'net_revenue' (tenant pays expenses directly, reducing gross revenue line), or 'not_applicable'. Used in Argus Enterprise NOI modeling and MRI Software investment reporting.
reference: Argus Enterprise; MRI Software Investment Management; Urban Land Institute (ULI)

description: Defines which party is responsible for property operating expenditures (OpEx) beyond CAM — including insurance, management fees, and administrative costs — under this lease type. Critical for NOI calculation accuracy in Argus Enterprise and financial reporting under FASB ASC 842.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; Argus Enterprise; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Indicates whether percentage rent (a portion of tenant gross sales revenue above a breakpoint) is a feature of this lease type (True). Applicable primarily to retail percentage leases. Activates percentage rent calculation and reporting workflows in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software.
reference: FASB ASC 842; Yardi Voyager Percentage Rent Module; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Indicates whether renewal options are a standard feature of this lease type (True) or not applicable (False). For example, ground leases and long-term commercial leases typically include renewal options, while month-to-month leases do not. Informs lease renewal option tracking in Yardi Voyager and WALT/WALE calculations in Argus Enterprise.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Defines the standard unit of measure for rent quotation under this lease type: PSF (Per Square Foot) annual, PSF monthly, flat monthly amount, flat annual amount, or percentage of sales. Aligns with industry PSF/SQF conventions and drives rent schedule generation in Yardi Voyager and Argus Enterprise underwriting models.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); CoStar Suite; Argus Enterprise

description: Defines the fundamental rent calculation methodology for this lease type: fixed (flat base rent), variable (indexed or market-reset), percentage (percentage of tenant sales revenue), hybrid (base + percentage), or ground_rent (land-only lease payment). Drives rent schedule generation logic in Yardi Voyager and Argus Enterprise cash flow models.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; Argus Enterprise Valuation Module

description: Indicates whether a security deposit is a standard requirement for this lease type (True). Security deposits are common in residential and commercial leases but may not apply to ground leases or certain institutional arrangements. Drives security deposit tracking and accounting in Yardi Voyager AR and MRI Software.
reference: Yardi Voyager Accounts Receivable; MRI Software Residential Management; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

business_glossary_term: Subordination Non-Disturbance and Attornment (SNDA) Agreement Required Flag
description: Indicates whether a Subordination Non-Disturbance and Attornment (SNDA) agreement is a standard requirement for this lease type (True). SNDAs are typically required for commercial leases where the property is encumbered by a mortgage. Tracked in DocuSign CLM and Yardi Voyager lease document management.
reference: DocuSign CLM; Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Numeric value controlling the display sequence of lease type options in user interface dropdowns, reports, and lease administration screens. Lower values appear first. Enables business-defined ordering independent of alphabetical or ID-based sorting.
reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Indicates whether subleasing is a standard permitted feature under this lease type (True). Relevant for distinguishing primary lease structures from sublease structures and for compliance with FASB ASC 842 sublease accounting requirements.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Specifies which party is responsible for real estate property taxes under this lease type. In a Triple Net (NNN) lease, the tenant bears tax responsibility; in a gross lease, the landlord absorbs taxes. Drives tax pass-through billing logic in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software.
reference: FASB ASC 842; Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: Indicates whether Tenant Improvement (TI) allowances are a standard feature of this lease type (True). TI allowances are common in commercial gross and modified gross leases but less common in NNN or ground leases. Drives TI allowance tracking and amortization in Yardi Voyager Fixed Assets and FASB ASC 842 right-of-use asset calculations.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; Yardi Voyager Fixed Assets; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: The typical maximum lease duration in months associated with this lease type (e.g., 12 months for short-term, 120 months for standard commercial, 1200 months for ground leases). Used in Argus Enterprise for WALT/WALE modeling, portfolio duration analytics, and investment underwriting.
reference: Argus Enterprise; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); Urban Land Institute (ULI)

description: The typical minimum lease duration in months associated with this lease type (e.g., 1 month for month-to-month, 12 months for short-term, 60 months for standard commercial NNN). Used in Argus Enterprise for WALT/WALE modeling and portfolio duration analytics.
reference: Argus Enterprise; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); Urban Land Institute (ULI)

description: The date and time when this lease type reference record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and audit trail requirements under SOX financial controls for reference data governance.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); FASB ASC 842; ISO 55000 Asset Management

description: Specifies which party is responsible for utility costs (electricity, water, gas, HVAC) under this lease type. Gross leases typically assign utilities to the landlord; NNN leases assign them to the tenant. Drives utility billing configuration in Building Engines and Yardi Voyager.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration; Building Engines; Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO)

description: The corresponding lease type code as configured in Yardi Voyager Lease Administration module. Enables direct mapping between this enterprise reference record and the source system code for data integration, reconciliation, and ETL processes from Yardi Voyager.
reference: Yardi Voyager Lease Administration Module

reference: Real Estate Standards Organization (RESO); NCREIF; INREV

description: Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: market_segment tracks financial benchmarks denominated in currency — target_irr_min/max_pct, target_cap_rate_min/max_pct, typical_asset_size_min/max_usd, equity_multiple_min/max. Adding currency_code_
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Name or identifier of the business data steward responsible for maintaining the accuracy and completeness of this market segment record. Supports data governance accountability and reference data change management processes.
reference: ISO 55000 Asset Management; Internal Data Governance Standard; SOX

description: Indicates whether ground-up development or major redevelopment projects are permitted within this market segment's investment mandate (True = allowed, False = not allowed). Opportunistic segments typically allow development; core segments do not.
reference: Urban Land Institute (ULI); INREV Guidelines; Procore Construction Management

description: Indicates whether distressed, REO (Real Estate Owned), or impaired assets are eligible for acquisition under this market segment (True = eligible). Used in deal sourcing filters and investment committee approval workflows.
reference: NCREIF; Urban Land Institute (ULI); SEC Regulation S-K

description: Date from which this market segment definition became effective and available for use in deal classification and portfolio reporting. Supports point-in-time reporting and historical segment definition tracking.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Maximum target equity multiple for investments in this market segment (e.g., 2.50x for opportunistic). Defines the upper bound of expected return multiples used in fund performance benchmarking and investor reporting.
reference: INREV Guidelines; NCREIF; Argus Enterprise Investment Analysis

description: Minimum target equity multiple (total distributions / total equity invested) for investments in this market segment (e.g., 1.50x for core-plus). Used alongside IRR in investor return reporting and deal underwriting benchmarks.
reference: INREV Guidelines; NCREIF; Argus Enterprise Investment Analysis

description: ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) classification for the market segment, indicating the level of ESG integration expected in investments. ESG Core = baseline compliance; ESG Enhanced = active ESG improvement targets; ESG Impact = measurable social/environmental outcomes required. Supports LEED, BREEAM, and GRESB reporting.
reference: U.S. Green Building Council (USGBC) LEED; BREEAM; GRESB Real Estate Assessment; SEC ESG Disclosure Rules

description: Primary exit strategy indicator for investments in this market segment. Defines the expected disposition mechanism: outright sale, recapitalization, refinance and hold, IPO/REIT listing, long-term hold, or portfolio/bulk sale. Used in investment advisory, asset management planning, and investor reporting.
reference: INREV Guidelines; Urban Land Institute (ULI); SEC Regulation S-K (REIT reporting)

description: Date on which this market segment definition was retired or superseded. Null if the segment is currently active. Enables historical point-in-time queries and audit trail for segment reclassification events.
reference: FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Name of the primary external performance benchmark associated with this market segment for investor reporting (e.g., 'NCREIF Property Index', 'NCREIF ODCE', 'MSCI Real Estate', 'INREV All Vehicles Index'). Used in Argus Enterprise portfolio reporting and investor presentations.
reference: NCREIF; INREV; MSCI Real Estate Index

description: Describes the geographic investment focus associated with this market segment (e.g., 'Gateway markets only', 'Secondary and tertiary markets', 'National diversified', 'Sun Belt metros'). Used in deal sourcing strategy, CoStar market research filters, and portfolio geographic allocation reporting.
reference: CoStar Suite Market Research; Urban Land Institute (ULI); NCREIF

description: Maximum typical investment hold period in years for assets classified under this market segment (e.g., 10.0 years for core). Used in fund lifecycle planning, Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) horizon setting, and exit strategy modeling in Argus Enterprise.
reference: INREV Guidelines; Argus Enterprise; Urban Land Institute (ULI)

description: Minimum typical investment hold period in years for assets classified under this market segment (e.g., 3.0 years for value-add). Used in Argus Enterprise DCF modeling, Weighted Average Lease Term (WALT) analysis, and investor fund lifecycle planning.
reference: INREV Guidelines; Argus Enterprise; Urban Land Institute (ULI)

description: Indicates whether this market segment is primarily income-return focused (True) versus capital appreciation focused (False). Core and core-plus segments are typically income-focused; opportunistic and value-add are appreciation-focused. Used in portfolio allocation and investor mandate classification.
reference: NCREIF; INREV Guidelines

description: INREV (European Association for Investors in Non-Listed Real Estate Vehicles) strategy classification code for cross-border fund reporting alignment. Used for European investor mandates and INREV benchmark submissions.
reference: INREV Guidelines; INREV Fund Classification

description: Classification of the investment strategy associated with this market segment per industry-standard NCREIF/INREV taxonomy. Core = stabilized, low-risk; Core-Plus = modest value creation; Value-Add = significant repositioning; Opportunistic = high-risk/high-return development or turnaround; Distressed = below-market acquisition of impaired assets. Null for residential segments where strategy type is not applicable.
reference: NCREIF Property Index; INREV Guidelines; Argus Enterprise Investment Analysis Module

description: Describes the target investor profile for this market segment (e.g., 'Institutional pension funds', 'High-net-worth individuals', 'Family offices', 'Sovereign wealth funds'). Used in investor relations, fund marketing, and Salesforce CRM deal pipeline classification.
reference: SEC Regulation D; INREV Guidelines; Salesforce CRM

description: Indicates whether this market segment is currently active and available for use in deal classification, portfolio reporting, and investment mandate assignment (True = active, False = retired/inactive). Inactive segments are retained for historical reporting continuity.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; NCREIF

description: Date on which this market segment definition was last reviewed and validated by the data steward or investment committee. Supports reference data governance, periodic review cycles, and audit trail requirements.
reference: ISO 55000 Asset Management; SOX; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Qualitative leverage strategy descriptor for the market segment. Unlevered = all-equity; Low = LTV < 40%; Moderate = LTV 40–60%; High = LTV > 60%. Complements the target_ltv_max_pct field for narrative reporting and investor mandate screening.
reference: INREV Guidelines; CMBS Underwriting Standards

description: NCREIF Property Index benchmark code associated with this market segment for performance comparison (e.g., NPI, ODCE). Used in portfolio performance reporting, investor presentations, and Argus Enterprise benchmark analysis.
reference: NCREIF Property Index (NPI); NCREIF Open-End Diversified Core Equity (ODCE) Index

description: Minimum acceptable physical occupancy percentage for assets classified under this market segment at acquisition or during the hold period (e.g., 85.00 for 85%). Used in deal screening, underwriting assumptions, and Yardi Voyager lease administration reporting.
reference: NCREIF; Appraisal Institute; Argus Enterprise Valuation Module

description: Describes the primary property type(s) targeted by this market segment (e.g., 'Office, Retail, Industrial', 'Multifamily', 'Mixed-Use'). Used in deal sourcing filters, CoStar comparable analysis, and portfolio sector allocation reporting. Free-text to accommodate multi-type segments.
reference: RESO Property Type Standards; CoStar Suite; NCREIF Property Index

description: Free-text field capturing specific regulatory or compliance considerations applicable to this market segment (e.g., HUD affordability restrictions for affordable housing, REIT distribution requirements, SEC reporting obligations for publicly traded vehicles, LIHTC compliance for tax credit properties).
reference: HUD; SEC; FASB ASC 842; IASB IFRS 16; SOX; GDPR

description: Residential market tier classification for residential segments. Affordable = income-restricted housing (HUD AMI thresholds); Workforce = moderate-income households; Market-Rate = unsubsidized standard rental/for-sale; Luxury = premium amenity-rich product. Null for commercial segments.
reference: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) AMI Guidelines; National Association of Realtors (NAR)

description: Qualitative risk descriptor for the market segment, reflecting expected volatility, leverage sensitivity, and execution complexity. Used in investor mandate screening, portfolio risk reporting, and deal approval workflows. Aligns with INREV risk classification framework.
reference: INREV Guidelines; Urban Land Institute (ULI); SEC Regulation S-K (for publicly traded REITs)

description: Broad asset class category to which this market segment belongs. Distinguishes commercial investment strategies (core, core-plus, value-add, opportunistic, distressed) from residential strategies (affordable, workforce, market-rate, luxury) and other asset classes. Drives portfolio allocation reporting and investor mandate compliance.
reference: NCREIF Property Index; INREV Guidelines; Urban Land Institute (ULI)

description: Short, system-unique alphanumeric code identifying the market segment (e.g., CORE, CORE_PLUS, VALUE_ADD, OPPORTUNISTIC, DISTRESSED, AFFORDABLE, WORKFORCE, MKT_RATE, LUXURY). Used as the stable business key referenced by investment, valuation, brokerage, and transaction domains.
reference: NCREIF Property Index; INREV Guidelines; Urban Land Institute (ULI) Investment Strategy Classification

description: Detailed narrative description of the market segment, including its investment thesis, risk characteristics, typical asset profile, and use cases. Used in investor reporting, investment committee materials, and data governance documentation.
reference: Urban Land Institute (ULI); NCREIF; INREV Guidelines

description: Full human-readable name of the market segment (e.g., 'Core', 'Core-Plus', 'Value-Add', 'Opportunistic', 'Distressed', 'Affordable Housing', 'Workforce Housing', 'Market-Rate', 'Luxury'). Displayed in investor reports, portfolio dashboards, and deal classification screens.
reference: NCREIF; INREV; Urban Land Institute (ULI)

description: Integer value controlling the display order of market segments in dropdown lists, reports, and dashboards. Lower values appear first. Enables business-defined ordering independent of alphabetical or ID-based sorting.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard

business_glossary_term: Target Capitalization Rate (CAP Rate) Maximum Percentage
description: Maximum target Capitalization Rate (CAP Rate) percentage for acquisitions in this market segment, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 7.00 for 7%). Defines the upper bound of the CAP rate range used in deal screening and portfolio benchmarking.
reference: Appraisal Institute; International Valuation Standards Council (IVSC); NCREIF; Argus Enterprise

business_glossary_term: Target Capitalization Rate (CAP Rate) Minimum Percentage
description: Minimum target Capitalization Rate (CAP Rate) percentage for acquisitions in this market segment, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 4.50 for 4.5%). CAP Rate = Net Operating Income (NOI) / Property Value. Used in Argus Enterprise underwriting models and CoStar comparable analysis.
reference: Appraisal Institute; International Valuation Standards Council (IVSC); NCREIF; Argus Enterprise

business_glossary_term: Target Debt Service Coverage Ratio (DSCR) Minimum
description: Minimum acceptable Debt Service Coverage Ratio (DSCR) for assets in this market segment (e.g., 1.25). DSCR = Net Operating Income (NOI) / Total Debt Service. Used in lender covenant compliance, debt instrument structuring, and portfolio risk monitoring.
reference: INREV Guidelines; CMBS Underwriting Standards; MRI Software Investment Management

business_glossary_term: Target Internal Rate of Return (IRR) Maximum Percentage
description: Maximum target Internal Rate of Return (IRR) percentage for investments classified under this market segment, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15%). Defines the upper bound of the IRR range used in deal underwriting and investor mandate compliance checks.
reference: NCREIF; INREV; Argus Enterprise Valuation and Cash Flow Modeling; CFA Institute

business_glossary_term: Target Internal Rate of Return (IRR) Minimum Percentage
description: Minimum target unlevered or levered Internal Rate of Return (IRR) percentage for investments classified under this market segment, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 8.00 for 8%). Defines the lower bound of the IRR range used in deal underwriting, Argus Enterprise cash flow models, and investor reporting against NCREIF benchmarks.
reference: NCREIF; INREV; Argus Enterprise Valuation and Cash Flow Modeling; CFA Institute

description: Maximum target Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio percentage for debt financing on assets in this market segment, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 65.00 for 65%). Defines the leverage ceiling used in debt structuring, CMBS underwriting, and DSCR compliance monitoring.
reference: INREV Guidelines; FASB ASC 842; CMBS Underwriting Standards; MRI Software Investment Management

description: Maximum typical asset value in USD for investments classified under this market segment (e.g., 500,000,000 for $500M). Defines the upper bound of deal size for mandate compliance and portfolio concentration limits.
reference: Urban Land Institute (ULI); NCREIF; MRI Software Investment Management

description: Minimum typical asset value in USD for investments classified under this market segment (e.g., 10,000,000 for $10M). Used in deal sourcing filters, portfolio allocation guidelines, and investment mandate compliance in MRI Software and Argus Enterprise.
reference: Urban Land Institute (ULI); NCREIF; MRI Software Investment Management

description: Narrative description of the primary value creation approach for this market segment (e.g., 'Stabilized income with minimal capital expenditure', 'Lease-up of vacant space with light renovation', 'Major repositioning, redevelopment, or change of use'). Used in investment committee presentations and investor reporting.
reference: Urban Land Institute (ULI); INREV Guidelines
